Elyne.bio: Italian post-hardcore band from Ravena. Current members: Daniele Faccani - Vocals/Guitar Elia Magnani - Guitar/Vocals Bruno Stella - Bass Dario Capacci - Drums Discography: What Burns Inside (2014)

KINDRID.bio: Kindrid is an electronic producer hailing from Houston, Texas. His first taste of fame was on Twitch, where deadmau5 heard his tune on stream and approved of it. That track, Demise, was released on March 9, 2018. It then became part of his debut EP, Godspeed, along with the tracks Kinetic, Abide, and Godspeed with JR. His first release of 2019 was Dynamia, included on mau5trap's We Are Friends, Vol. 8. He then released a remix of Can't You See by Avestra and TNAN.

Bleed.bio: There are many bands under the name Bleed. 1) An alternative metal/rock band from Dallas, Texas. Often distinguished on social media from other bands named "bleed" by incorporating their local area telephone code of 214. The band comprised of former and current members of Narrow Head at the time of their formation. On September 12, 2021 they dropped the 4-track EP Somebody's Closer. 2) Bleed is a Dutch thrash/metalcore band. These are dire times for metalcore.

The Plot in You.bio: The Plot in You is an American metalcore band formed in 2010 in Hancock County, Ohio. Originally a side project of former Before Their Eyes member Landon Tewers, the group is composed of Tewers, guitarist Josh Childress, bassist Ethan Yoder and drummer Michael Cooper. The Plot in You have released three EPs and five studio albums. Their debut EP Wife Beater was released in 2010, followed by their first studio album First Born in 2011, and second studio album Could You Watch Your Children Burn in 2013.

Cry Club.bio: Cry Club - a 2 piece noisy queer pop band formed in January 2018 After a big first year playing shows with the likes of RAAVE TAPES, Moaning Lisa & Bec Sandridge and appearances at Yours and Owls Festival and BIGSOUND, Cry Club are starting 2019 with their 2nd single “DFTM”. Produced by Gab Strum and mixed by Scott Horscroft (previous credits include The Presets, Silverchair), “DFTM” reflects the unfortunately relatable experience of deliberate personal space invasion in public spaces, especially at live shows.

Grant.bio: (1) Alma Carolina Cederlöf, a 26 years old artist from Ösmo in Sweden. 2) US Born, Marseille based house producer and DJ. (3) 24 year old producer from Los Angeles. Grant, formerly known as Grant Bowtie, is an electronic music producer from San Diego, California. Grant made his debut on Monstercat with his original track "Reach" in 2014 when he was 16, under the name Grant Bowtie. Following the release of "Wake Up" in late 2016, he changed his name to Grant, as a "mark of adulthood and growing maturity as a person and an artist.

Interloper.bio: There are three artists/bands with this name on Last.fm: 1) Interloper consists of Miles Dimitri Baker (Ænimus), Aaron Stechauner (Rings of Saturn, ex Abiotic), and Lucas Mann (Rings of Saturn). Melodic, Technical, and Progressive music bringing forward a new style of music that fans of all genres can enjoy and appreciate. youtube | facebook | bandcamp Launched: October 7, 2014 Contact: Interloperofficial@gmail.com Hometown Orange, CA. Costa Mesa, CA. Tracy, CA.

Hyde.bio: 1) HYDE (ハイド) is the solo project of hyde, the singer and main lyricist for the Japanese rock band L'Arc~en~Ciel and Vamps. Since HYDE keeps his personal information scarce, much about him is either unknown or unofficial, including his real name, purported to be Hideto Takarai. He confirmed his birthdate (January 29, 1969) in an interview for the 2002 March issue of Rockin On Japan. HYDE's debut as a solo artist was in October 2001, when he released his first solo single, Evergreen.

Black Sabbath.bio: Black Sabbath were an English heavy metal band formed in Birmingham in 1968 by guitarist Tony Iommi, drummer Bill Ward, bassist Geezer Butler, and vocalist Ozzy Osbourne. The band initially performed under the names Polka Tulk Blues Band and later Earth, before adopting the name Black Sabbath in 1969. They became known for lyrical themes influenced by horror and the occult, as well as for the use of down-tuned guitars. Their first three albums, "Black Sabbath" and "Paranoid" (both 1970)

Accept.bio: Accept is a German heavy metal band from Solingen, formed in 1976. The band’s first stable lineup included vocalist Udo Dirkschneider, lead guitarist Wolf Hoffmann, bassist Peter Baltes, rhythm guitarist Jörg Fischer and drummer Stefan Kaufmann. The current lineup includes Hoffmann, vocalist Mark Tornillo, guitarist Philip Shouse, drummer Christopher Williams and bassist Martin Motnik. Accept has undergone numerous lineup changes, with Hoffmann remaining the only constant member.

chouchou merged syrups..bio: chouchou merged syrups.(シュシュマージドシロップス) A four-piece alternative band formed in November 2010 Guitar ＆Vocal Kawato Chiaki Guitar Yoshizuka Shota Bass Toriishi Ryota Drums Takagaki Ryosuke ex. member Dr.宇野元大 OHP : http://chouchoumergedsyrups.com/

Fizzy Blood.bio: Fizzy Blood are 4 good friends who came together to make music in 2014 through a shared incompetence in the modern workplace. Based out of Leeds they combine retro-inspired rock, blistering riffs and a modern songwriting ethos to create something that stands apart from the current trend of rock music. Although they first met at school in a small Midlands city, it took years of various musical endeavours before the boys found themselves together in Leeds.

Scandroid.bio: The synthwave project of Los Angeles-based artist and producer "Red" aka Klayton, Scandroid is a love letter to the ‘80s delivered with modern production and a genre-crossing set of influences. Best known for his work on the multifaceted project Celldweller, Klayton formed Scandroid in 2013 and found a new inspiration for his densely layered compositions by combining ‘80s pop sensibilities with a host of other creative ideas, ranging from classic darkwave to modern electronic music.

Pearly Drops.bio: A music duo based in Helsinki, Finland, crafting eerily haunting pop dirges; Pearly Drops feature Sandra Tervonen and Juuso Malin. With concise and sparse compositional methods, ethereal vocal performances underlined by dreamy, precise synths and percussion, Pearly Drops are in the process of weaving - something made for keeping the hope inside and the unmeaningful at bay.

Heart of a Coward.bio: Ex-Sylosis frontman Jamie Graham has hooked up with the fast-rising British groove metal outfit Heart of a Coward as their new vocalist. Metalcore band from Milton Keynes, United Kingdom Representatives of the band offered this statement: "This is an exciting time to bring in a well trained and experienced new vocalist, Jamie has brought so many new ideas to the table which will see the album reach new levels and excite all of our fans plus many

Tillie.bio: As a teenager in Atlanta, singer/songwriter Tillie played basement shows with her punk band. After a brief stint at NYU’s prestigious Clive Davis Institute, Tillie followed her musical dreams to Los Angeles. One of her key career moments happened in late 2016, when Machine Gun Kelly saw her perform live and asked her to collaborate on a cover of the Foo Fighters’ hit “Everlong.” Another audience member at that serendipitous show was Vans Warped Tour founder Kevin Lyman

UnityTX.bio: UnityTX is a rap metal/hardcore band from Dallas, Texas. The group was formed in 2014 and has been making their way through the underground scene since their inception. The cover of UNITYTX’s new EP, MADBOY, features a cartoon depiction of frontman Jay Webster shouldering a massive boulder, though from the illustration it’s hard to tell whether this represents the final step of a Herculean triumph or another entry in a Sisyphean sentence. As it turns out, the Dallas-based hardcore/hip-hop band’s career has been both.

League of Legends.bio: League of Legends (abbreviated LoL or just League) is a multiplayer online battle arena video game developed and published by Riot Games for Microsoft Windows and OS X. The game follows a freemium model and is supported by microtransactions, and was inspired by the Warcraft III: The Frozen Throne mod, Defense of the Ancients. In League of Legends, players assume the role of an unseen "summoner" that controls a "champion" with unique abilities and battle against a team of other players or computer-controlled champions.

similar: N O W A V E

Oni.bio: There are multiple artists that go by the name oni. 1) ONI is a croatian hip-hop group. Members are Scriptor, Jantar, Kali (MC's), KolAK47 (producer) and DJ Noki Nole. Their first album titled Lajtmotiv came out on 6th of June 2015 and their second album titled Kino came out exactly 10 years later, to the date, in 2025. 2) Akinyeme Olagdeji Odawali Oni Orison is a rapper who made sophomoric slump with Beatmonger's productions. The two met while going to the University of Michigan.

Oddko.bio: ODDKO, a Los Angeles-based band, is the inception of award-winning director Giovanni Bucci (Korn, Fleshgod Apocalypse). ODDKO is the animal within – the wild beast in all of us – driven by instinct and the longing to break free of society’s cage. The band lineup is comprised of Giovanni Bucci (Lead Vocals), Francesco Paoli (Drums), Gideon “Obsidian” Garcia (Guitar), and Dave Tavanti (Bass). The band’s debut 5-track EP, “Digital Gods” is out now on all platforms.

Toothgrinder.bio: Toothgrinder was created in 2010 by a group of musicians wanting to hit the music scene with something broader and more inventive than had been previously done by each in the past. Toothgrinder resides in Asbury Park, NJ and has recently won Best Heavy Rock Band (2010,2011,2012,2013) and Best Drummer (2011,2013) by the Asbury Park Music Awards.

Martin Stig Andersen.bio: Martin Stig Andersen (b. 1973) creates acousmatic music, sound installations and electroacoustic theatre performances, and collaborates with a number of distinguished musicians, performance and video artists. Recently he ventured into the world of film sound, blurring the traditional dividing line between film music and sound design. As a composer Martin Stig Andersen has received commissions from various ensembles and organizations and his music has been performed in numerous international festivals.

Pain of Salvation.bio: Pain of Salvation is a Swedish progressive metal band founded in Eskilstuna in 1984 and led by Daniel Gildenlöw, who serves as the primary songwriter, lyricist, guitarist, and vocalist. The band's music is characterized by riff-based guitar work, a wide vocal range, dynamic shifts between heavy and subdued passages, complex vocal harmonies, and the use of syncopation and polyrhythms. All of the band's studio albums have been released as concept albums

THE ORAL CIGARETTES.bio: THE ORAL CIGARETTES is a Japanese rock band from Nara Prefecture, formed in 2010. The band consist of vocalist and guitarist Takuya Yamanaka, guitarist Shigenobu Suzuki, bassist and backing vocalist Akirakani Akira and drummer Masaya Nakanishi. The band was formed by vocalist Takuya and bassist Akira. Takuya used to work at a bank but he didn’t like working under someone else so he decided to make music instead. They were joined by guitarist Shigenobu, who went to the same kindergarten as Takuya.
similar: TK from 凛として時雨

Evergrey.bio: Evergrey is a Swedish progressive metal band from Gothenburg, formed in 1993 by guitarist and vocalist Tom S. Englund and guitarist Dan Bronell. The band was established as a progressive power metal act, differing from the melodic death metal style commonly associated with the Gothenburg music scene. Evergrey's lyrical themes and visual presentation have consistently focused on darker subject matter. After establishing a stable lineup, the band recorded its debut album, "The Dark Discovery", in 1996, which was released in 1998.

Babylon Zoo.bio: Babylon Zoo was a British rock band of the mid-1990s from Wolverhampton, England, fronted by Jas Mann. They were best known for the song "Spaceman", which on its release on January 21, 1996, went straight to Number 1 on the UK singles chart, selling 418,000 copies in the first week of release - a record at the time. Jas Mann had formerly been in an indie music band, called The Sandkings, which he left in 1992, because of musical differences. Babylon

Sofie Letitre.bio: Sofie Letitre (Utrecht, The Netherlands) has a way of doing everything slightly different. After the first piano chord and when her mesmerizing voice kicks in, you are guaranteed to go on a blissful journey with sharp emotional edges. Influenced by artist like Fiona Apple, Radiohead and Sia, Sofie takes you from bombastic cynicism to fragile melancholy and back. Curious? She even managed to take this approach to her rendition of Deadmau5' track I Remember, which the DJ/producer himself called "Bloody Amazing!"

Manowar.bio: Manowar is an American heavy metal band from Auburn, New York. Formed in 1980, the band is known for lyrics based on fantasy themes, particularly sword and sorcery, as well as mythology including Norse and Greco-Roman traditions, alongside songs that celebrate heavy metal music and its audience. The group is also associated with a loud and emphatic sound. In 2007, bassist Joey DeMaio stated in an interview that he believed contemporary metal lacked large, epic productions with heavily layered instrumentation.

Till Lindemann.bio: Till Lindemann (born 4 January 1963) is a German singer and songwriter. He is the lead vocalist and lyricist of Neue Deutsche Härte band Rammstein and the side project Lindemann. Rammstein has sold over 25 million records worldwide, with five of their albums receiving platinum status, and Lindemann has been listed among the "50 Greatest Metal Frontmen of All Time" by Roadrunner Records. He has also appeared in minor film roles and has published three books of poetry, presenting some of his original poems and scripts to galleries.

Teenage Wrist.bio: Teenage Wrist is an American alternative rock band from Los Angeles that formed in 2014 by Kamtin Mohager (bass, vocals), Anthony Salazar (drums) and Marshall Gallagher (guitars, vocals). In 2015 the band released their debut EP Dazed. In late 2017, they announced they had signed to Epitaph Records, the label published the bands first full length album, Chrome Neon Jesus in 2018. In 2019, founding member Kamtin Mohager departed from the band. In 2021, the group's second album Earth is a Black Hole was released.

Air.bio: There are at least five artists with this name: 1. A popular electronic music duo from France 2. A jazz trio from the United States 3. A noise rock band from Japan 4. An alias of ambient musician Pete Namlook 5. A pop group from the Philippines 1) The French band Air is a duo consisting of Nicolas Godin and Jean-Benoit Dunckel. They went to school in Versailles, (Lycée Jules Ferry) before forming the band in 1995 . Their critically acclaimed first album Premiers Symptômes was followed by the release of Moon Safari

Modern Day Babylon.bio: Modern Day Babylon is a djent project of Tomáš Raclavský formed in Teplice, Czech Republic. Members: Tomáš Raclavský - guitar, studio bass, drum programming, synth, production, etc. Přemysl Kunčický - bass Petr Hataš - drums Here, you can get the 2011's EP Manipulation Theory The 2013's album Travelers is out now. For more info check out: www.moderndaybabylon.net www.facebook.com/moderndaybabylon http://moderndaybabylon.bandcamp.com

The Totemist.bio: Long time friends Rabea Massaad (TOSKA) and Liam Kearley (Black Peaks) have wanted to create music together and in February 2021, they went into the studio having never created music together and made the EP 2880 - created in two days, by two dudes. credits released May 2, 2021 Guitar - Rabea Massaad Drums - Liam Kearley Bass - Rabea Massaad Synth - Rabea Massaad Recorded at Brighton Electric Studios Engineered by Alex Gordon Mixed by Rabea Massaad Mastered by Mark Roberts Bandcamp: https://rabeamassaad.bandcamp.com/album/2880

Depeche Mode.bio: Depeche Mode is an English electronic band formed in Basildon, Essex, in 1980. The original line-up included Dave Gahan, Martin Gore, Andy Fletcher, and Vince Clarke. Since 2022, the band has consisted of Gahan and Gore. With Clarke as the primary songwriter, Depeche Mode released their debut album "Speak & Spell" in 1981 during the British new wave movement and were part of the MTV-driven Second British Invasion. Clarke left the band later that year and went on to form the groups Yazoo and Erasure.

Temple of the Dog.bio: Temple of the Dog was an American rock supergroup formed in Seattle, Washington, in 1990. It was created by Chris Cornell of Soundgarden as a tribute to Andrew Wood, the late lead singer of Malfunkshun and Mother Love Bone. The lineup featured Stone Gossard on rhythm guitar and Jeff Ament on bass guitar, both previously of Mother Love Bone and later members of Pearl Jam, along with Mike McCready on lead guitar and Matt Cameron on drums. Eddie Vedder contributed guest lead and backing vocals and later joined Pearl Jam as its lead vocalist.

Amon Tobin.bio: Amon Tobin (born Amon Adonai Santos de Araújo Tobin on February 7, 1972) is a Brazilian musician, composer and producer of electronic music. He is described as a virtuoso sound designer and is considered to be one of the most influential electronic music artists in the world. He is noted for his unusual methodology in sound design and music production. He has released seven major studio albums under the London-based Ninja Tune record label. It began for him sometime in the mid-90s when

Maximum the Hormone.bio: Maximum the Hormone is an nu metal band formed in Hachioji, Tokyo in 1997 with the line-up of Daisuke-han (vocals), SUGI (guitar), KEY (bass) and Nao Kawakita (drums). The band played a mixture of alternative metal, hardcore punk, hip-hop, pop punk, and various other forms of music within that framework. After releasing their first album A.S.A. Crew through indies label SkyRecord, SUGI and KEY left the band and the present line-up of Daisuke-han, Maximum the Ryo-kun
similar: マキシマム ザ ホルモン

Essenger.bio: Essenger is an electronic artist from Kansas City, Missouri. Essenger stands on the cutting edge of electronic music, blending an array of influences for a sound that pays tribute to modern alt rock, EDM, and synthwave. The artist marks his highly personal songwriting style with striking lyrics, and the result is a singular creative style that opens new possibilities for both retro and future synth music. The artist’s adept compositional skills

SNAYX.bio: The British Alt-Rock band hailing from the South East, are renowned for ferocious live sets and visceral, hard-hitting bass riffs

Liquid State.bio: There are multiple bands with this name: 1) Liquid State is a San Francisco bay area based band, a collaboration between vocalist Jennifer Wilde and multi instrumentalist Mark Fassett. The music is mostly acoustic, featuring acoustic guitars, upright bass, drums, hand percussion, piano, trumpet... but also synthesizer and electronic beats. Their self released debut CD, "Late Bloom," was released in March of 2008. Liquid State sounds something like Suzanne Vega, David Sylvian, Beck's "Sea Change," Hem, and Sufjan Stevens.

Jigsaw Youth.bio: An all-girl feminist punk/grunge band from NYC. https://jigsawyouth.bandcamp.com/

Beartooth.bio: Beartooth is a band from Columbus, Ohio started by Caleb Shomo (Class, ex-Attack Attack!) Current members: Caleb Shomo - Vocals (2012-present) Zach Huston - Guitar (2018-present) Oshie Bichar- Bass (2014-present) (ex-City Lights) Will Deely - rhythm guitar (2021-present) Connor Denis – drums, percussion (2016–present) Former members: Taylor Lumley - Guitar (2013-2018) Nick Reed – bass, backing vocals (2013–14) Brandon Mullins – drums, percussion (2013–16)

a flood of circle.bio: a flood of circle (ア フラッド オブ サークル) is a Japanese alternative rock band from Tokyo/Shimokitazawa which formed in 2006. Members: Vocals & Guitar: 佐々木亮介 (Ryousuke Sasaki; since 2006) Guitar: アオキテツ (Tetsu Aoki; support since 2016, member since 2018) Bass: Guitar: HISAYO (since 2010) Drums: 渡邊一丘 (Kazutaka Watanabe; since 2006) Ex members: Guitar: 岡庭匡志 (Masashi Okaniwa;from 2006 to 2009) Bass: 石井康崇 (Yasutaka Ishii; from 2006 to 2010)

Bone Cult.bio: Driving beats and synths power Bone Cult's sound which is best described as 'Death Electronica' . A hybrid of rock band and electronic production, the duo break the conventions and defy all expectations of genre. Since the release of their debut album Death Electronica the duo have reached new audiences after their inclusion in the horror cinema hit Terrifier 2 Soundtrack for their track Fever to Stay taken from the debut. Taking inspiration from Daft Punk

Mariko Goto.bio: Goto Mariko (後藤まりこ) is a singer from Osaka, Japan signed to DefSTAR Records. She is best known for being the former singer and guitarist of the jazz fusion group ミドリ (Midori). Goto entered the public eye during the success of her band ミドリ. The members of Midori first met when they were members of two different bands. Goto was a member of the band うさぎ (Usagi) along with Kuwano Yoshifumi. The two were friends with a fellow band in Osaka known as Teromeya.

Cartoon Theory.bio: Cartoon Theory is a modern progressive, groovy and ambidjent music project with cartoon-esque and asian vibes which has been created by Maxime Lathiere in Limoges, France. The project also features several guest musicians including Travis Orbin, Luke Martin, Plini RH and David Abad Segovia.

Himalayas.bio: Himalayas is an Indie / Garage band from Cardiff comprising Joe Williams (rhythm guitar and vocals), Mike Griffiths (lead guitar and vocals), Louis Heaps (Bass) and James Goulbourn (drums). The band started in mid 2015 and in December 2015, having cemented their reputation on the South Wales Music scene embarked on a six-date UK tour comprising Cardiff, Bristol, London, Brighton, Manchester and Liverpool. Sonically the band offer seductive guitar lines and hip bass parts that will groove you to the core, all driven by an avalanche of drums.

Protest the Hero.bio: Progressive metal band from Whitby, ON., Canada. Originally named Happy Go Lucky. The band changed their name to Protest the Hero shortly before releasing their debut EP, Search for the Truth, in 2002. In 2005, the band released their first full-length album, Kezia, on the indie label Underground Operations. On January 23, 2006, the band signed with Vagrant Records for Kezia's American release on April 4, 2006. Their second full-length album, Fortress

Alessandro Cortini.bio: Alessandro Cortini is an Italian electronic musician. Cortini has created remarkable, brooding work out of nothing. He creates a rich and dreamlike sonic universe of surprisingly emotional sound scapes and textures. Cortini is known for touring as the keyboardist for Nine Inch Nails from 2004 to 2008 and again from 2013. He is the frontman for the Los Angeles based electronic-alternative band Modwheelmood and was the rhythm guitarist for The Mayfield Four from 2000 to 2002.

Shelf Lives.bio: Taking inspiration from the likes of Peaches and Gilla Band, Toronto-born vocalist Sabrina Di Giulio and Northampton guitarist / producer Jonny Hillyard aim to put “the punk back into house parties”. They’re a bit like if Confidence Man were formed in a grotty London squat. Meshing electroclash, dance, electronica and hardcore, Shelf Lives’ maximalist sound distorts the party until it’s something horrifying and grotesque. Much like an abominably debauched afters

Billy Vicente.bio: Billy Vicente is an artist from the midwest USA who makes shoegaze and post punk goth music

ヌンチャク.bio: Japanese Hardcore/Metal with Japanese Hard Core Legend Vocalist TATSURO MUKAI Leader in KCHC (Kashiwa City Hard Core) Music Scene. NUNCHAKU is : MUKAI (Vo) / KUNI (Vo) / MIZOGUCHI (G) / KOJIMA (B) / HIZUMI (Dr)

Inferiority Complex.bio: Inferiority Complex are a progressive metal quintet from Melbourne. Drawing on a diverse array of musical influences from pop punk to metalcore, they've created their own unique sound.

Nevertel.bio: Nevertel was formed in 2013 by Raul Lopez, Jeremy Michael, and Alec Davis. Though the three of them were all in different parts of the country, they all shared the same goal. With Raul in Boston, and Jeremy and Alec in two different locations of Florida, a challenge had presented itself. Together they worked on digitally assembling over 50 songs and they eventually cut it down to the 10 songs on Living Fiction. Later that year Raul returned to Florida

Gezan.bio: Formed in Osaka in 2009, GEZAN (下山) is a Japanese alternative rock band based in Tokyo as of 2012. GEZAN has become popular across their home country for their high-speed noise, punk, hardcore, and psych-rock music. The band has so far released six albums: かつて うた といわれたそれ (2012); 凸-DECO- (2014); NEVER END ROLL (2016); Silence Will Speak (2018); 狂 (KLUE, 2020); and あのち (Anochi, 2023), with Million Wish Collective.

Covet.bio: There are two bands with the name covet: Covet (Math rock band from San Jose) Based in San Jose and consisting of guitarist Yvette Young, bassist Brandon Dove, and drummer Jessica Burdeaux. CoVet (side project by Michael Areklett) Former bassist of the rock band London After Midnight, making dark pop with a strong soundtrack feeling.

Bush.bio: Bush are an English rock band formed in London in 1992. As of 2025, the lineup consists of lead vocalist and rhythm guitarist Gavin Rossdale, lead guitarist Chris Traynor, bassist Corey Britz, and drummer Nik Hughes. In 1994, the band achieved commercial success outside the United Kingdom with the release of their debut album "Sixteen Stone", which was certified six-times multi-platinum by the Recording Industry Association of America. Bush have

In Search of Sun.bio: In Search Of Sun are a new band that are bursting onto the underground scene with an abundance of head bopping beats, epic choruses and ambient soundscapes. Band Members: ADAM LEADER - Vocals RORY KAY - Guitars DAVID MENA FERRER - Guitars FAZ COURI - Bass SEAN GORMAN - Drums & Backing Vox

Velvet Revolver.bio: Velvet Revolver was an American hard rock supergroup consisting of Guns N'Roses members Slash (lead guitar), Duff McKagan (bass, backing vocals) and Matt Sorum (drums, backing vocals), alongside Dave Kushner (rhythm guitar) formerly of punk band Wasted Youth, and Scott Weiland (lead vocalist) formerly of Stone Temple Pilots. The band formed in 2002 and was active until 2008, when Weiland left the band abruptly to rejoin Stone Temple Pilots. In 2004, the band achieved commercial success with their debut album, Contraband.

Kingdom of Giants.bio: Kingdom of Giants (known as Hello Sailor until 2011) is a metalcore band from California Bay. The band consists of Dana Willax (Vocals/Engineering), Stephen Rezza (Vocals/Keys/Engineering), Stephen Martin (Guitar), Max Bremer (Guitar), Levi Norris (Bass) and Truman Berlin (Drums). Their first EP Abominable was recently released on Jan 25, 2011.

South Central.bio: “We just do what we feel like,” said Rob as he marched off stage and straight past me after a sold out and heaving La Machine du Moulin Rouge in Paris. I run up the stairs and round the corner to find him in the green room necking a bottle of vodka while people fight for his attention. Keith walks in behind me to an onslaught of high-fives, affirming salutes, bottles of champagne popping all over the place. I need to do my interview before things get too messy.

Slough Feg.bio: 'The Lord Weird Slough Feg', whose name is derived from a character in the British mythology-inspired Sláine comics, originates from Central Pennsylvania. The band relocated to San Francisco, California in 1990 in hopes of resurrecting the ailing 'true metal' scene in the United States. The band quickly gained a reputation as, 'The band San Francisco loved to hate' by taking a stand against the trends and incorporating stage savagery with strong visual imagery.

Danzig.bio: Danzig is a heavy metal band from the United States, formed in 1987, in Lodi, New Jersey. The band serves as a musical outlet for singer/composer Glenn Danzig, and their music fuses dark lyrics and imagery with blues influenced music. Danzig can be seen as the third stage in Glenn Danzig's musical career, preceded by the punk band The Misfits and the deathrock band Samhain. On July 14, 1986, Samhain performed what was to be their final show, at The Ritz in New York City.

Daughters.bio: Daughters was an American Mathgrind (early) / noise rock (recent) band formed in 2002, in Providence, Rhode Island, USA, after the break-up of As the Sun Sets. The band's current line-up consists of vocalist Alexis S.F. Marshall, guitarist Nicholas Andrew Sadler, drummer Jon Syverson, and bassist Samuel Moorehouse Walker. Daughters' early material was primarily described as grindcore while their later material has been described as noise rock.
similar: The Number Twelve Looks Like You

Haywyre.bio: Haywyre consists of Martin Vogt, who has been studying piano since the age of six. His interest in the production of hip-hop, dubstep and electronica began growing when he lived in Austria. Shortly after moving back to the United States, in 2008, Haywyre was created. He is studying Music Business and has gotten a reasonable amount of attention for combining synthetic and organic elements in his compositions as well as providing a wide variety of music including glitch-hop, jazz, dubstep and more.

Straightener.bio: ストレイテナー (STRAIGHTENER) is a Japanese alternative/indie rock band. Members: Vocals, guitar, keyboard: [member]ホリエアツシ[/member] (Atsushi Horie) Also plays guitar, keyboards and sings for the band FULLARMOR and has a solo project named ent. Drums: [member]ナカヤマシンペイ[/member] (Shinpei Nakayama) Also played drums in the band, The Predators, another sunnyday and Zantö) with Sawao Yamanaka (guitar, The Pillows), and Jiro (bass, Glay) .

Anup Sastry.bio: Anup Sastry is a drummer from Maryland, USA. Sastry made a name for himself in the early 2010s, performing drum covers of progressive metal artists such as Skyharbor and Vildhjarta. He currently drums for Intervals and Skyharbor, and is the touring drummer of guitarist Jeff Loomis. Sastry also writes his own music; programming guitar and bass riffs and performing his own drum parts. Sastry's debut album 'Ghost' was released independently on March 22nd 2013 and is available for pay-what-you-want download on his BandCamp.

Walking Across Jupiter.bio: Walking Across Jupiter is a young formation from St. Petersburg, Russian Federation. Formed by Alexander Valitov in 2009. Atmospheric music which includes many genres. All works are full of different feelings. First LP 'Scent' was released in 2013 . Members: Alexander Valitov - guitars Nikita Valamin - vox Artem Saschenko - bass Ilgiz Iunusov - drums Links to listen and download: https://soundcloud.com/walkingacrossjupiter http://vk.com/walking_across_jupiter

Cynthoni.bio: Cynthoni [they/them] (stylized CYNth𝑜𝓃𝒾) is an electronic/dnb/jungle music project created by Australian artist Sewerslvt. They currently have two albums and four EPs released as follows: May 09th, 2024 (Sewerslvt Presents: Cynthoni, Pt. 1)August 02nd, 2024 (Sewerslvt Presents: Cynthoni, Pt. 2)October 04th, 2024 (SMOKE IT TO THE BUTT)January 25th, 2025 (Draining Love Story (In The Eyes Of Cynthoni))September 18th, 2025 (Lost in Her Virtual Eyes)December 04th, 2025 (LAMENTING MALICE)

Babebee.bio: Babebee, pronounced “baby,” the musical moniker of the elusive, Korean American artist (they/them pronouns), is an idiosyncratic soloist who could only exist now, in the modern era: prolific, collaborative, convention-breaking, endlessly ambitious, and impossible to define. Call them hyperpop, and you’d miss out on their experimental dance signatures, their penchant for bedroom pop melodicism. Call them a singer and you miss out on their expert production work.

Bad Nerves.bio: Bad Nerves are a 5-piece Powerpop-RocknRoll band from East London. The bastard child of a Ramones/Strokes one night stand, they play ferociously fast distorted pop songs, with melodies that hook so deep, even the most skilled lobotomist would struggle to scrape them out. BOBBY NERVES on vocals, WILLIAM PHILLIPSON and GEORGE BERRY on guitars, JONATHAN POULTON on bass and SAMUEL THOMPSON on drums.

Crystal Lake.bio: There are multiple acts called Crystal Lake. 1) Japanese metal band from Tokyo 2) A hardstyle duo. 3) Polish progressive rock/metal band 4) Brazilian thrash metal band 5) Dutch thrash metal band, later renamed to Crustacean 6) Hip-hop group from Huddersfield, UK. Released first EP under this name in 1999, then later albums in 2006 and 2011. 1) Crystal Lake is a Japanese djent/metalcore band from Tokyo, formed in 2002. The band comprises vocalist John Robert Centorrino

Aaron F. Bianchi Jupiter.bio: Available to compose and produce music for video games and films. Contact me for paid work at: aaronf.bianchi@gmail.com

Amira Elfeky.bio: Amira Lynne Elfeky is an American-Egyptian singer/songwriter based out of Los Angeles, California. Amira, a Connecticut native, met producer/multi-instrumentalist, Brandon Iljas in 2017 by posting a “drummer wanted” ad. Together, they have crafted several tracks in Brandon’s DIY bedroom studio in San Francisco. Their production style combines the synth and guitar tones of lo-fi bedroom pop with modern vocals and boom bap style drums. Having dealt with anxiety and depression for most of her life, Amira sought solace in music.

similar: One Step Closer [Official HD Music Video]

Fit For A King.bio: Fit For A King is a metalcore band from Dallas, Texas, United States formed in 2007. The band comprises vocalist Ryan Kirby, bassist-vocalist Ryan "Tuck" O'Leary, guitarists Bobby Lynge and Daniel Gailey, and drummer Trent Celaya. The band was formed in Dallas, Texas in 2007 by Jared Easterling, Aaron Decur, Justin Juno, Jared McFerron, Alex Danforth and Jed McNeill. The band had performed locally and regionally and released two EPs in their beginning years.

Static-X.bio: Static-X is an American industrial metal band formed in Los Angeles, California, in 1994. The band's lineup has changed several times throughout its history, although founder, vocalist, rhythm guitarist, and frontman Wayne Static remained its central member until his death in 2014. The band was founded by Wayne Static and drummer Ken Jay following the dissolution of Static's previous group, Deep Blue Dream, which had also included future "The Smashing Pumpkins" vocalist Billy Corgan.

Forbidden.bio: 1. Forbidden is an American thrash metal band from the San Francisco Bay Area, California. They were formed in 1985 as Forbidden Evil and changed their name to Forbidden in 1987. The band has experienced multiple breakups and reunions, with several line-up changes. Forbidden initially disbanded in 1997, reunited in 2007, went on an indefinite hiatus in 2012, and reformed again in 2023. The current line-up consists of Norman Skinner (vocals), Craig Locicero (guitar), Matt Camacho (bass), and Chris Kontos (drums).

Black Orchid Empire.bio: Black Orchid Empire create dense, powerful rock music that combines fearless complexity, aggressive heaviness and beautiful, hook-laden melody. Band Members: Paul Visser – Vocals/Guitars Dave Ferguson – Bass/Vocals Billy Freedom - Drums

Fluke.bio: A three-piece combo who managed to make inspired music during the 1990s while blazing a path through most of the popular electronic styles of the decade, Fluke was formed by occasional vocalist Jon Fugler, Mike Tournier and Mike Bryant after the three had spent several years living in the same combination house and recording studio. At first an eclectic, widely inspired house-pop act, the three later moved into trip-hop (and the British charts) during

aiko.bio: There's more than one artist with this name: 1) aiko (born Aiko Yanai (柳井愛子) on November 22, 1975 in Suita, Osaka Prefecture, Japan) is a Japanese pop singer-songwriter. She studied popular music vocals at the Osaka College of Music, graduating in 1996. aiko is known for her cheerful disposition. Her music, which is primarily love songs, reaches a wide audience all over East Asia and beyond. Oricon published the results of a survey with 20,000 subscribers' favourite singers, and aiko headed the list.
similar: サザンオールスターズ

Intrøspect.bio: Intrøspect is an emerging progressive metal band hailing from Sydney, Australia. Their unique flavour of progressive metal blends post-rock and ambient influences to create a spiritual atmosphere in which the transcendent extremes of the human experience are given proper sonic treatment. In their 2019 Debut EP Ad Astra, the quartet combines soaring vocal melodies, complex riffs and heavy grooves to construct a sonic landscape drenched in emotion, and imbued with passion and hope.

Weezer.bio: Weezer is an American alternative rock band formed in Los Angeles, California in 1992. They have released 15 full length albums, 1 cover album, six EPs, and a DVD. Their latest album, titled 'Van Weezer', came out on May 7th, 2021, Although, they released 4 EPs in 2022, known as SZNZ. They've sold over ten million records in the United States to date, and 35 million worldwide. They play music influenced by alternative bands and power pop artists among others.

Strawberry Girls.bio: There are at least two bands called Strawberry Girls. (1) Strawberry Girls is an instrumental trio from Salinas, CA, featuring Zachary Garren (Ex-Dance Gavin Dance) on guitar, Ben Rosett on drums, and Ian Jennings on bass. The project began as a duo in April 2011 when Ben Rosett (drums) and Zachary Garren (guitar) decided to attempt writing music together for fun. It worked so well that they decided to keep writing and eventually performed together.

Traverser.bio: Traverser opens the floodgates of creative, intense and contemplative progressive/alternative rock on unsuspecting audiences at every performance. Formed in 2004 by David Medairos (guitars/vocals), Robert Spence (bass guitar) and David Shaw (drums), three friends imagined a style of music that carried true and honest weight both in concept and execution. Invoking influences ranging from classical music, metal and jazz, to progressive and experimental rock

The Birthday Massacre.bio: The Birthday Massacre is a synth-rock band formed in 1999 and based in Toronto, Ontario, Canada. From 1999 until 2002, the band name was Imagica. The current lineup consists of Chibi (vocals), Rainbow (guitars and programming), Michael Falcore (guitars), Owen (keyboards), Brett Carruthers (bass), and Philip Elliot (drums). Their sound is mostly a fusion of alternative rock, new wave, industrial, and synthpop. So far, TBM have released eight studio albums: Nothing and Nowhere (2002)

I Built the Sky.bio: Based in Melbourne, Australia Rohan ‘Ro Han’ Stevenson has been releasing self produced instrumental guitar music under the name I Built The Sky since 2012. Ranging from complex riffs, shredding guitars, catchy melodic lead work through to intricate acoustic pieces, Ro has gained worldwide attention as one to watch among the modern guitar community. Over the last few years Ro has transformed what was essentially a studio project into a fully fledged live band

Within Destruction.bio: Within Destruction is a deathcore (early)/alternative metal band that formed in 2010 in Jessenice, Slovenia. They have played as support acts for Suicide Silence, Benighted and Belphegor, as well as appearing several times on the biggest Slovenian metal festival Metaldays. Discography: From the Depths - 2012Void - 2016Deathwish - 2018Yōkai - 2020Lotus - 2022Rebirth (EP) - 2023ANIMETAL - 2025 Website

Dreamer Isioma.bio: Dreamer Isioma (they/he) is a Nigerian-American singer-songwriter, guitarist and multiple-genre artist based in Chicago. After Isioma's breakout song "Sensitive" went viral on TikTok in 2020, they released a debut album titled Goodnight Dreamer in 2022. His second album, Princess Forever, was released in 2023 by AWAL. Isioma’s third album, StarX Lover, is due for release in Spring 2025.

Emmure.bio: Emmure is an American metalcore band formed in 2003. Originally based in New Fairfield, Connecticut, the group later relocated to Queens, New York. Emmure has released eight studio albums, beginning with an EP titled "The Complete Guide to Needlework" in 2006. The band signed with Victory Records early in their career. Their debut full-length album, "Goodbye to the Gallows," was released in 2007 under the label, followed by "The Respect Issue" (2008), "Felony" (2009), and "Speaker of the Dead" (2011).

Darko US.bio: Darko US is an American deathcore / metalcore duo formed in 2020 by multi-instrumentalist and producer Josh Miller (Spite, ex-Emmure) and vocalist Tom Barber (Chelsea Grin, ex-Lorna Shore). The project is recognized for its technical precision, cinematic production, and use of electronic and ambient textures within modern deathcore. Their catalogue includes Pt. 1 Dethmask (2020), Darko (2021), Oni (2022), Dethmask, Pt. 2 (2022), Starfire (2024), and DETHMASK 3 (2025).

Attila.bio: There are at least six artists named Attila: (1) A metal band from Atlanta, GA. (2) A traditional heavy metal band from New York City formed in 1983. (3) A Dutch power/speed metal band formed in 1983. (4) Psychedelic group including Billy Joel that released one self-titled album. (5) A French DJ who produces flashcore, glitch, noise, and experimental music. (6) An Italian dancehall singer. (1) Party metal rockers Attila formed in their hometown of Atlanta in 2005, meeting each other at their high school and through mutual friends.
similar: We Butter the Bread with Butter
サイコパス2 Psycho Pass 2 Ending ED Full

George Carlin.bio: George Denis Patrick Carlin (May 12, 1937 - June 23, 2008) was a Grammy-winning American stand-up comedian, actor, and author. Carlin is especially noted for his irreverent attitude and his observations on language, psychology, and religion along with many taboo subjects. In fact, Carlin and his "Seven Dirty Words" comedy routine were central to the 1978 U.S. Supreme Court case F.C.C. v. Pacifica Foundation, in which a narrow 5-4 decision by the justices affirmed the government's right to regulate the public airwaves.

Emilie Autumn.bio: Emilie Autumn Liddell (born on September 22, 1979), better known by her stage name Emilie Autumn, is an American singer-songwriter, poet, and violinist. Autumn draws influence for her music—the style of which she has alternatively labeled as "Victoriandustrial" and glam rock—from plays, novels, and history, particularly the Victorian era. Performing with her all-female backing band The Bloody Crumpets, Autumn incorporates elements of classical music, cabaret, electronica, and glam rock with theatrics, and burlesque.

ZZ Top.bio: ZZ Top is an American rock band formed in Houston, Texas, in 1969. The group is known for its blend of blues rock, boogie rock, and hard rock, as well as for the distinctive visual image of guitarist and vocalist Billy Gibbons and bassist and vocalist Dusty Hill, who for many years appeared with long beards, sunglasses, and hats. For more than five decades, the band's line-up consisted of Gibbons, Hill, and drummer Frank Beard. Following Hill's death in 2021, longtime guitar technician Elwood Francis became the band's bassist.

Another Animal.bio: Hard Rock supergroup Another Animal was formed when Godsmack members Tony Rombola, Shannon Larkin, and Robbie Merill went to the studio and recorded music. After writing almost 40 songs, only 14 were used on the album, IV. The left-over songs were then recycled by the members after they recruited Whitfield Crane of Ugly Kid Joe and Another Animal was born.

Skid Row.bio: 1. Skid Row is an American heavy metal band formed in 1986 in Toms River, New Jersey. Its current lineup consists of bassist Rachel Bolan, guitarists Dave Sabo and Scotti Hill, and drummer Rob Hammersmith. The band achieved commercial success in the late 1980s and early 1990s with its first two albums, "Skid Row" (1989) and "Slave to the Grind" (1991), both of which were certified multi-platinum. "Slave to the Grind" reached number one on the Billboard 200.

Skeletonwitch.bio: Skeletonwitch is a blackened thrash metal band that formed in 2003. The band formed in Athens, Ohio by Nate Garnette (Guitar), Scott Hedrick (Guitar),Chance Garnette (Vocals), Evan Linger (Bass), Derick Nau (Drums). In 2011, Derrick Nau left the band, replaced by Dustin Boltjes. Skeletonwitch released their first album, At One with the Shadows, on August 11, 2004 with Shredded Records. The band then released a quick fiery demo in 2004 and then released an EP Worship the Witch in 2005

OXYMORRONS.bio: Oxymorrons began as a collaboration between Queens-bred brothers, K.I. and Deee, who joined forces to cultivate a sound that modern music can no longer turn its back on. After years spent playing and touring with other groups, the brothers decided it was time to set the New York scene ablaze with their sonic creations. The lineup expanded with the addition of staple drummer Matty Mayz, whose blistering talent along side bassist Joe Jordan and guitarist Adam November has become an intricate part of the bands live show.

Wayside.bio: There are multiple artists named Wayside: 1) Wayside are a Swedish punk band with shoegaze influences. Straight from in-between the lakes of Stockholm, Sweden comes punk quartet Wayside bringing flavors from both Emo and Shoegaze to give you their hard-hitting and honest sound. After a few member changes the line-up finally landed in it’s complete form, Adam Hagerum(Vocals, Guitar), Emanuel Wildung(Vocals, Guitar), Jens Kokko(Bass) and Sverker Andrenius(Drums).

Lacuna Coil.bio: Lacuna Coil is an Italian gothic metal band from Milan. Since their formation in 1994, the group has had two name changes, being previously known as Sleep of Right and Ethereal. Inspired by the combination of gothic imagery and music, the members have been known, musically, for composing mid-tempo songs consisting of prominent guitar lines and contrasting dual female/male vocal harmonies to help create a melodic, detached sound. Much of the band's recent material

Halestorm.bio: Halestorm is a rock band from Pennsylvania (US) that signed a recording contract with Atlantic Records, a major recording label, on June 28, 2005. The group has released four albums on Atlantic: "Halestorm" (2009), "The Strange Case Of..." (2012), "Into the Wild Life" (2015), and "Vicious" (2018); won a Grammy for "Love Bites (So Do I)", and charted three #1 singles on Mainstream Rock. The group has been consistently active, writing and performing original music since the mid-1990's and becoming increasingly successful.

The Messenger Birds.bio: The Messenger Birds is not a rock band—it’s just two guys. But there is more to this two-piece than meets the eye and a lot of noise to back it up. The Messenger Birds have earned a reputation for an abrasively loud and energetic live show, and their Detroit brand of alt-rock seamlessly blends monster riffs, catchy hooks, intricate melodies, droning synthesizers, and unapologetic lyrics into a coalescence of sub-genres, a sound that all at once is difficult to categorize as simply alternative rock.

Ice Nine Kills.bio: Ice Nine Kills is an post-hardcore/metalcore band from Boston, Massachusetts. The band is currently signed to Fearless Records and is known for its horror-themed lyrics and theatrical style. It was originally formed in 2000 by high school friends Spencer Charnas and Jeremy Schwartz. Charnas remains the only founding member still active in the band. Ice Nine Kills has released three extended plays and six full-length studio albums. These include "Last Chance to Make Amends," "Safe Is Just a Shadow," and "The Predator Becomes the Prey.

Sleeping With Sirens.bio: Sleeping With Sirens is an American post-hardcore band from Orlando, Florida, United States currently residing in Grand Rapids, Michigan, United States, formed in 2009 by members of For All We Know, Broadway and We Are Defiance. The band is currently signed to Sumerian Records and has released seven full-length albums, With Ears to See and Eyes to Hear, Let's Cheers To This, Feel, Madness, Gossip, How It Feels to Be Lost and Complete Collapse. The first album debuted at number 7 on Billboard's Top Heatseekers chart

Discrepancies.bio: There are several artists using this name: 1. Aberdeen SD band from the mid to late 90's. 2. Discrepancies started in 2003 with two friends from High School, Addison Bracher and Garrett Weakley, who sought to pursue music for the rest of their lives. After finding members with similar interests, they recruited Vocalist, Antonio ATG Metalcalf, and Bassist, Daniel Maddy. The four agreed on what they would strive to accomplish and allow everyone's ideas to blend, creating an innovative sound.

IMMERSE.bio: bandcamp facebook Formed In: 2012 Origins: Leicester, UK. London, UK. Genre: Technical Metal/ Technical Metalcore Record Label: Unsigned Band Members Tom Gardner Joel Scott Ben Sutherland Kris Aarre BIO Technical/Progressive Metal from Leicester/ London. Immerse started as Connor Sweeney's solo project and over two years has evolved into a band with a debut EP out on 25.2.13.

Invent Animate.bio: Invent Animate (originally spelled as Invent, Animate) is a progressive metalcore band from Port Neches, TX, formed in 2011. After their formation they immediately began working on their debut EP Waves, which was prominently featured on djent-oriented blogs at the time of its release one year later. The band's debut and sophomore LPs Everchanger and Stillworld were released in 2014 and 2017 respectively, with the latter being their last record with founding vocalist Ben English who departed from the scene - albeit returning 8 years later

Taproot.bio: Taproot is an American nu metal/alternative rock band. Hailing from Ann Arbor, Michigan, Taproot formed in late 1997, and has toured with Deftones, Sevendust, Incubus, and Korn. In 1997, Taproot sent their demo to Limp Bizkit frontman Fred Durst. Impressed with their material, he offered to get them a recording contract through Interscope Records. However, after extended negotiations, Taproot looked elsewhere, where they finally landed a lucrative record deal with Atlantic Records.

Maserati.bio: Maserati is a band from Athens, Georgia, United States whose sound has been described as a combination of post-rock and psychedelic influences. Their music is instrumental, relying on the standard rock instrumentation of guitar, bass, and drums. They are currently signed to Temporary Residence Limited, based in Brooklyn, New York. The band formed in January 2000 and in 2001 self-released their debut EP, 37:29:24. They released a full-length, The Language of Cities, on Kindercore Records in 2002.

Nolo.bio: 1. Solo artist from Copenhagen, Denmark. nolo is a solo music project from Jesper Vega, Copenhagen, Denmark, working with computer, guitars, a bit of field recording and samples and a bit vocals. The project startet out in 2004, and has featured collaborations with nolo's alter ego Dean Vega. Released the EP 'Slacker City' in 2008, and the remix 'Milljion - We Have Hearts - Vice RMX' in 2015. Soundcloud: https://soundcloud.com/soundofnolo Twitter: https://twitter.com/soundofnolo

Elderbrook.bio: Alexander Kotz, known professionally as Elderbrook, is a English musician, songwriter and producer. His career as a musician began in 2015, when he released his first EP, which contained the song, "How Many Times" which went on to be remixed by German duo Andhim, becoming one of Mixmag's best songs of 2015.

October Ends.bio: October Ends are a 5 piece Hardcore band from the north east of England. With a passion for performing and delivering a great show, October Ends shows no boundaries with their heavy breakdowns and powerful melodic lead sections. Founded in 2010 by rhythm guitarist John Paul Patterson, drummer Matt Eland, and vocalist Simon Mitchell, they aimed to deliver performances that would break the moulds of chart music and breathe life back into the hardcore genre.

Delta Sleep.bio: Formerly known as Sàvlön, Delta Sleep is a British math rock quartet from Canterbury, Kent, England. Formed in 2010, Delta Sleep is driven by a desire to create fun and original, forward-thinking music. Their songs combine guitar driven melodies, held together by jazz influenced percussion and relentless bass, topped with sprinkles of passionate shouts and lulling chants. Originally from Canterbury, the quartet moved to Brighton and started playing in earnest.

Focus.bio: There is more than one artist with this name. 1. Focus is a Dutch progressive rock band formed in Amsterdam in 1969 by keyboardist, vocalist, and flautist Thijs van Leer, drummer Hans Cleuver, bassist Martijn Dresden, and guitarist Jan Akkerman. The band has undergone numerous line-up changes; since December 2016, it has comprised Van Leer, drummer Pierre van der Linden, guitarist Menno Gootjes, and bassist Udo Pannekeet. They have sold one million RIAA-certified albums in the United States.

Melleefresh vs. Deadmau5.bio: Melleefresh and Deadmau5 are two separate artists constantly collaborating with each other since 2006. They have released a number of singles through Play Digital, and their musical direction can be described as progressive house with obscene lyrics.

brototype.bio: BROTOTYPE is the artist known as N3b on guitar and his brother on the drums.

Sprints.bio: Sprints are a four-piece post-punk band from Dublin, Ireland. The band consists of Karla Chubb (Lead Singer and songwriter), Colm O’Reilly (Guitar), Jack Callan (Drums), and Sam McCann (Bass). Following 2020 singles Kissing Practise and The Cheek, the band released their debut EP, Manifesto, via Nice Swan Records, in March 2021.

Garbage.bio: Garbage is an American rock band formed in 1993 in Madison, Wisconsin. The band's line-up, consisting of Scottish musician Shirley Manson (vocals) and American musicians Duke Erikson (guitar, bass, keyboards), Steve Marker (guitar, keyboards) and Butch Vig (drums, percussion), has remained unchanged since its formation. All four members contribute to songwriting and production. Garbage has sold more than 17 million albums worldwide. The band's self-titled debut album

Vexes.bio: Rock, Metal, Experimental, Ambient band from Asbury Park, NJ. Vexes was formed from the ashes of previous projects including: A Life Once Lost, Vessl, Fury of Five and Downstage. Band Members: Charlie Berezansky-- Vocals/Guitar/Keys John Klagholz-- Guitar/Keys Bobby Carpenter- Bass Justin Graves-- Drums

aenygma.bio: Alternative Metal band from Vienna, Austria.

BRAHMAN.bio: 1.) BRAHMAN is a Japanese alternative punk band with four members; Kohki-Guitar, Makoto-Bass, Ronzi-Drums and Toshi-Low-Vocals. As their sanskrit name suggests, they bring asian influences to the Western punk genre. They ventured to the US in 2005, following the success in Japan of their album A Forlorn Hope. 2.) Progressive, slightly pop, band from 1971. Released one album. 3.) Brahman is the oldest known persona of Joshua Hamilton, better known as Lil Darkie.

Iota.bio: There is more than one artist with this name: 1) Iota was a short-lived 60's psychedelic rock band from Texas. They released one 45 and recorded an LP's worth of material in Memphis which was posthumously released by Shadoks records and Rockadelic records in the 2000's. 2) iOTA is an Australian musician making his name not only in music, but on stage. He arrived on the music scene in 1999 with his debut album "The Hip Bone Connection". Since then

Porter Robinson.bio: Porter Robinson is an American DJ, record producer, musician, and singer from Chapel Hill, North Carolina. He started his career at the young age of 12, his passion for music being ignited by the media he consumed at the time, especially Dance Dance Revolution. Starting off making music online, he found his way to popularity and was in the mainstream by the end of 2010, after which he started gaining recognition from popular artists. His first EP

Siamese.bio: There are more than one band using the name Siamese: 1) We Are SIAMESE from Copenhagen, Denmark. Previously known as Siamese Fighting Fish. Pre-order our new album on www.siameseband.shop "Copenhagen's SIAMESE have an incredible spirit and deliver a crystal-clear, anthemic sound. More refreshing than a dip in the Nyhavn harbour!" - Alex Baker, Kerrang! Radio "Instantly Catchy Tunes" - Sophie K., Kerrang! 2) An ambient experimental project of Grayson Gilmour, from Wellington, NZ.

Deep Purple.bio: Deep Purple is a British rock band formed in London in 1968. They are regarded as pioneers of heavy metal and modern hard rock, although their musical style has incorporated elements of psychedelic rock, progressive rock, and blues rock over the course of their career. After beginning with a more psychedelic and progressive approach, they moved toward a heavier sound with the 1970 album "Deep Purple in Rock". Along with Led Zeppelin and Black Sabbath

Original God.bio: Based in Los Angeles but with his heart in Japan, experimental artist Original God has found a way to do what he loves in a way that is completely his own. Growing up in Philadelphia, the anarchic singer incorporates elements of manga, anime, philosophy and film into his art while still preaching a message of hope and individualism. Original God has created a distinct sound that has started to capture the attention of many being influenced by various genres such as hip-hop, metal, industrial, pop, and many others.

Marmalade butcher.bio: Japanese Insturumental. Post Rock/Math Rock/Electronica/Chaotic https://soundcloud.com/x2n-1 m-x2n@hotmail.co.jp

Voices From The Fuselage.bio: Voices From The Fuselage are a particularly melodic sound, with influences spanning through genres such as rock, metal, progressive, alternative, orchestral, trip-hop, and ambient. Formed in Northampton in 2010 they released their debut EP "To Hope" in 2011. Vocalist Ashe O'Hara is also known for his stint as vocalist in Tesseract between 2012 and 2014. After leaving Tesseract, and making Voices From the Fuselage his main focus they released their debut full-length Odyssey: The Destroyer of Worlds in 2015.

Alexisonfire.bio: Alexisonfire is a post-hardcore band from St. Catharines, Ontario, Canada. Their music is characterized by turbulent and explosive dynamics with intricate guitar melodies. Consisting of five friends, they formed in late 2001 as the result of a three-band break up. The band’s name is inspired by a lactating contortionist stripper named Alexis Fire. This caused problems when she discovered that they were using her website name (alexisonfire.com) as their band name, and threatened to sue.

Nothing But Thieves.bio: Nothing But Thieves is an English alternative rock band, formed in 2012 in Southend-on-Sea, Essex. They consist of lead vocalist and guitarist Conor Mason, guitarist Joe Langridge-Brown, guitarist and keyboardist Dominic Craik, bassist Philip Blake, and drummer James Price. Their style of music has been compared to the likes of Foals, The Neighbourhood, and Civil Twilight, and they have been played on alternative and active rock radio. Nothing But Thieves’ origins lie in schoolmates Conor

Norma Jean.bio: There are several artists named Norma Jean. 1. Norma Jean is a metalcore / mathcore band from Douglasville, Georgia, USA- a suburb of Atlanta. They were originally known as Luti-Kriss. They released two albums: an EP (titled 5ep) in 2000, and a full-length release, Throwing Myself on March 13, 2001. After the release of Throwing Myself, the band changed their name to Norma Jean to avoid confusion with rapper Ludacris. Their original vocalist, Josh Scogin
similar: Maylene and the Sons of Disaster

Willow.bio: There are at least 9 artists with this name: 1. Willow Smith, an American pop singer 2. An American instrumental group 3. A Belgian indie rock band 4. An American singer/songwriter 5. A Dutch rock band 6. An American math rock band 7. Sophie Wilson, a UK-based producer. 8. German hard rock band, 1990s. 9. A Canadian folk-roots band 10. XO_willow☆, a bedroom punk artist 1. Willow Camille Reign Smith (born October 31, 2000 in Los Angeles, United

Town Portal.bio: Town Portal are a 3-piece progressive, instrumental rock from Copenhagen, Denmark. They have released 5 albums to date: "Vacuum Horror" in 2011, "Chronopoly" in 2012, "The Occident" in 2015, "Of Violence" in 2019, "Grindwork" in 2025. All releases available here: www.townportal.bandcamp.com. For more information, visit https://www.facebook.com/townportalband.
similar: And So I Watch You From Afar

Floral.bio: A math rock group from California. Their self-titled EP was released in September 2014. https://floral1.bandcamp.com/

Johnny Booth.bio: Johnny Booth is a 5 piece hardcore band from Long Island and Oneonta, NY. Johnny Booth is composed of Andrew Herman, Adam Halpern, Nick Martell, Ryan Strong, and Scott Owens. The band recorded their first EP in the summer of 2008, entitled, "Sagua" and have since been touring as often as possible. Johnny Booth can be marked by their crushing melodies, stop-go rhythms, and multi-genre influence. In January of 2012, they released "Connections" on Hotfoot Records

DIR EN GREY.bio: DIR EN GREY (previously typeset as Dir en grey) is a Japanese metal band formed in February 1997. Members: ** Vocals: 京 (Kyo) ** Guitar: 薫 (Kaoru) ** Guitar: Die ** Bass: Toshiya ** Drums: Shinya Biography: With a consistent lineup of vocalist Kyo, guitarists Kaoru and Die, bassist Toshiya, and drummer Shinya, they have released eleven full-length albums. Numerous stylistic changes have made the genre of their music difficult to determine, though it is generally considered to be a form of metal.

TNL VZN.bio: Formed in the UK's industrial second city, Birmingham based TNL VZN are the product when the aggression of 90's alternative rock and grunge collides with melancholy and angst of 2000's Emo. With gnarly guitar and bass riffs, haunting melodies and powerful lyrics that describe scenes of love in all its glory and pain as well as darker themes such as obsession, TNL VZN is painting a new image of modern alternative.

Art Vs Science.bio: Art vs. Science are an Australian alternative/dance band from Sydney, New South Wales; who formed early in 2008. The group consists of Jim Finn on vocals and keyboards; Dan McNamee (styled as Dan Mac) on vocals, guitars and keyboards; and Dan Williams (styled as Dan W., also of Philadelphia Grand Jury) on drums and vocals. The group's debut release was an eponymous Art vs. Science EP in 2009, featuring the singles "Flippers" and "Parlez-vous français?;" the latter of which reached number 2 in the Triple J Hottest 100 of 2009.

Windrunner.bio: Windrunner is a four-piece metalcore band from Hanoi, Vietnam. The group was formed in early 2015 through the merger of two prominent Vietnamese metalcore bands: Epione and Alive In Sight. The band name was inspired by a character from the video game Defense of the Ancients. The current members of Windrunner include vocalist Nân, guitarists Nam Đào and Trung Tone, bassist Trang Chuối, and drummer David Hudd. Former members include vocalist Dương Bùi, guitarists Minh Cò, bassist Híu NT, and drummer Bờm.

Capture.bio: Capture (formerly known as Capture the Crown) is an Australian metalcore band formed in early 2010 after the break-up of another metalcore outfit, Atlanta Takes State. The band rose to prominence when they released the music video for their song, "You Call That a Knife? This Is a Knife!" (2011) on YouTube. The band was signed to Sumerian Records in December 2012 but were dropped in October the following year. Since their formation they have issued two studio albums

Afterlife.bio: There are many bands/artists named Afterlife: 1) Nu metal band from Florida, US. Signed to Hopeless Records, debut album Breaking Point released January 2019. Site: www.afterlifeofficial.com 2) Steve Miller is the writer / producer / remixer behind the blissful beach grooves of Afterlife. This album is the natural progression from working with a number of major artists including K-Klass, Karl Pitterson and Jose Padilla and having tracks included on the Cafe Del Mar series.

Dance With the Dead.bio: Dance with the Dead aka Justin Pointer and Tony Kim are two American musicians from California. They produce electronic music based on melodies made with synthesizers, with the peculiarity of having sounds coming from the 80s, as well as guitar riffs due to their past in Heavy Metal. These two musicians have known each other since childhood and were even neighbors, they’ve played guitar since they’re childs until today in their compositions and in their concerts.

Żabson.bio: Żabson, właściwie Mateusz Zawistowski – polski raper oraz autor tekstów. Członek grupy Chillwagon.

This Will Destroy You.bio: This Will Destroy You was formed by guitarists Chris King and Jeremy Galindo, bassist Raymond Brown and drummer Andrew Miller in San Marcos, Texas, in 2005. They had met through mutual friends, and played together in various different bands throughout high school, before the line-up was finalised by around 2002. Early iterations of the band experimented with vocals, sung by Galindo, but after recording some tracks they decided the results were “awful” and didn’t fit in with the rest of their music.

Ennio Morricone.bio: Ennio Morricone, Grand Officer OMRI (10 November 1928 - 6 July 2020) was an Italian composer, orchestrator, conductor, and former trumpet player. He composed a wide range of music styles, making him one of the most versatile, experimental and influential composers of all time, working in any medium. Since 1946 Morricone composed over 500 scores for cinema and television, as well as over 100 classical works. His filmography includes over 70 award-winning films

Saxon Shore.bio: At the time of recording The Exquisite Death... in June 2005, Matthew Doty (guitar, keyboards) was still working with line-up changes, keeping Stephen Roessner (drums, percussion) and Matthew Stone (guitar) as the only full-time members of the live group. It was not until the band's spring 2006 Japan tour that William Stichter (bass) and Oliver Chapoy (guitar, keyboards) came on board full time. The sound of this new, five-member group was what Saxon Shore wanted to capture with a new album.

Exciter.bio: Exciter is a speed metal band from Ottawa, Ontario, Canada. Despite many changes in personnel, they have managed to keep a small but dedicated following for over 25 years. In 1978 the band Hell Razor formed in Ottawa ON,Canada. The band consisted of John Ricci (guitars), Dan Beehler (drums) and Allan James Johnson (bass). In 1980 they changed their name to Exciter and recorded a demo which they then sent to Mike Varney of Shrapnel Records. Varney included one song from it, "World War III", on the compilation album US Metal Volume II in 1982.

Akaikurage.bio: 赤いくらげ. Tokyo-based Psychedelic-Pop/Art-Rock band.

CVLTE.bio: CVLTE (カルト; pronounced: cult) is Japanese rock band formed in Sapporo in 2017. The band consists of Aviel Kaei (vocals), Takuya Wada (guitar), Hibiki Fujisawa (bass).

Chokecherry.bio: Chokecherry is a band from San Francisco, California

Trails.bio: Trails is the name of at least 4 bands: 1) Trails is Bryan Burgos & Kyle Potter out of Orlando, FL. http://trailsmusic.bandcamp.com https://soundcloud.com/trailsss https://www.facebook.com/trailsss 2) Trails, Oakland, USA. Folk pop bossa nova band. trailmap.bandcamp.com 3) Trails, UK. Forming late 2010, TRAILS are currently ripping the UK a new a-hole on the underground circuit. Their live show is raucous; their music an energetic collision of melodic vocals and heavyweight riffage.

Astrale.bio: Astrale is the electronic project of Trevor Higuera, a producer from California, USA. He creates highly textured and emotional productions that have resonated with the thousands of followers he has amassed on websites like SoundCloud and wave.ac. He released an EP, "The Things You Cannot See," in early February of 2019, followed by three albums: 2021's "Cyberline," 2022's "Aural," and 2023's "pastpresentfuture." He has also released popular collaborations with Puppet & Scro.

KYONO.bio: Vocalist: WAGDUG FUTURISTIC UNITY / T.C.L / !!!KYONO+DJBAKU!!! / T.M.C.M(休止中) Live band: vo:KYONO g:MIYA from MUCC ba:HIROMITSU from AIR SWELL / deadbites dr:DUTTCH from UZMK

Machine Gun Kelly.bio: Colson Baker (born April 22, 1990), known professionally as Machine Gun Kelly or mgk, is an American musician, rapper, singer-songwriter and actor from Ohio. He is noted for his genre duality across alternative rock with hip hop. Machine Gun Kelly released four mixtapes between 2007 and 2010 before signing with Bad Boy Records. He released his debut studio album, Lace Up, in 2012, which peaked at number four on the US Billboard 200 and contained his breakout single "Wild Boy" (featuring Waka Flocka Flame).

Zeahorse.bio: Zeahorse are a band based in Sydney, Australia. They originally formed in Lismore, NSW. The members are: Morgan Anthony - Guitar/Vox, Max Foskett - Baritone guitar, Ben Howell - Bass, Julien Crendal - Drums They released their self-titled debut EP in 2010.

THE NOVEMBERS.bio: THE NOVEMBERS are a shoegaze-band from Japan. In the summer of 2001, Yusuke Kobayashi and Takamatsu Hirofumi met and started a band together the following year. That band disbanded in 2005 and that's when THE NOVEMBERS were born. Kobayashi and Takamatsu recruited Yoshiki Ryousuke as their drummer in August of 2005, before its current lineup solidified in October 2005 with the arrival of Matsumoto Kengo on guitar. Kobayashi Yusuke (小林祐介) - Vocalist, Guitarist, Songwriter

Superbloom.bio: Alternative rock/grunge band from Brooklyn, New York.

Bilmuri.bio: Bilmuri is a American one man alternative/pop rock/post-hardcore project founded by former Attack Attack! member Johnny Franck in 2016. The name Bilmuri is a abbreviated parody of the great comedian and actor Bill Murray. Bilmuri debuted in 2016 with "Jaguar Shark", "Letters" and a self-titled album, followed up by "Frame" and "Banana" in 2017, along with "Taco" and "Solid Chub" in 2018. A year later in 2019, he would release "Pasteurized Milk", "Wet Milk" and "Rich Sips", which were all well received among his current fanbase.

InChaos.bio: Inchaos is a metalcore band from Hangzhou, China, formed in 2015. The band's sound blends heavy metalcore riffs and harsh screams with melodic clean vocals, creating a dynamic mix of aggression and melody. Their music incorporates both English and Mandarin lyrics, reflecting their dual-language approach. Their current lineup includes vocalists Tang Qian (clean vocals) and Lei Sheng (screaming vocals), guitarist Jackie, bassist Zhang Qing, and drummer Till Chan.

Who-ya Extended.bio: Who-ya Extended is a Jpop and alternative rock Japanese band, or as referred to on their website, "a creators' unit" which debuted in late 2019. The band is centered around vocalist Who-ya, the only named member of the band. The rest of the band's members as well as their identities remain unknown currently. Their debut single Q-vism released on the 27th of November 2019, alongside S-cape 2 the abs. The former single garnered the band a reputable amount of attention after its' feature in popular Japanese anime 'Psycho Pass 3'.
similar: TK from 凛として時雨

Nuclear Daisies.bio: Post-punk band from Austin (Texas) playing music bordering on dream pop / shoegaze, consisting of Alex Gehring (Ringo Deathstarr vocalist), Rob Glynn and Robert Williams.

Daron Malakian and Scars on Broadway.bio: Daron Malakian and Scars on Broadway (formerly just "Scars on Broadway") is an American rock band founded by System of a Down member Daron Malakian.

Life After Youth.bio: 1. Grunge-inspired emo band from Arlington, Texas. Active since 2017. 2. We were a five-piece hardcore band from Livermore, CA. Our shows were few and far between, but we managed to play a good bit of shows. We recorded a full-length, but it was never released due to lack of funds at the time. Continually working on our music and booking shows, all progress would be halted due to tragedy. In the early hours of October 31st, 2009, our guitarist Tyler Booth lost his life due to a freight train.

Spirit of the Beehive.bio: Philadelphia's restlessly creative Spirit of the Beehive write intricate, sometimes contradictory songs that unpredictably dart between several different styles, with lyrics that often express frustration, numbness, and self-doubt. Debuting with a shoegaze-influenced self-titled album in 2014, the band have constantly shifted both their lineup and their sound, with releases like 2018's Hypnic Jerks veering between dreamy psych-pop and catchy indie rock
similar: They Are Gutting a Body of Water

Polar.bio: There are at least ten artists named Polar: 1) Polar (aka Polar.) is a hardcore / metalcore band from Guildford, UK, which formed toward the end of 2009. Members include: Adam Woodford (vocals), Fabian Lomas (guitar), Max O'Neill (guitar), Rick Keenan (bass) and Nick Jones (drums). Facebook page: https://www.facebook.com/polaruk/ 2) Polar is the pseudonym of electronic music artist Kjetil Dale Sagstad (b. Meland, Norway - aka Jeff Dam and K). The project débuted with EP 37° C.

Persefone.bio: Persefone is the name of more than one artist: 1) Andorran death metal band 2) Greek metal band 3) Colombian metal band 4) noise artist 1) Death metal band from Andorra la Vella, Andorra. The band was formed in 2003 and their first album was "Truth Inside the Shades". The band consists of Carlos Lozano (guitar and vocals), Miguel Espinosa (keyboards and vocals), Jordi Gorgues (guitar) and Toni Mestre (bass). Bands like Opeth, Borknagar, Arcturus, Symphony X or Cacophony are all a reference for this album.

Imminence.bio: Imminence is a Swedish metalcore band formed in Trelleborg, Sweden, in 2009. The group consists of vocalist Eddie Berg, lead guitarist Harald Barrett, rhythm guitarist Alex Arnoldsson, bassist Christian Höijer and drummer Peter Hanström. They are currently signed to Arising Empire and have released five studio albums. Their most recent studio album, The Black, was released on 12 May 2024. The band was started in 2009 by Harald Barrett, with singer Eddie Berg joining shortly after.

The Album Leaf.bio: The Album Leaf officially began in 1998 as the solo project of Jimmy LaValle, guitarist for San Diego, California-based post-rock band Tristeza. LaValle has also performed in several other San Diego-area bands, including Swing Kids, The Locust, GoGoGo Airheart, and The Black Heart Procession. An Orchestrated Rise to Fall Jimmy LaValle had been playing an ocarina in the post-rock instrumental band Tristeza. Being a multi-instrumentalist, during downtime in the fall of 1998, he began playing drums for San Diego band GoGoGo Airheart.

Proletaryat.bio: Proletaryat is a Polish band founded in November 1987. They are famous in Poland thanks to their first two post-punk albums "Proletariat" & "Proletaryat" and for their performance at Jarocin Festival in 1989, where they managed to get 3 awards. Today Proletaryat are: * Tomasz "Oley" Olejnik - vocals * Dariusz "Kacper" Kacprzak - bass * Wiktor Daraszkiewicz - guitar * Robert "Mały" Hajduk - drums Discography: * Revolt (1989) * Proletariat (1991)

similar: Zankyou no Terror/残響のテロル

The Eden Project.bio: Jonathan Ng, going by the name of The Eden Project (Now EDEN), is a Electro, House and Melodic Dubstep producer, originating in Hong Kong SAR but now lives in Dublin, Ireland. The Eden Project so far only has one release on Monstercat as of yet; The fire, which is a collaboration between Eden and Puppet. Since then, The Eden Project has mentioned on social media that he would love to be part of the Monstercat family, however wants to remain with his current music label, NoCopyRightSounds for a while longer. https://www.iameden.eu/

Ok Goodnight.bio: Combining the talents of Casey Lee Williams (vocals), Martín de Lima (piano/guitar), Augusto Bussio (drums/guitar), and Peter de Reyna (bass), Ok Goodnight is a modern progressive metal band based out of Boston, Massachusetts. The group formed when they were all studying at Berklee College of Music. In early 2019, the band released their first single titled ‘’Rapture’’. On October 31st of the same year, they released their debut album ‘’Limbo’’, featuring bassist Ron Bernhaut and backing vocalist Elizabeth Hull.

Fractalize.bio: One-man band of Alexey Posohin, also known as Alex Djent, formed in 2016 in Chișinău, Moldova.

MISSIO.bio: MISSIO is an American electronic alternative duo formed in Austin, Texas in 2014. The project was founded by vocalist and producer Matthew Brue and later joined by producer and instrumentalist David Butler. Known for their genre-blending style and emotionally charged lyrics, MISSIO quickly gained attention for their unique fusion of electronic, pop, and rock influences. The name MISSIO—Latin for “mission”—reflects Brue's personal journey through addiction recovery and spiritual exploration.

Foxblood.bio: Foxblood is a five-part metal band from Melbourne, Australia. Making a large impact with their vocally strong debut album called, “The Devil, The Dark & The Rain” which is released with MGM Distribution with help from Adam Getgood (of Periphery) as well as Jonathan Gering (of The Devil Wears Prada). **The band line-up (as of 10/2017): Vocals: Chris Millward Guitar: Brett Powell/Steven Powell/Tom Beale Drums Aaron Beale Facebook: /foxbloodaus Instagram: @foxbloodau Twitter: @foxbloodau

Normandie.bio: "Normandie" may refer to more than one band: 1. An alternative rock band from Örebro, Sweden, known for their seamless fusion of post-hardcore energy, cinematic electronics, and emotionally charged melodies. Formed in late 2013, the band began as a six-piece outfit with Philip Strand and Johan Lindström sharing vocal duties, joined by guitarists Joel Felix and Håkan Almbladh, bassist Stefan Timmerholm, and drummer Jesper Malmberg. Initially rooted in metalcore and post-hardcore

Tokky Horror.bio: Tokky Horror was an electro-punk act consisting of Mollie Rush, Zee Davine and Ava Akira.

Balance Breach.bio: Balance Breach is a metalcore band from Mikkeli, Finland, formed in 2015. The band consists of Aleksi Paasonen on vocals, Terho Korhonen and Saku Heimonen on guitars, Joni Härkönen on bass, and Antti Halonen on drums. Initially, these musicians had formed the band under a different name with a different vocalist during their school years. However, with the recruitment of Paasonen in 2015, the group decided to relaunch the project under the name Balance Breach.

Johnny Cash.bio: John R. Cash (born J. R. Cash; February 26, 1932 – September 12, 2003) was an American singer-songwriter. His music often explored themes of sorrow, moral reflection, and redemption, particularly in his later work. He had a deep bass-baritone voice and was accompanied by the Tennessee Three, a backing band noted for its distinctive guitar rhythms. Cash was known for performing prison concerts and frequently wore an all-black stage wardrobe, which led to his nickname "Man in Black."

The Algorithm.bio: The Algorithm is Rémi Gallego, an electronic music composer from Toulouse, Midi-Pyrénées, France. Born October 7, 1989, he began releasing music under this name in 2009 following the dissolution of his former band Dying Breath.
