It's a fusion of several styles of electronic music with progressive metal, mathcore, and djent. For live performances, he uses an Akai APC40, a MIDI controller produced by the company Akai, co-developed with the German company Ableton, connected to a laptop running Ableton Live.

The Killers.bio: There are multiple artists using this name: 1. The Killers is an American rock band from Las Vegas, Nevada, formed in 2001. They got the name from the fictional band The Killers in the video for New Order's song Crystal. As of 2021, the band has released six studio albums, which have sold 28 million records worldwide. The Early Years Initially, The Killers played at large clubs in Las Vegas. With the band's line-up stable and their repertoire of songs expanding

Maybeshewill.bio: Maybeshewill is an instrumental band based in Leicestershire, UK where they were formed in 2005. They are James Collins (drums), Matt Daly (keyboards) , John Helps (guitar), Robin Southby (guitar) and Jamie Ward (bass). They’ve worked tirelessly over the last five years through endless touring and three full-length albums to build a loyal and enthusiastic following across the world. Sticking to steadfastly DIY ethics, the band have self recorded all their material to date and aspire to be as self-sufficient as possible.

Colourblind.bio: 1) Colourblind are a 4-piece alternative rock / emo band from Adelaide, South Australia, formed in 2017. A name that may already be familiar to those having explored Adelaide's rock-ier worlds in the past, with the group's debut EP - 2018's Move Like You - a melting pool of sorts for a range of genres and sounds, plucking influences from punk, hardcore, indie, shoegaze, alt-rock and beyond; the wide-ranging influences washed over with a distinctly DIY-esque shading that's become somewhat of a selling point for the group in their rapid rise.

Ulver.bio: Ulver (Norwegian for “wolves”) is a multi-disciplinary musical group founded in Oslo, Norway and currently based in London, England. The band was formed by Kristoffer "Garm" Rygg in 1993, and the current line-up includes Rygg (vocal, programming), Jørn H. Sværen (misc.), Tore Ylwizaker (programming, piano) and Daniel O'Sullivan (guitar, piano, various). Since their first black metal releases in 1993, Ulver’s style has ever been changing by combining elements of experimental

Porn.bio: 1.) PORN is a French industrial-rock band, founded in 1999. After releasing their first album, "Glitter, Danger & ToyBoyz", the band went on hiatus for several years, reforming in 2009. Since, they've released a compilation of their first ten years of music, "A Decade In Glitter And Danger", and a new album, "From The Void To The Infinite". 2.) Porn (The Men of), or Porn, is a San Francisco band founded by leader Tim Moss. Moss is joined by Dale Crover (Melvins), and Billy Anderson.

GWAR.bio: GWAR is a satirical thrash metal/punk band formed in 1985-1986 by a group of artists and musicians at Virginia Commonwealth University in Richmond, Virginia. The band is perhaps best known for their elaborate sci-fi/horror film inspired costumes, raunchy lyrics, and graphic stage performances, which can consist of scatology, sadomasochism, necrophilia, pedo-necrophilia, beastio-necrophilia, fire dancing, fake pagan rituals, mock executions/mutilations, and other controversial violent and political themes.

Free.bio: Free were an English rock band formed in London in 1968 by Paul Rodgers (vocals), Paul Kossoff (guitar), Andy Fraser (bass, piano), and Simon Kirke (drums, percussion). The band is best known for the songs "All Right Now" and "Wishing Well". Although initially more successful as a live act, their commercial breakthrough came with their third studio album, "Fire and Water" (1970), which included "All Right Now". This track contributed to their appearance at the 1970 Isle of Wight Festival

Viridian.bio: There are at least 4 artists using the name 'Viridian': I. Japanese indie rock band II. A Polish formation III. A producer by the name of Sean Keyt IV. A Swedish alternative artist V. Viridian is progressive metalcore band from US formed in 2019, in Jackson, New Jersey, United States. I. Viridian is a folk/indie-rock trio from Japan. Members: Sano Hitomi - Acoustic Guitar, Vocals Miyaji Kimura - Electric Guitar Kamiya Yuu - Drums Discography:

PIXEL GRIP.bio: Pixel Grip is a Chicago-based trio fusing industrial grit with pop sensibility to create a sound as confrontational as it is seductive. Their third album, Percepticide: the Death of Reality, is a dark, emotionally raw record that explores trauma, rage, and rebirth—born out of a near-breakup and creative reunion. Known for their magnetic live shows, Pixel Grip has toured across North America, lighting up main stages like Riot Fest and supporting acts like HEALTH.

Aqua Timez.bio: The band formed in 2000 after two of the original members met on the internet and discovered that they attended the same university. At their formation with all their current members in 2003, the band's name was Aqua Times, the correct spelling of their current name. No major releases came from the band during this period. The band renamed themselves to Aqua Timez in 2005. Their first release under this name was a mini-album which charted well on Oricon.

Sneaker Pimps.bio: Sneaker Pimps is an English downtempo/trip-hop band, initially active from 1995 to 2006 and working together again since 2019. As Line of Flight and Frisk, DJs and childhood friends Chris Corner from Middlesbrough and Liam Howe from Hartlepool released two EPs: "Soul of Indiscretion" and "World as A Cone" in the early 1990s. While not nearly as complex as the song-based material they would produce, these bedroom-recorded mixes of sampled beats and folk sounds would form the blueprint for the later sound.

Sharks in your Mouth.bio: From Post-hardcore to Djent , Sharks In Your Mouth (Andrea “Andy” Pali [Vocals], Daniele “Bobo” Monaldi [Guitar], Valerio “Vaela” Quirini [Lead Guitar], Diego “Dydo” Nardelli [Bass] and Enrico “Enry” Rivetti [Drums]), never stops to improve their sound and style. Starting from Promises (2016), the first album of the band, basically Metalcore with Post-hardcore influences, the boys begin their journey to find the ultimate sound, wich they create with the second full-length Sacrilegious, to be released in 2019.

Jutes.bio: Jutes is a Canadian singer, songwriter, and former basketball player known for his raw, emotional lyrics and genre-blending sound that mixes pop, alternative R&B, and hip-hop. Born Jordan Lutes in Toronto, Canada, he initially pursued a career in basketball at the collegiate level before leaving the sport to follow his true passion: music. He began releasing music independently around 2015, steadily building a loyal fanbase through heartfelt storytelling and lo-fi production.

LLLL.bio: In the mid-2000's, Kazuto Okawa's talent as a songwriter in the J-Pop industry was unparalleled, with some of Asia's most prominent artists such as Akiko Yano, YUKI, Kaela Kimura, Mika Nakajima, Genki Rockets, and more attached to their name. However, there was one significant event that changed everything - the 2011 Tohoku earthquake. This life-changing moment forever altered the course of Okawa's life, leading them on a path of using their talent for a greater purpose. In 2012, LLLL was born.

Whatever, Forever.bio: Melodic Punk/Emo band from Sydney, NSW. https://whateverforeverau.bandcamp.com/

The Last Martyr.bio: The Last Martyr is an experimental rock band based out of Greenville, SC. It includes Nick and Daniel..they have been around for three years and have produced two albums and one EP. They are currently enjoying the release of their latest CD, Blue Unicorn Convention, which is selling for $2, message for details. Myspace: www.myspace.com/lastmartyr In 2018 : The Last Martyr are Melbourne’s newest melodic metal sensation. The first single ‘Into the Black’ saw the band achieve national radio play on triple j and The Faction

Alura.bio: ALURA is hard rock/metal band from Las Vegas, NV blending the chaotic aggression of Slipknot, Ill Niño and Korn with the melody of Deftones and Thrice. Band Members: Taylor Adsit - vocals Drew Guerrero - guitar Ryan Avery - bass Parker Adsit - drums

Movements.bio: There are multiple artists with the name Movements: 1.) Movements are a post-hardcore band from Rancho Santa Margarita, California, formed in 2015. Reflecting personal changes from a whirlwind five years, Movements realize the full scope of their storytelling, musicianship, and vision on their second full-length album, No Good Left To Give [Fearless Records]. Not only does the music address the emotional push-and-pull of relationships, but it also explores loss

Powerman 5000.bio: Powerman 5000, sometimes abbreviated as PM5K, is an American rock band formed in Boston, Massachusetts, in 1991. The group has released eleven studio albums and achieved its greatest commercial success with the 1999 album "Tonight the Stars Revolt!", which reached number 29 on the Billboard 200. The album included the singles "When Worlds Collide", "Nobody's Real", and "Supernova Goes Pop". Vocalist Spider One, the younger brother of musician Rob Zombie, has been the band's only constant member since its formation.

Ill Niño.bio: Ill Niño is an American nu metal band formed in Union City, New Jersey, in 1998 by drummer Dave Chavarri, who is the band's sole original member. The band has released seven studio albums, two EPs, and one compilation album, with total worldwide album sales exceeding 1.3 million. Ill Niño was initially formed as El Niño in May 1998 by former Pro-Pain drummer Dave Chavarri. He recruited Merauder vocalist Jorge Rosado, guitarist Daniel Gomez, and bassist Christian Machado, with Chavarri taking on the role of drummer and percussionist.

Scalping.bio: Based in Bristol, UK, SCALER (fka SCALPING) are a four-piece band: Isaac Jones on drums, James Rushforth on bass guitar, Nick Berthoud on guitar and Alex Hill on electronics. For the last five years, SCALPING have been writing, recording and performing music together, trying to answer the question that guides their work; “How can a band bridge the gap between live guitar music and live electronic music?” The new single “LOAM” marks a new chapter for SCALER.

Tiësto.bio: Tijs Michiel Verwest, known professionally as Tiësto, is a Dutch DJ and music producer from Breda. He was voted "the Greatest DJ of All Time" by Mix magazine in a 2010/2011 poll amongst fans. In 2013, he was voted by DJ Mag readers as the "best DJ of the last 20 years". He is also regarded as the "Godfather of EDM" by many sources. In 1997, he founded the label Black Hole Recordings with Arny Bink, where he released the Magik and In Search of Sunrise CD series.

Black Label Society.bio: Black Label Society is a heavy metal band formed by former Ozzy Osbourne/Pride & Glory guitar player Zakk Wylde in Los Angeles, California in 1998. They have released ten albums to date, eight of which are studio albums. The band, aside from Wylde consists of bassist John "JD" Deservio, drummer Jeff Fabb, and rhythm guitarist Dario Lorina. The band is not to be confused with Zakk Wylde's solo project (sometimes known as Book of Shadows after its only album) or with Pride & Glory, Wylde's southern rock band.

Daniel Avery.bio: Daniel Avery is an electronic music producer and DJ from Bournemouth, a resort town on the south coast of England. Avery's music encompasses abstract techno, house, IDM, and shoegaze. Initially going by the name "Stopmakingme", he began releasing well-received indie dance tracks and remixes starting in 2009, but broke through after he switched to his own name in 2012. His expansive full-length debut, Drone Logic, was released to wide acclaim in 2013.

Julie.bio: There are multiple artists by the name of Julie: 1. julie is an American shoegaze band from Los Angeles, signed with Atlantic Records. julie, originating from Orange County, California, began in 2020, independently releasing several singles that garnered early support for the band, including “flutter,” one of the band's most successful tracks to date. A year after their single run, the group released their debut EP titled pushing daisies. In 2022, julie released a joint (non-album) single titled pg.

bôa.bio: bôa is a British alternative rock band formed in London in 1993 by drummer Ed Herten, keyboard player Paul Turrell, and guitarist/vocalist Steve Rodgers. Bôa's current lineup consists of Jasmine Rodgers (vocals), Steve Rodgers (vocals & guitar), Alex Caird (bass), and Lee Sullivan (drums & piano). The band progressed from a funk band to a rock band over the years. They produced two major albums, Twilight in 2001 and Get There in 2005. The band was

Oliver Heldens.bio: Oliver Heldens (born Olivier J. L. Heldens; 1 February 1995) is a Dutch electronic music producer and DJ from Rotterdam in the Netherlands. His 2013 song, "Gecko", caught the attention of fellow Dutch DJ Tiësto, who signed him to his label, Musical Freedom, and released the track with vocals from British singer Becky Hill on June 23, 2014. Heldens also has a weekly podcast titled Heldeep Radio, which has been ongoing for over 2 years. In 2015, he began producing bass house songs under the alias HI-LO

Nitroverts.bio: Metal duo Nitroverts hit the ground running with their red-hot new single, "Starting Point." This aptly titled track is the beginning of a series of singles from the band, fusing modern metal and electronics.

Hocico.bio: Hocico is a Mexican dark electro band, commonly placed in the aggrotech subgenre. The band was officially formed in Mexico City, Mexico in 1993 by Erk Aicrag (lyrics & vocals) and Racso Agroyam (programming), but both cousins had been making electronic music since they were fifteen years old. The names are anagrams of the real names Erik Garcia and Oscar Mayorga. Their music can be described as dark and aggressive electronica or more specifically as aggrotech. The lyrics are commonly in English, however, they are sometimes in Spanish.

Rising Insane.bio: Rising Insane is a metalcore band hailing from Ganderkesee, near Bremen, Germany. The current lineup of Rising Insane includes Aaron Steineker on vocals, Florian Köchy on vocals and guitar, Sven Polizuk on guitar, Ulf Hedenkamp on bass, and Robert Kühling on drums. Helge Dannebaum was a former guitarist who was part of the band until 2013. The band was formed in 2012, and before their debut album's release, Rising Insane established themselves in the German metal scene by founding the Springsane Festival

Gunship.bio: GUNSHIP is a neon soaked, late night, sonic getaway drive, dripping with luscious analog synthesizers, cinematic vocals and cyberpunk values, exploding from the front cover of a dusty plastic VHS case which has lain forgotten since 1984. Members: Dan Haigh (Synths) Alex Westaway (Vocals) Alex Gingell (Drums)
similar: W O L F C L U B

Head Cloud.bio: Head Cloud are a four-piece grunge-gaze band from Grantham, East Midlands, United Kingdom. Formed in 2021, the band's sound blends influences from 90s grunge, shoegaze, and early post-hardcore, creating a unique sound that will appeal to fans of Superheaven, Hum, Soul Blind, Split Chain, Soundgarden, and similar acts.

Crosses.bio: There is more than one artist with this name: 1.) A hardcore/southern rock/thrash band formed in London, UK, born and split in the late '00s. www.myspace.com/crossesuk 2.) Hardcore punk band from Montclair, NJ http://crossesnj.bandcamp.com/ -------- For the musical side project of Deftones singer Chino Moreno, Far guitarist Shaun Lopez and Scott Chuck, please refer to †††

Avenged Sevenfold.bio: Avenged Sevenfold (often abbreviated to A7X) is a heavy metal/hard rock band which formed in 1999 in Huntington Beach, California, United States. The band consists of vocalist M. Shadows, lead guitarist Synyster Gates, rhythm guitarist Zacky Vengeance, bassist Johnny Christ, and former Bad Religion and Suicidal Tendencies drummer Brooks Wackerman (2016-present). Heartbreakingly, the band's best friend and original drummer James "The Rev" Sullivan passed away in 2009.

Bunny.bio: Bunny refers to a number of bands and artists. 1) a Belgian band led by Nico ‘Bunny’ Renson. Bunny was born in a small town squeezed between the Dutch and German border in a small country called Belgium. Growing up in a loving family, in a farm overlooking an orchard it was clear he would not end up writing hip hop tunes about street crime. At a young age he started writing about what he did know, that included observing the smaller things in life.

Love Is Noise.bio: We welcome you to the world of Love is Noise, your newest form of escapism. Residing from the UK, Love is Noise creates true, passionate energy through a modern audiovisual experience influenced by the best of what came before. Created to breathe diversity into the worldwide musical space, the duo strive to build a new way of being, a way to finally belong. You will find a way.

Ocean Sleeper.bio: Ocean Sleeper have wasted no time in their relatively short spanning career. Since their humble founding in regional Victoria, they’ve managed to sell out their own headlining shows, tour alongside some of heavy music’s finest, record an album with renowned producer Kris Crummett (Sleeping With Sirens, Dance Gavin Dance, Issues) and have managed to score festival bills on Unify 2017 + 2019. Since their inception in 2016 Ocean Sleeper have hastily made their mark on the Australian Heavy Music landscape.

Day Aches.bio: Day Aches is an American Grunge band formed in 2019 by Drew Lambert, Kyle Benson, Brady McGraw, and Mauricio Romero. The Fredericksburg, VA group released their debut EP, One Last Dream Before Dying, on September 19th, 2023 via Really Rad Records.

Hacktivist.bio: Nu Metal/Rap Metal/Djent From: Milton Keynes, The United Kingdom Founded: 2011 To progress, risk comes before reward, and convention must give way to invention. In a scene saturated by sound-alike bands, the time has come to evolve or be left behind. Featuring ex-members of Heart of a Coward, hard-hitting, raw energy is assured. However, Hacktivist is no mere re-tread of what has come before. Hardwiring huge, devastating grooves with intelligent and insightful rhyme, Hacktivist are not afraid to split opinion.

Plastic Dagger.bio: Indie band composed of FOREVER CLUB's musicians ITSOKTOCRY and Lil Rozey.

Hyro The Hero.bio: In any cultural movement there are leaders and there are followers. But most importantly, there are those uniquely innovative provocateurs that take the familiar, turn it upside down, and burn it with new creative fire. Like a b-boy mad scientist smashing the windows of the mainstream with a Molotov cocktail of passion and inspiration, Hyro The Hero takes the fusion of rap and rock and resurrects it. His combustible concoction is one part The Clash, one part Bad Brains, and several doses of reverence for hip-hop relevance.

Sokół.bio: Sokół (real name Wojciech Sosnowski) (born as Wojciech Sokół on March 11, 1977 in Warsaw, Poland ) is a Polish rapper, as well as entrepreneur and radio journalist. Member of the ZPAV Phonographic Academy. Wojciech Sosnowski is known primarily as being member and a co-founder of Warsaw's rap group WWO. He also co-creates the ZIP Skład collective and the TPWC team. In 2011, Sokół was ranked 2nd on the list of 30 best Polish rappers according to Machina magazine.

CHON.bio: Chon (sometimes stylized as CHON) are an American math rock band from San Diego, California. Their music is largely instrumental-based with only a few songs containing vocal performances. The band consists of Mario Camarena (guitar), Erick Hansel (guitar), Esiah Camarena (bass) and Nathan Camarena (drums). On November 8, 2015, Chon stated via their Twitter feed that they parted ways with Drew Pelisek due to 'artistic differences'. It was also confirmed by Drew on a personal account.

Kings of Leon.bio: Kings of Leon is an American rock band formed in Nashville, Tennessee, in 1999. The band is composed of brothers Caleb, Nathan and Jared Followill, and their cousin Matthew Followill. The band's early music was a blend of Southern rock and garage rock with blues influences, but it has evolved throughout the years to include a variety of genres and a more alternative, arena rock sound. Kings of Leon achieved initial success in the United Kingdom with nine Top 40 singles

Foals.bio: Foals are a rock band which formed in Oxford, England in 2005. The band consists of Yannis Philippakis (vocals, guitar), Jimmy Smith (guitar), Walter Gervers (bass) and Jack Bevan (drums). The band has released seven albums: "Antidotes" (2008), "Total Life Forever" (2010), "Holy Fire" (2013), "What Went Down" (2015), "Everything Not Saved Will Be Lost, Pt. 1" (2019) , "Everything Not Saved Will Be Lost, Pt. 2" (2019), and "Life is Yours" (2022). In January 2018, it was announced that Walter Gervers (bass) was leaving the band.

Dope Stars Inc..bio: Cyberpunk Rock. Victor Love: Producer, Vocals, Guitar, Synthesizers, Drum Machine La Nuit: Guitar Darin Yevonde: Bass Ash Rexy: Keyboards With lyrics inspired by Cyberpunk imagery and the Cyberpunk subculture, Dope Stars Inc. have been mixing industrial beats, acid synths and distorted sounds with a bratty rock’n’roll sound and attitude since their foundation in May 2003, Italy. With their debut self-produced EP “10,000 Watts Of Artificial

Laaz Rockit.bio: Lääz Rockit is an American thrash metal band. Formed in 1982 in Berkeley, California, Lääz Rockit is considered to be one of the founders of the genre of thrash metal unique to the Bay Area music scene. The band's new style of metal helped start a huge fan base, with their high energy live performances becoming their signature trademark. Sold out performances in the burgeoning club scene soon gave Lääz Rockit label attention, and the Indie label Target Entertainment inked the band for their debut album in 1984, "City's Gonna Burn".

Aerosmith.bio: Aerosmith is an American rock band formed in Boston, Massachusetts, in 1970. The group consists of lead vocalist Steven Tyler, bassist Tom Hamilton, drummer Joey Kramer, and guitarists Joe Perry and Brad Whitford. Their musical style is primarily rooted in blues-based hard rock and also incorporates elements of pop rock, heavy metal, glam metal, and rhythm and blues. The band’s primary songwriting partnership between Tyler and Perry is often referred to as the Toxic Twins.

CFCF.bio: CFCF is the stage name of Canadian electronic musician Michael Silver. Based in Montreal, Silver took the name CFCF from the call sign of the city's CFCF-TV. CFCF's debut LP, Continent, was an impressive collage of vintage electronic textures evoking grandiose landscapes worthy of a Michael Mann film. The River, an EP based partly on Werner Herzog's classic film Fitzcarraldo, deepened a growing obsession with conjuring far-away landscapes and voyages through a style which is at times melancholy, propulsive and ecstatic.

People In The Box.bio: People in the Box is a Japanese alternative rock band formed in 2003 in Kitakyushu, Fukuoka, Japan. Its original lineup consisted of vocalist and guitarist 波多野裕文 (Hirofumi Hatano), bassist 中山敦史 (Atsushi Nakayama), and drummer 上田五郎 (Goro Ueda). Nakayama left the band after a concert on May 29, 2008 at 下北沢SHELTER and was replaced by 福井健太 (Kenta Fukui). Their original drummer has also been replaced by 山口大吾 (Daigo Yamaguchi).

Marmozets.bio: Marmozets is an English rock band from Bingley, West Yorkshire. Formed in 2007,[4] the band consists of Rebecca 'Becca' Macintyre (vocals), Jack Bottomley (guitar), Sam Macintyre (guitar/vocals) Will Bottomley (bass/vocals) and Josh Macintyre (drums). They released their first EP, Passive Aggressive in 2011 and followed that with Vexted in 2012. Marmozets signed to Roadrunner Records in October 2013 and released the singles Born Young and Free and Move, Shake, Hide that same year.

Sylosis.bio: Sylosis is a British metal band, from Reading, Berkshire formed in 2000. They have used various genres in their music over the years, previously basing their sound on 1980s thrash metal crossed with modern styles of music like melodic death metal and metalcore, blending the genres seamlessly into one coherent and unique sound. With their 2023 LP, "A Sign of Things to Come," and the singles preceding it (Worship Decay; Immovable Stone; Heavy is the Crown)

LEAD HORIZON.bio: Lead Horizon (formerly The Lead Horizon) is a Russian nu-metal band from Novosibirsk, Russia. The idea of ​​creating an all-female rock band came to Irina Tararova and Anna Epachintseva in 2014, and the group was practically formed in the first half of 2015. First line-up (2015-2019): – Irina Tararova – guitar, – Anna Epachintseva – bass, – Ekaterina Karpova – drums, – Polina Delenda – guitar, – Lina Sovetova – vocals. At the time of the band's creation, none of its members had yet reached the age of eighteen.

SUNDAZER.bio: SUNDAZER is a four-piece alternative metal band from Moscow, Russia. They fuse elements of a bygone MTV culture into a mix of shoegaze, grunge, and metal in its sound.

Lane 8.bio: San Francisco-based house producer Lane 8 (real name: Daniel Goldstein) made quite a splash when The Magician played his debut single, “Don’t Want You Back”, in the 23rd installment of the wildly popular Magic Tape series. The follow-up single, titled ‘Dusty’, surpassed all expectations, racking up diverse DJ support from the UK’s Jody Wisternoff and Kidnap Kid to California’s Viceroy and Geisha Twins. From there, a 3-track EP, reworks of Fleetwood Mac and Brandy

k?d.bio: Patrick Cybulski, (born 1997) known by his stage name k?d (pronounced "kid") is an American electronic musician and DJ. He is known for his single "Lose Myself" (featuring Phil Good), which charted on the US Billboard Dance/Mix Show Airplay at #40, as well as his single "Distance" (featuring Blair), which peaked at #16 on the aforementioned chart. He is also known for his remixes of popular electronic songs, most significantly "Doin' It Right" by Daft Punk and "Renaissance" (featuring Clairity) by Steve James.

Haku..bio: Haku (ハク。) is a four-member band based in Osaka, Japan. The band was formed in 2019 and is signed under the label SPACE SHOWER MUSIC. The members include Ai (あい, vocals, guitar), Nazuna (なずな, guitar), Kano (カノ, bass, chorus), and Mayu (まゆ, drums). The name "Haku" (白) means "white" in Japanese, symbolizing their desire to remain free and adaptable in their music without being confined to one particular genre. The "。" at the end of their name was added to represent their origins in a music circle during high school.

Vana.bio: Yvonne Winckel aka Vana (she/they) An independent artist from Auckland, New Zealand "Vana is the red haired pop incendiary whose music is fuelled by dark femininity and villainous themes such as lust, jealousy love and revenge. Soft and feminine vocals paired with hard hitting gritty bass and screaming electric guitar gives Vana her lean yet unmistakeable sound that leaves listeners craving more. With notable platforms on Tiktok, Twitch and Instagram

American Nightmare.bio: American Nightmare (briefly known as Give Up the Ghost) is a hardcore punk band from Boston, Massachusetts, formed in 1999 by vocalist Wes Eisold. They have released three albums, one EP and a compilation of earlier released material. After seven years of disbandment, the band reunited under their original name and performed two reunion shows in December 2011. The band performed in Revere, Massachusetts on December 29, and in Los Angeles on December 31.

Phoxjaw.bio: Bristol based noise makers and brain shakers. Born from a cabin-fevered recording experience at Devil’s Bridge in Wales, Royal Swan album harks back to a fantastical medieval England. Without being overtly political, Royal Swan also details the tension inherent in modern day Great Britain – the sense of isolation and resentment the United Kingdom has fostered versus trying to retain a sense of pride in one’s own country. Listen out especially for the title-track and opening track for this.

Roadrunner United.bio: Roadrunner United was a project organised by the alternative record label Roadrunner Records to celebrate its 25th anniversary. It culminated in the release of the album Roadrunner United: The All Star Sessions on October 11, 2005 (October 10 in the UK) Four "team captains" were chosen: Joey Jordison (Slipknot, Murderdolls) Matt Heafy (Trivium) Dino Cazares (Asesino, Fear Factory, ex-Brujeria, Divine Heresy) Robb Flynn (Machine Head, ex-Vio-lence)

Elephant Gym.bio: 大象體操 (Elephant Gym) is a fusion trio from Kaohsiung, Taiwan that play an intense bass-lead style of music that elegantly mixes math rock and jazz, with elements of post-rock and world fusion influencing their experimental sound. Since 2012 the band has released two EPs (平衡 Balance in 2012 and 工作 Work in 2016) and four full-length albums (角度 Angle in 2014, 水底 Underwater in 2018, 夢境 Dreams in 2022, and 世界 World in 2023).
similar: How to count one to ten

Mad Kelly.bio: Mad Kelly is a Florida-based artist and producer. A multi-instrumentalist, Mad Kelly writes and produces all of his own music. His influences span from 1990's punk rock to scream rap, all of which can be heard in the way he bends genres in his own music.

Limbs.bio: There are multiple artists with this name: 1. LIMBS (often shown in all-caps) is a post-hardcore outfit hailing from Tampa Bay, Florida. Originally formed in 2013 under a different moniker, LIMBS released their debut album entitled ‘Dead Ends’ before undergoing a fairly drastic lineup change. After taking eight months to reform, LIMBS released their sophomore release ‘Admission,’ in late 2014. 2. Grindcore/Noisecore/Experimental band from Manila, Philippines.

Jambinai.bio: Jambinai (Hangul: 잠비나이) are a Korean post-rock fusion gugak band formed in Seoul in 2009. Their debut album Différance won the award for Best Crossover Album at the 2013 Korean Music Awards. Their sophomore album Hermitage was released in 2016 on Bella Union. Jambinai sound less like a band than a force of nature, fusing the full dramatic range of post-rock dynamics to Korean folk roots to create an exhilarating, vivid and unique fusion.

Klone.bio: There multiple artists with this name: 1. After eight years of existence, Klone has reached a personal and studied musical style. Today, the band releases a new inspired album, with various influences: Metal, Heavy Rock, Stoner, Grunge, Pop, Psychedelic. Klone finds its identity in going deeper in the command of each musical style. "All Seeing Eye" uses well-orchestrated arrangements, uncommon in Metal, like the use harpe, the saxophone, the chinese flute and various electro tones.

Moon Tooth.bio: The new project of long-time collaborators Nick Lee & Ray Marte. MOON TOOTH formed from the dissolve of their previous band, Exemption. The two quickly found kinship with John Carbone (Rice Cultivation Society) and Vincent Romanelli (Give Up The Goods) while embarking on their new musical journey.

The Disaster Area.bio: THE DISASTER AREA are mixing metalcore and alternative rock with a fresh and open-minded approach. They stand out from the scene with creativity, authenticity and first of all emotionality. The band first appeared under the name THE DISASTER AREA in 2012 and their musical goal that was outlined with the debut ‘Sell Your Soul’ (Deafground Records) in 2016. In 2018 the band made some of the biggest steps in their career by signing to REDFIELD RECORDS and releasing their acclaimed second album “Alpha//Omega” in November of the same year.

Trophy Eyes.bio: Trophy Eyes is an Australian rock band currently signed under Hopeless Records, who have gained popularity from their blend of modern rock and pop with their hardcore roots. The band consists of 4 members, vocalist John Floreani, bassist and backing vocalist Jeremy Winchester, guitarist Josh Campiao and drummer Blake Caruso. Their former drummer, Callum Cramp, departed from the band on November 8, 2017. Trophy Eyes have released four full length albums and one EP.

Fear, and Loathing in Las Vegas.bio: Fear, and Loathing in Las Vegas is a electronicore band formed in the summer of 2008 in Kobe, Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan. The band was formed by members from Ending for a Start and Blank Time. The band mainly plays heavy music with both high and low shouts, but unlike typical metal sounds, they have electronic beats and autotuned singing. The band spent much time searching for a clean vocalist, So (ex. Bombreligion) joined in the spring of 2009 as their 6th member.

Scale the Summit.bio: Scale the Summit is an American instrumental progressive metal band based out of Houston, Texas. It formed in 2004 and signed to Prosthetic Records. The band is influenced by other progressive acts such as Cynic and Dream Theater. The band gained notice as part of the Progressive Nation 2009 tour with Dream Theater, Zappa Plays Zappa and Bigelf. Chris Letchford and Travis Levrier grew up in the same neighborhood, but never played music together until 2004

Vitalic.bio: Vitalic (born as Pascal Arbez in 1976) is an electronic music artist. He was born in France and is of Italian descent. His first singles were released in 1996 and 1997, but were confined to the underground electronic music scene. He decided to make music when he heard Daft Punk's Rollin' & Scratchin' in a club in his hometown Dijon, France. He became good friends with Michel Amato, also known as The Hacker, whom he met in the Rex, the "techno temple" of Laurent Garnier.

Ylvis.bio: Ylvis is a variety show group from Bergen, Norway, consisting of the two brothers Bård Ylvisåker (born 1982) and Vegard Ylvisåker (born 1979). The brothers originated from Sogn, but grew up in Bergen and Africa. The brothers debuted professionally on 19 October 2000 at the Ole Bull Theater in Bergen with the show "Ylvis – en kabaret" ("Ylvis – a cabaret"). In connection with the show was the song "Rumor says" recorded, pressed as a CD-single, and sold during the show, but was never put out for regular sales.

Tenacious D.bio: Tenacious D (often referred to as "The D" and sometimes introduced as "The Greatest Band in the world. Period.") is an American satirical rock duo formed by musicians and actors Kyle Gass and Jack Black in Los Angeles, California in 1994. Their music fuses vulgar absurdist comedy with rock music in a style that critics have described as "mock rock". The band first gained reputation in 1999, starring in a self-titled HBO TV series. The drumming on
similar: The Presidents of the United States of America

Sonata Arctica.bio: Sonata Arctica is a Finnish power metal band formed in Kemi. The group originally began as a hard rock band under the name “Tricky Beans”, later changing to “Tricky Means”, before adopting the name Sonata Arctica when they shifted toward a power metal style. The band’s lineup has changed over time, but key members have included vocalist Tony Kakko, drummer Tommy Portimo, keyboardist Henrik Klingenberg, guitarist Elias Viljanen, and bassist Pasi Kauppinen.

Van Halen.bio: Van Halen was an American rock band formed in Pasadena, California, in 1972. The band was recognized for revitalizing hard rock and for their energetic live performances and the guitar work of Eddie Van Halen. From 1974 to 1985, the lineup consisted of Eddie Van Halen on guitar, keyboards, and backing vocals, Alex Van Halen on drums, David Lee Roth on lead vocals, and Michael Anthony on bass. Their self-titled debut album, released in February 1978, reached No.

Flying Lotus.bio: Regarded upon his 2006 arrival as a promising beatmaker looming in the shadows of J Dilla, Madlib, and Dabrye, Steven Ellison -- known as Flying Lotus -- nullified all comparisons only two years later with Los Angeles. On that album, his first of five for the Warp label, Ellison distilled a uniquely crisp, spasmodic, and heady sound with elements of hip-hop, avant-garde jazz, IDM, and dubstep. While the abovementioned producers fueled Ellison, he

Destroy Rebuild Until God Shows.bio: Destroy Rebuild Until God Shows (commonly called D.R.U.G.S.) is an American post-hardcore supergroup, formed in 2010. The band's current line-up is Craig Owens (lead vocals), Jona Weinhofen (guitars), and Aaron Patrick (bass). They released their debut self-titled album on February 22, 2011. The band broke up in 2012, after vocalist Craig Owen rejoined Chiodos. In 2020, Owens revealed that D.R.U.G.S. would be returning, and in February 2021, the band announced they have signed to Velocity Records.

Rabea Massaad.bio: Rabea Massaad is an English guitar player, probably most famous for his YouTube-channel. Besides his solo work, he is the lead guitarist in FrogLeap, Toska and Dorje. He has guest appeared with Leo Moracchioli and Hannah Boulton at Frog Leap Studios for covers of Africa (Metal Cover), In My Place (Metal Version) and Dance Monkey (Metal Version). He also played on covers of Californication, Get Lucky and Back In Black. Links:YouTubeMerchSoundcloudFacebookTwitterInstagram

Space of Variations.bio: Space Of Variations - ukrainian chaotic metalcore/ post hardcore band which was formed in 2009. Current line-up: Dima Kozhuhar - vocalsAlexey Zatserkovnyi - vocals/guitarAnton Kasatkin - bassTima Kasatkin - drums Discography:*2009 - Осталось Лишь Придумать Зачем Я Это Сделал (EP)*2010 - Закрытый Космос (EP)2016 - Blackmail (EP)2018 - Mind Darknet2019 - XXXXX *released under name Пространство Вариантов

Nothing.bio: 1) Nothing is an American shoegaze band from Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, United States formed in 2011 by Domenic Palermo. Previously, Palermo was the brains behind the early 2000's hardcore punk act Horror Show, which was put on hold in 2002 after Palermo was incarcerated for a stabbing that eventually led to a 2 year prison sentence. Upon his return, Palermo would take a lengthy hiatus from music. In 2011, Palermo released a demo tape entitled Poshlost / пошлость under the moniker Nothing.

Have a Nice Life.bio: Have a Nice Life is an American post-punk / shoegaze duo from Middletown, Connecticut, formed in 2000 by Dan Barrett and Tim Macuga. The project is known for blending gothic rock, industrial, ambient, and drone elements into an atmospheric and emotionally intense sound. Barrett is also known for his solo project Giles Corey and the now-defunct band In Pieces, while Macuga has been involved with black metal project Nahvalr and Western Massachusetts hardcore band The Danger Strangers.
similar: LSD and the Search for God

My Dead Girlfriend.bio: My Dead Girlfriend (死んだ僕の彼女) is a Japanese alternative rock band from Tokyo, Japan. The band was formed in July of 2005 by Guitarist Yuki Ishikawa. The present line up of band members includes Yuki Ishikawa on guitar and vocals, Megumi Ideta on vocals and keyboards, Akihiro Kinoshita on guitar, Takashi Shimano on drums, Mai Takahashi on bass guitar and vocals, and Yuki Otsuka on guitar. links: My Dead Girlfriend's myspace official band website
similar: Seventeen Years Old and Berlin Wall

3nd.bio: 3nd is an instrumental post-rock band from Tokyo, Japan. The band was started by Hayamizu at the end of 2001, recruiting members that already were in respective bands. 3nd (三度; sando) is Japanese for "three times". Hayamizu Naoyuki : Guitar Anzai Tetsuya : Guitar (pocketlife) Yamamoto Masashi : Bass (NATSUMEN) Endou Atsushi : Drums (pocketlife)
similar: How to count one to ten

Soul Blind.bio: Soul Blind is a four-piece alternative metal & post-hardcore band from The Hudson Valley, New York, formed in 2018. They come from the same hardcore scene that birthed bands like Mindforce and All Out War, but Soul Blind don’t make hardcore. Instead, their sound is a dreamy fuzz-pedal trudge that exists somewhere on the continuum between grunge and shoegaze. Soul Blind have been self-releasing music since 2018, and they’ve claimed bands like Hum, Failure, Quicksand and Deftones as influences.

Point North.bio: Point North are an American band based in Los Angeles, California consisting of Jon Lundin (vocals), Andy Hershey (guitar) and Sage Weeber (drums). The band is signed to Hopeless records and released their debut album Brand New Vision in August of 2020, featuring popular song Into The Dark. To date, the band have only headlined once on a sold North American tour in April 2022. Point North dropped their sophomore record Prepare For Despair in August of 2023.

Taken By Tides.bio: Taken By Tides is a Texas-based band consisting of 4 friends, each seasoned veterans of their respective scenes, who came together during the pandemic to break the mold and redefine the world of active rock and metalcore. Consisting of vocalist Omar Martinez, guitarist Memo Rodriguez, bassist Jack Strunk, and drummer Jayden Mermella, Taken By Tides is the result of years of perseverance and dedication, and, in its new revitalized incarnation, a vehicle for cathartic transformation of hardships into success.

The Amity Affliction.bio: The Amity Affliction is a post-hardcore band originally from Gympie, Queensland, Australia. It seemingly took The Amity Affliction years to get their music together, releasing a demo in 2003 and a self-titled EP in 2004, but from the moment they released their High Hopes EP in 2007, the touring rampage began. In no time, The Amity Affliction was a household name amongst the hardcore community of Australia. Their mixture of metallic riffage, rock swagger and sing/scream vocal attacks made its mark on all who got in their way.

The Beatles.bio: The Beatles were an English rock band formed in Liverpool in 1960. The group’s core lineup consisted of John Lennon, Paul McCartney, George Harrison and Ringo Starr. They are widely regarded as one of the most influential acts in popular music and played a central role in the development of 1960s counterculture. The Beatles’ music incorporated elements of skiffle, beat, 1950s rock and roll, classical music and traditional pop, and they explored styles including folk, Indian music, psychedelia and hard rock.

Sofi Tukker.bio: Sofi Tukker is an electronic music duo, formed in 2014, consisting of Sophie Hawley-Weld and Tucker Halpern, who live in New York. Sophie is originally German but considers herself a Brazilian at heart, because of her passion for Latin and African rythyms. Tucker was a basketball player before an injury ended his career as an athlete and brought him to music. Discography: Albums: 2018: Treehouse EPs: 2016: Soft Animals 2019: Dancing on the People

Behemoth.bio: There are multiple artists with the name Behemoth: 1. Behemoth is a Polish blackened death metal band from Gdańsk. Formed in 1991, the band is often credited with helping to shape the Polish extreme metal scene, alongside acts such as Vader, Decapitated, Vesania, and Hate. Initially rooted in traditional black metal, Behemoth later transitioned toward a blackened death metal style. They have released eleven full-length studio albums. The band's

Hide.bio: Hide is the name of more than one artist: 1) Japanese rock artist, first known as a member of X Japan. 2) Chicago-based industrial noise duo featuring Heather Gabel and Seth Sher. 3) Drum & Bass artist, also known as Computer Genius. 1) hide松本秀人(born on December 13,1964 - died on May 2,1998) was the self-titled 【hide】solo project of X JAPAN's lead guitarist. He released his first album, HIDE YOUR FACE (on which he arranged and played most of the instruments), in 1994 to huge success.

similar: Devil May Cry 5 OST | Casey Edwards feat. Ali Edwards

Witchery.bio: Witchery is a Swedish thrash metal band, formed by former members of Satanic Slaughter in 1997. To date, the band has released five full length studio albums and an EP. It began in 1997 when their cult band Satanic Slaughter splintered, Witchery started as guitarists Patrik Jensen (The Haunted) and Rille Rimfält, vocalist Toxine, drummer Mique, and bassist Sharlee D'Angelo (Arch Enemy, Mercyful Fate). In 1998 they recorded and released their debut, Restless And Dead by Necropolis Records.

Rob Dougan.bio: Rob Dougan, who started as Rob D, is a genre-blending music composer. Mixing the sound of orchestral film music, the beat of club Trip Hop, and bluesy vocals, he is only tangentially relatable to electronic music. He was known primarily for his 1995 breakthrough single "Clubbed To Death (Kurayamino Variation)", popularized by 1999's The Matrix soundtrack. It wasn't until 2002 that he released his debut album Furious Angels in the UK. He was born in 1969 in Sydney, Australia and moved to London, UK ca. 1990.

Tiffany Day.bio: Tiffany Day Ruan (born January 30, 2000), is a Canadian-American singer-songwriter. Born in Toronto, raised in Kansas, and now based in Los Angeles, Tiffany Day started creating music at a young age. Classically trained in both piano and violin, she takes inspiration from her basic aural skills to produce captivating progressions and original melodies. Her current sound reflects different aspects of indie, bedroom, and electronic influenced pop, making her music a unique mix between sub-genres.

Spiderbait.bio: Spiderbait is an alternative rock band formed in 1991 in Finley, New South Wales, Australia which consists of Mark "Kram" Maher (drums, lead vocals), Janet English (bass, vocals) and Damien Whitty (guitar). They have had two top ten albums and another three albums reach the Australian top 40. The band moved to Melbourne, Australia where Kram was studying at the Victorian College of the Arts School of Music. The band played its first gig at a loungeroom for a friend's party.

Exist Immortal.bio: facebook | bigcartel | twitter | bandcamp Formed In: March 2011 Origins: London, UK Genre: Progressive Metal | Math Metal | Djent Record Label: Revolution Harmony Contact Info | Bookings lulu@incendia-management.co.uk adam@possessivemanagement.com Band Lineup Meyrick de la Fuente | Vocals Tom Montgomery | Guitar/Vocals Kurt Valencia | Guitar David Billote | Bass Fergus Gardiner | Drums BIO In a short space of time, West-London based EXIST IMMORTAL have become one of the UK's rising stars in the tech metal scene

Samurai Pizza Cats.bio: There are multiple artists with the name Samurai Pizza Cats 1) This Ska/Party band originated in the town of Oneonta, located in the Central New York region. The Samurai Pizza Cats began practicing as a 7-piece band in a cramped 2 bedroom apartment in the Spring of 2010. They soon became the energetic and hilarious 9-piece ska band that you would make a point to go out and watch during your drunken adventures. They now play as a 6-piece band and are based out of Long Island, NY. Their debut album "Cheers" is available pretty much everywhere!

Fatboy Slim.bio: Fatboy Slim is a pseudonym of Quentin Leo Cook (b. 31 Jul 1963 in Reigate, Surrey - aka Norman Cook), an English big beat musician. Quentin Leo Cook was educated at Reigate Grammar School. He started a punk fanzine titled Peroxide with his neighbour Andrew Thomas and art student Ian McKay (formerly Laidlaw). Contemporary with his publishing activities, he played drums in Disque Attack (a British new-wave-influenced rock band) for which he later performed lead vocals.

SAINT SLUMBER.bio: Hailing from small towns throughout New Jersey, SAINT SLUMBER is a band eager to eschew conventional labels. The band found its earliest start when vocalist Joshua Perna and guitarist Aaron Brown met as children; the duo spent their formative years writing music and opening for acts such as twenty one pilots, Andrew McMahon & the Wilderness, and Circa Survive, honing their craft as songwriters and performers. When the pair met drummer Matt Carpenter, SAINT SLUMBER was born.

Kaskade.bio: Kaskade is the stage name for Ryan Raddon, a DJ/producer/remixer/artist who was born in Evanston, Illinois in 1972. He went to college at BYU Provo prior to serving a church mission for The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter Day Saints in Japan, and when he came back he transferred the University of Utah and lived in Salt Lake city Utah. He now resides in Los Angeles, CA. Prior to being a recording artist, he was a record store owner and a club DJ at a nightclub in Salt Lake City as a way to make ends meet for him and his wife.

Graphic Nature.bio: Graphic Nature are a British nu metalcore band, based in Kent, England. The band's style mixes nu metal and metalcore with influences of electronic music, whilst its lyrics are themed around mental health. After self-releasing four singles, the band signed to Rude Records in 2021. The band released their debut album A Mind Waiting to Die in 2023, which they supported with performances at the 2000trees, Download, Reading & Leeds and Misery Loves Company festivals.

Kumo 99.bio: Kumo 99's brand of dance music is cut through with a punk spirit, mixing jungle and drum n’ bass breaks with a moshpit ready aesthetic to create something visceral and explosive.
similar: DJ これからの緊急災害

Lite.bio: 1. LITE are an Instrumental Math Rock band from Tokyo, Japan, formed in 2003. Their members are: Nobuyuki Takeda (guitar), Kozo Kusumoto (guitar), Jun Izawa (bass) and Akinori Yamamoto (drums). Releases: LITE (2005) Filmlets (2006) Live in Limerick (2007) A Tiny Twofer (split with Funanori, 2007) Phantasia (2008) Live in Leeds (2009) Live in New York (2009) Turns Red EP (2009) Live in Los Angeles (2010) Illuminate (2010) For All the Innocence (2011)
similar: How to count one to ten

Age Factory.bio: Eisuke Shimizu (Vocal, Guitar), Naoto Nishiguchi (Bass), Hisato Mashiko (Drums): a rock band from Nara, Japan, with the average age of 21 years-old. Since their formation on April of 2010, they developed their live activities nation-wide, with their home town of Nara as base, and accomplished about 100 live shows during the period of a year. Their fans keep increasing rapidly, thanks to their live performances called “destructive”, the strong messages conveyed with Eisuke Shimizu’s husky voice

WAGDUG FUTURISTIC UNITY.bio: WAGDUG FUTURISTIC UNITY is a side-project from Hiroshi Kyono (of The Mad Capsule Markets). He released a single in 2007 (september) and a mini-cd just month later on October 24th, which features guest appearances from artists/bands like DJ Starscream (of Slipknot), Shitdisco, Motor, Cycheouts G and Ultra Brain. WAGDUG FUTURISTIC UNITY released its first full length album "HAKAI" on July 23rd of 2008 featuring guests such as Chino Moreno (of Deftones fame)

To Kill Achilles.bio: Dundee, Scotland (2009-present): A very lively group with a big presence on stage, To Kill Achilles fast growing success has pushed them harder to make everything thing they do the best. No strangers to playing large crowds they have built up a reputation as exciting, big performers keeping the excitement from start to finish. The band approach everything they do with a lot of professionalism and a very open mind, while keeping their ambition very prominent making them a brilliant band to work with in every aspect of the business

SWEET SPINE.bio: Emerging from Greenville, South Carolina, SWEET SPINE (stylized in all caps) is a vibrant alternative rock trio blending grunge, shoegaze, and punk. Founded in 2021 by Fox Haynes (vocals and lead guitar), alongside Taylor Priola (bass) and Brannan Crook (drums), the band quickly captured attention with their heartfelt sound and infectious energy. Their breakout single Darkness went viral in 2022, amassing nearly a million views on TikTok and over 1.

Totorro.bio: Totorro are an instrumental math rock/post rock band from Rennes, France. They have released 2 EPs (Self-Titled & All Glory To John Baltor) and 2 full lengths - Home Alone and Come To Mexico. All are available at http://totorro.bandcamp.com/. Visit http://www.facebook.com/pages/TOTORRO/117966698228573?sk=info or http://www.myspace.com/totorromusic for more information.
similar: And So I Watch You From Afar

KinokoTeikoku.bio: Kinoko Teikoku (きのこ帝国, lit. "Mushroom Empire") was a Japanese shoegaze/dream pop band, formed in 2007. The band suspended activities in 2019 when bassist 谷口滋昭 left to support his family business at a temple. With the other members feeling unable to continue without all 4 founding members they put the project on hiatus. Official statements from the band and all members: https://web.archive.org/web/20210723185857/https://kinokoteikoku.com/news/4334/
similar: Seventeen Years Old and Berlin Wall

Earthists..bio: Earthists. is a four-piece progressive metalcore band hailing from Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan. The band was formed in early 2015 and consists of vocalist Yui Kawashima, guitarist and vocalist Yuto Hirabayashi, bassist Shugo Kurokawa, and drummer Yuya Fujii. Initially, the band had a second guitarist, Yuta, who later departed. Their musical style is rooted in djent and metalcore, with additional influences from jazz and pop, reflecting the diverse musical backgrounds of each member.

The Callous Daoboys.bio: The Callous Daoboys is a mathcore band from Atlanta, Georgia. They have released two EPs, "My Dixie Wrecked" and "Animal Tetris," a single, "The Great Multi-Stage Rocket Robbery," and a full-length album, "Die On Mars," which was released on June 21, 2019 via Dark Trail Records (CD), Acrobat Unstable (12" vinyl), and Pattern Recognition Records (cassette). The band performed at Mathcore Index Fest 2018 and MIF2020, were featured in Altpress' list of top 20 rising hardcore bands you need to hear right now

HourHouse.bio: HourHouse is a metalcore band from New Orleans, LA. The band is Justice Martin (vocals), Nick Trahan (guitar/vocals), John Trosclair (bass), Danny Solet Jr (guitar), and Sean Doria (drums).

The Oklahoma Kid.bio: The Oklahoma kid is a progressive metalcore band from Rostock, Germany. The group originally played deathcore in their first formation in 2007, but the band broke up in 2009 and officially reformed in 2014 releasing their debut EP Fortuneteller with the new sound/style. bandcamp: http://theoklahomakid.bandcamp.com facebook: https://www.facebook.com/theoklahomakidofficial

Oceans Ate Alaska.bio: Oceans Ate Alaska is a British metalcore band hailing from Birmingham formed in 2010. The band was signed to Fearless Records and are known for "uniting boundaries between multiple genres in modern metal" and for "fusing unpredictable polyrhythms and sporadic partnering of melodic and dissonant passages". Original vocalist, James Harrison came up with the band name; naming it after the 1958 Lituya Bay earthquake and megatsunami that swallowed one of Alaska’s gulfs.

House of Pain.bio: House of Pain was an Irish-styled American hip-hop group who released three albums in the early to mid 90's before lead rapper Everlast decided to pursue his solo career again. The group is best known for its 1992 hit single Jump Around. The band's history starts with a failed short solo career, Everlast teamed up with DJ Lethal and high school friend Danny Boy to form House of Pain. The group was signed to Tommy Boy Records, and their self-titled debut album (1992) went multi-platinum

DEAD POET SOCIETY.bio: Dead Poet Society is a rock band from Boston, Massachusetts. Members: Lead vocals/Guitar-Jack Underkofler Guitar-Jack Collins Bass-Dylan Brenner Drums-Will Goodroad

Polkadot Stingray.bio: Polkadot Stingray (ポルカドットスティングレイ) is a 4-piece indie rock band from Fukuoka. Members: Shizuku (雫) - Vocals / Guitar Harushi Ejima (エジマハルシ) - Guitar Yuuki Uemura (ウエムラユウキ) - Bass Kazuma Mitsuyasu (ミツヤスカズマ) - Drums

Valorant.bio: VALORANT is a 5v5 character-based tactical shooter video game from Riot Games launched worldwide June 2, 2020.

Their Dogs Were Astronauts.bio: Their Dogs Were Astronauts is an instrumental progressive Metal and Djent band project from Austria which was founded in 2014. The music of the two brothers Denis and Leonard Roth, who both studied music, focuses on riff heavy instrumental music seasoned with catchy melodies. With the use of 7- and 8-string guitars they explore all the musical possibilities that these extended range instruments offer. The project is musically influenced by many different styles and genres

White Zombie.bio: White Zombie was an American heavy metal band formed in 1985 in New York City. The group initially played noise rock, releasing three EPs and one studio album in that style before transitioning to a heavy metal sound that gained them mainstream recognition. Their albums "La Sexorcisto: Devil Music Volume One" (1992) and "Astro-Creep: 2000" (1995) were influential in the development of groove metal and industrial metal, respectively. Some of their best-known songs include "Thunder Kiss '65," "Black Sunshine," and "More Human than Human.

Vildhjarta.bio: Vildhjarta is a progressive metal band from Hudiksvall, Sweden. The band plays in an aberrant Meshuggah-influenced musical style with use of djent guitar. The band is characterized by harmonic minor progressions, jarring enharmonic staccato riffs, layers of reverberant guitars and dual lead vocalists. They were signed to Century Media Records in 2011. The band's name, Vildhjarta, originates from the Swedish role-playing game Drakar och Demoner. Vildhjärta (not "vildhjarta") means "wild heart" in Swedish.

Dealer.bio: There are four bands with this name; 1) Dealer — a nu-metalcore band from Melbourne, Australia. 2) Dealer, formed in Cirencester (UK) in 1979, were a NWOBHM band. 3) DEALER, founded in 1999, made their way through deserts, over mountains and through deep valleys, with a heart for Rock 'n' Roll and a six-string to state it. This four-piece is rooted in several corners of Rock music. Finally the guys find themselves rockin' out like the Rolling Stones in fury, Deep Purple in anger and a bunch of Austrian shepherds on magic mushrooms.

PRINCESSBRI.bio: PRINCESSBRI is a singer, artist from Ottawa, Canada. BRI’s all-platform released album “LITTLE BABY B!TCH” was published in July 2, 2019, making her style of music even more recognized. The Record “Who TF is She?” proved her differen take on alternative, indie, metal, hip hop sound. Earlier in her career, PRINCESSBRI started getting musical attention and new fans from her pop, melodic style of music with the song “Touch” that was released June 14, 2018 that’s currently at 331K views and counting.

The Haunted.bio: 1) The Haunted is a Swedish heavy metal band from Gothenburg, formed in 1996. The original lineup consisted of Anders Björler, Jonas Björler, Peter Dolving, Adrian Erlandsson, and Patrik Jensen. Anders and Jonas Björler, along with Erlandsson, had previously been members of the melodic death metal band At the Gates. As of 2025, the band's lineup consists of Marco Aro on vocals, Patrik Jensen on rhythm guitar, Ola Englund on lead guitar, Jonas Björler on bass, and Adrian Erlandsson on drums.

Lotus Eater.bio: There are multiple artists with this name: 1) A Serbian electronic artist. 2) A folk band from USA. 3) Metalcore band from the UK 4)Doom metal/stoner metal/progressive metal band formed in 2012 5) A collaboration between electronic artists Rrose and Lucy 1) Lotus Eater is an Electronic music producer and DJ operating under variety of pseudonyms. Brought up in Belgrade, Serbia, he spends his days mastering the arts of programming, production and web design.

Brooklyn Funk Essentials.bio: Brooklyn Funk Essentials is an acid-jazz, funk, and hip hop collective featuring musicians and poets from different cultures. The band was conceived in 1993 by producer Arthur Baker and bassist and musical director Lati Kronlund. Lati Kronlund played the bass, and other members included Hanifah Walidah (aka Sha-key), Joi Cardwell, Papa Dee and Stephanie McKay (vocals), Everton Sylvester and David Allen (dub poetry), Josh Roseman (trombone), Bob Brockmann (trumpet), Paul Shapiro (sax & flute), Yancy Drew and Tony Allen (drums), E.

Eric Prydz.bio: Eric Prydz is a Swedish DJ and producer best known for his 2004 hit single "Call On Me," his remix of Pink Floyd's "Another Brick In The Wall" (known as "Proper Education"), and more recently the anthem "Pjanoo." He also releases music under the aliases of Cirez D, Pryda and Sheridan. Prydz produces electronic music across a variety of subgenres, most notably house (including electro house and tech house), minimal, and techno. Aside from producing music

Wolfgang Gartner.bio: Wolfgang Gartner, (real name: Joey Youngman), is a Californian electro / tech house producer and DJ currently based in Austin, Texas, United States. He produced deep house until late 2007 under his actual name before switching to his current Wolfgang Gartner moniker. He began producing electronic music at age 11 by syncing a drum machine to a keyboard via MIDI, and taught himself to DJ at age 12 with a 4-track tape recorder and 2 tape decks. In 2004 after a year of successful releases

Boards of Canada.bio: Boards of Canada are a Scottish electronic music duo consisting of brothers Mike Sandison and Marcus Eoin. They initially signed to Skam Records and later to Warp Records in the 1990s, gaining wider recognition after the release of their debut studio album "Music Has the Right to Children" in 1998. The album was followed by Geogaddi (2002), The Campfire Headphase (2005), Tomorrow's Harvest (2013), and Inferno (2026), all of which contributed to their reputation within electronic music for distinctive production and atmospheric composition.

Little Boots.bio: Little Boots is the stage name of the electropop singer-songwriter Victoria Christina Hesketh (born in 4 May 1984 in Blackpool, Lancashire, England). Her stage name comes from a nickname given to her by a friend, a reference to her unusually small feet. As a musician, Little Boots sings and plays the piano, keyboards, synthesizer, stylophone and a Japanese electronic instrument called Tenori-on. After a return to college to focus on her studies,

Parkway Drive.bio: Parkway Drive is a metalcore band from Byron Bay, Australia. The band formed in the summer of 2003, their name coming from the street they used to practice on in their home town of Byron Bay, Australia. Not long after they had formed, they released a split EP with Adelaide band I Killed The Prom Queen. Later that year, the band also made an appearance on the Byron Bay Hardcore compilation What We've Built, preceded by their debut EP, Don't Close Your Eyes.

Venom.bio: Venom is an English heavy metal band formed in 1978 in Newcastle upon Tyne. Coming to prominence towards the end of the new wave of British heavy metal, Venom's first two albums—Welcome to Hell (1981) and Black Metal (1982)—are considered a major influence on thrash metal and extreme metal in general. Venom's second album proved influential enough that its title was used as the name of the extreme metal subgenre of black metal. However, Venom's later discography remained speed/thrash metal

Bon Jovi.bio: Bon Jovi is an American rock band formed in 1983 in Sayreville, New Jersey. The current lineup features singer Jon Bon Jovi, keyboardist David Bryan, drummer Tico Torres, guitarists Phil X and John Shanks, percussionist Everett Bradley, and bassist Hugh McDonald. Founding bassist Alec John Such left in 1994, and longtime guitarist and co-songwriter Richie Sambora departed in 2013. The band first gained attention with their 1984 self-titled debut album, propelled by the Top 40 single "Runaway.

S.O.D..bio: S.O.D. (Stormtroopers Of Death), formed in New York in 1985. They are commonly credited as being among the first bands to fuse hardcore punk with thrash metal into a new sound dubbed crossover thrash. They have also been the victim of controversy due to their utterly politically-incorrect lyrics. However, Dan Lilker has stated that “The lyrics were never intended to be serious, just to piss people off.” Stormtroopers Of Death includes the famous Billy Milano (M.

Def Leppard.bio: Def Leppard are an English rock band formed in Sheffield in 1977. Since 1992, the band has consisted of Rick Savage (bass, backing vocals), Joe Elliott (lead vocals), Rick Allen (drums), Phil Collen (guitar, backing vocals), and Vivian Campbell (guitar, backing vocals). They emerged as part of the new wave of British heavy metal in the early 1980s. The band's debut album, "On Through the Night" (1980), reached the Top 15 in the UK but received little attention internationally.

Sentinel Beast.bio: Sentinel Beast was formed in Sacramento, Ca in 1984 by Debbie Gunn (vocals), Mike Spencer (bass), and Scott Awes (drums). During this time, the axes were played by Greg Williams and Jerry Fraiser, later to be replaced by Barry Fischel and Mark Koyasako. Discovered by Kerry King of Slayer, the band recorded and released the influential Depths of Death album, on Metal Blade Records, in 1986. Beast opened up for the likes of Slayer, Exodus, Megadeth, Mercyful Fate, King Diamond, and others.

Thrash or Die.bio: Thrash or Die is a Thrash Metal band hailing from South Florida. The band was formed under unusual circumstances on May 9, 2007 by Vokillist Dr. Fukk and Guitarist, Hellvomit Sodomizer. Just months before the birth of Thrash or Die, Dr. Fukk and Hellvomit Sodomizer were arch enemies. Oddly enough, in the midst of chaos, Dr. Fukk saved Hellvomit Sodomizer from a very dangerous situation and even ceased the opportunity to threaten his life. This unsuspected

Ghost Iris.bio: GHOST IRIS is a product of many Danish bands, who all have put their stamp, on the Danish and European metal scene. From bands like AS WE FIGHT, SHAPED LIKE SWANS, ROAD TO MANILA, A SHELTERING SKY, comes an aggressive, atmospheric, epic and hard-hitting quartet, who will surely rock the foundation of the metal scene. Ghost Iris saw the light of day, when past projects dwindled into obscurity. Their goal was one and the same: They wanted to create music, that was unique for the Danish metal scene and completely its own.

He Is Legend.bio: When He Is Legend signed to Solid State Records, the band had already stirred a blaring buzz all their own. But they were just getting started. 2004 put He Is Legend on the map and on the move. In June, the five North Carolina natives released their debut EP, 91025 with Tribunal Records, a label most noted for helping launch the careers of bands like Atreyu and From Autumn to Ashes. The release received rave reviews, stirring the anticipation even further for He Is Legend's debut full-length album, I Am Hollywood, released November 2nd, 2004.
similar: Maylene and the Sons of Disaster

August Burns Red.bio: August Burns Red is an American metalcore band from Lancaster, Pennsylvania, formed in 2003. The band was formed while most members were in their final year of high school, with early practice sessions taking place in drummer Matt Greiner’s family farm buildings. The lineup of guitarists John Benjamin "JB" Brubaker and Brent Rambler, drummer Matt Greiner, lead vocalist Jake Luhrs, and bassist Dustin Davidson has remained consistent since 2006. The band is recognized for technical guitar riffs

I Prevail.bio: I Prevail is an American metalcore band formed in Southfield, Michigan, in 2013. They released their first EP „Heart vs Mind” a year later. Their rise to popularity came after releasing a cover of Taylor Swift���s Blank Space (also in 2014), which ended up reaching certified platinum in the US. Since then, the band has released three albums: - Lifelines in 2016, which reached #15 on the Billboard 200, - Trauma in 2019, which became their most successful album yet - receiving both a Best Rock Album Grammy nomination

Urban Heat.bio: Urban Heat are a 3-piece American post-punk band from Austin, TX. The band formed in 2019 when multi-instrumentalist and vocalist Jonathan Horstmann enlisted Kevin Naquin and Paxel Foley to form a live show based on his analog sound design. The result fuses dark 80's stylings with modern flourishes, with Horstmann's throaty baritone delivering musings on existence and romanticism against an unrelenting industrial gothic pulse. Their live show is undeniably energetic and engaging

Haisuinonasa.bio: Haisuinonasa (ハイスイノナサ) is a band from Tokyo whose songs are influenced by post-rock, math rock, experimental rock, electronic, and minimalism. The band was formed in 2004 by Yoshimasa and Atsumasa Terui, with Ai Kamano later joining in 2005. They released their first 1st mini album "About Town" in 2009 with the lineup that follows: Ai Kamano (vocal), Yoshimasa Terui (guitar), Atsumasa Terui (bass), Tomoyuki Tamura (key), Keisuke Nakamura (drum). This lineup stayed consistent until Tamura departed in early 2012.
similar: How to count one to ten

Declan Welsh and The Decadent West.bio: Declan Welsh and the Decadent West are a punk band with strong pop influences hailing from East Kilbride. Guaranteed to appeal to lovers of The Clash, they have supported artists including The Proclaimers, Ocean Colour Scene, Eddi Reader and Glasvegas. Welsh’s songwriting covers everyday frustrations, suburban boredom, social commentary and politics. Artists with genuine integrity and something to relevant to say, Welsh and band are a must see. Described by some as a modern amalgamation of Joe Strummer and Billy Bragg

Phantogram.bio: Phantogram are a trip-hop/psych-pop duo which formed in 2007 in Saratoga Springs, New York, USA. The band consists of Sarah Barthel (vocals, keyboards) and Joshua Carter (guitar, vocals). Their music can be described as a culmination of unique street beat rhythms, psychedelic melodies and organic textures. The band practices and writes music in an out-of-town farm called Harmony Lodge. The group name was inspired by the optical illusion called phantogram.

Cog.bio: 1. Cog is an Australian rock band formed in Sydney, New South Wales, in 1998. The group consisted of Flynn Gower (lead guitar, lead vocals), Lucius Borich (drums, samples, backing vocals), and Luke Gower (bass guitar, backing vocals). Flynn Gower and Borich had been friends since their school years and began collaborating musically before forming Cog. Prior to the band’s formation, Borich performed with Juice, while Flynn Gower led a metal band called The Hanging Tree.

Mono No Aware.bio: There has been at least two bands named Mono No Aware: 1) Mono no Aware is a Japanese alternative/indie rock band from Tokyo. The band formed when Shuki Tamaki and Seijun Kato met Keiko Takeda and Yutaka Yanagisawa at university in 2013. Since then they have released 2 albums under P-VINES and SPACE SHOWER MUSIC and self-produced an EP in 2016. 2) An experimental post-punk project from Spencer Curtis of Montreal Quebec. Since 2020 the project expanded into a band and the name was changed to Sunforger.

Cö shu Nie.bio: Cö shu Nie (Japanese: コシュニエ) is a Japanese rock duo, formed in 2011 in Osaka, Japan. The band's current lineup comprises of vocalist/guitarist/keyboardist Miku Nakamura and bassist Shunsuke Matsumoto. Releasing first mini album Hydra independently in 2013, they made their major label debut in 2018 with the release of their first single "Asphyxia" through Sony Music Japan, whose title track was used as the opening theme to the anime series Tokyo Ghoul:re (Season 1).
similar: TK from 凛として時雨

Twelve Foot Ninja.bio: Twelve Foot Ninja is an alternative/progressive metal band from Melbourne, Victoria. They formed in 2007. The band are independent, self-produced and self-funded. Their music encompasses a wide variety of musical styles (metal, alternative rock, funk, jazz, latin, dub and reggae). They are also well known for their visually striking and comedic music videos. Twelve Foot Ninja currently consists of Nik "Kin" Etik (vocals), Steve "Stevic" Mackay (guitar, production), Rohan Hayes (guitar, backing vocals) and Shane "Russ" Russell (drums).

Nubia.bio: 1. Nubia is a metalcore band from Switzerland. 2. Nubia participated in the Dutch preselection contest for the Eurovision Song contest 1998 with 'Ze kwamen overzee'. 3. Dutch balfolk band which released their first CD 'In de Wolken' in 2017. Nubia brings respect for silence in a musical sense to dancers. The delicate melodies are full of grace and intimacy. This creates a dreamy sound experience stimulating people to be together and to be happy.

blacktoothed.bio: blacktoothed, an exciting powerhouse in the diverse heavy scene, is known for their ability to seamlessly blend different genre elements. Whether it's breathtaking 80s shred guitar solos, catchy autotune pop melodies or brutal breakdown riffs, the Leipzig-based trio knows how to capture the attention of a wide audience. Since the release of their first album JULI in summer 2022, singer Hendrik Rathgeber, guitarist Matti Keitel and drummer David Sommer have scored over 800k streams worldwide.

Lamb of God.bio: Lamb of God is an American groove metal band, originally formed under the name Burn the Priest. The roots of Lamb of God were planted in the year 1990, when Mark Morton, Chris Adler and John Campbell were friends at Virginia Commonwealth University. The trio began playing at Adler's house in Richmond weathering chilly conditions. "There was no heat in the house," recalls Campbell. "We would hang around the kerosene heaters, get really drunk and write metal songs. Fumes and Black Label beer were definitely what fueled our early days."

Marina.bio: Marina Lambrini Diamandis, known mononymously as Marina and previously by the stage name Marina & the Diamonds, is a Welsh singer and songwriter. Born in Brynmawr to a Welsh mother and Greek father, Diamandis was raised in Abergavenny and moved to London as a teenager to become a professional singer, despite having little formal musical experience. In 2009, she came to prominence upon placing second in the BBC's Sound of 2010. Her debut studio album, The Family Jewels (2010), incorporated indie pop and new wave musical styles.

Guzior.bio: Guzior Prolog Guzior Ilustracja Guzior podczas nagrywania teledysku do BOILER ROOM Imię i nazwisko Mateusz Bluza Pseudonim Guzior Data i miejsce urodzenia 16 lipca 1992 Wrocław Pochodzenie Polska Instrumenty Wokal Gatunki hip-hop, trap, cloud rap Zawód raper, autor tekstów Aktywność od 2012 Wydawnictwo QueQuality, EVILTWINcorp. Powiązania Quebonafide, Deys, ReTo, Szpaku Wiadomości w Wikinews Wstęp Guzior, właśc. Mateusz Bluza[1] (ur.

Absolute Valentine.bio: Absolute Valentine is a 80's influenced electro band based in Marseille, France.

Like Moths to Flames.bio: Like Moths to Flames is a metalcore band from Columbus, Ohio, United States, that formed in 2010. The band started when vocalist Chris Roetter, former frontman of Agraceful, had many issues concerning members dropping out. Like Moths to Flames was formed with four previous members of Terrafirma. They have five EPs and six full-length albums, the most recent being The Cycles of Trying to Cope which was released on May 10th, 2024 through UNFD. On October 17

Metal Church.bio: Metal Church is an American heavy metal band formed in 1980. The band was established in San Francisco, California, by guitarist and songwriter Kurdt Vanderhoof. In 1981, Vanderhoof relocated to Aberdeen, Washington, where the group briefly operated under the name Shrapnel before reverting to Metal Church in 1983. Over the course of its career, Metal Church has released fourteen studio albums and has been associated with the Seattle-area heavy metal scene of the 1980s.

similar: Zoe Kamen;Máire Brennan;The London Metropolitan Orchestra;Michael Kamen
Hans Zimmer and The Magnificent Six; Pharrell Williams; Johnny Marr
Lord Of The Rings 3 Soundtrack-The Return Of The King (Featuring Sir James
Budapest Strings, Johannes Brahms & Béla Bánfalvi

MD.45.bio: MD.45 was a side project put together by Megadeth's guitarist/frontman Dave Mustaine ,Fear's Lee Ving on singing duties, bassist Kelly LeMieux and former Alice Cooper member Jimmy DeGrasso on drums. Dave Mustaine, fed up with Megadeth at the time, but still interested in music, wanted to create an album showcasing a different musical style than Megadeth. According to an answer given on the Megadeth website, "Mustaine and Ving created the band name by reversing their initials.

Mike Candys.bio: Mike Candys is one of the most successful house producers and remixers of Switzerland. His productions are featured on the biggest house compilations and are played by DJs all over the world. With every new release he reaches top-positions on download stores all over the world.. .. Mike Candys is the official producer of Christopher S & Jack Holiday.. He also produced and remixed tracks for Horny United, DJ Antoine, Rosenstolz, Pat Farrell, MR Fiction, MR P!NK, Jaybee, Lunik and many more. ..

Loser.bio: There are at least 5 artists/bands performing under the name Loser: 1)Loser is a 4 piece band out of Detroit, MI. The band is most commonly categorized as a mix between Nu Metal and Grunge. For fans of Slipknot, Deftones, Korn and Nirvana. 2) Loser is an Australian rock band, formed early 2018. Consisting of members from Apart From This, The Bennies and The Smith Street Band . 3) Loser is an American Rock band, formed in late 2004. The band most notably features John 5 of Marilyn Manson fame as the guitarist.

Sweet Pill.bio: Sweet Pill is the fast and fun band your sweet tooth has been craving. With driving rhythms, complex guitar melodies, and catchy lyrics, this new band is redefining a dying genre. Bandcamp

Softspoken.bio: Formed in December 2015, Kentucky rock/hard rock/post-hardcore band Softspoken is comprised of 5 members: original members Chris Wethington and Billy Zimpelman, Sam Scheuer, Nick Sits and Austin Armstrong.

The Cost.bio: There are 2 known artists under the name "The Cost" 1: "The Cost have "it;" they have the quality which makes a band work, be powerful, original, interesting. Their heavy, intricate, dark songs are completely engaging and fresh, but very much in keeping with what many post- "emo" punk bands are striving for; music that is melodious but not poppy; lyrical but rough. Their music is a modern punk which incorporates sounds from the mid-eighties DC scene (Gray Matter)

Hybrid.bio: At least six musical acts are known to go by the name Hybrid. In order of popularity: 1) Mike Truman and Chris Healings have been writing, producing, touring and DJing all over the globe for the best part of a decade, amassing near 250 productions. The duo has written four LPs to date: Wide Angle, 1999; Morning Sci-Fi, 2003; I Choose Noise, 2006; and Disappear Here, 2010 – all on Distinctive. Since first bursting onto the scene with the now classic album

Strawberry Hospital.bio: Neptune Gomez is the name behind Strawberry Hospital, a one-piece band from Texas. Strawberry Hospital uses the electronic elements of trancecore, along with the droning riffs and harsh screams of black metal. Neptune's lyricism is deeply sentimental and handle struggles with identity and emotional vulnerability, delivered with melodic vocals, screams and the use of vocaloid.
similar: Fear, and Loathing in Las Vegas
A Rose Dying In The Rain

Zeal & Ardor.bio: The African American Soul/Gospel influenced black metal of Zeal & Ardor's music is the brainchild of musician Manuel Gagneux. He moved from his native Switzerland to New York, and took up the moniker Zeal & Ardor, having previously released music under the name Birdmask. The chamber pop of his Birdmask recordings is a far cry from the darker, heavier music he created for his Zeal & Ardor debut Devil Is Fine. He released the record in 2016, and Gagneux claims the project was kickstarted by a thread on a music board on the website 4Chan
