After completing a stack of remixes for the likes of Gorillaz, Chase & Status and Chris Lake, Feed Me released his much anticipated 'Feed Me's Big Adventure EP' (also on Mau5trap) exclusively to Beatport on Christmas Day 2010.

Mildrage.bio: mildrage was a Japanese metalcore/post-hardcore band from Atsugi, Kanagawa Prefecture, formed in March 2016. The original lineup consisted of vocalists MEG and t.e.p.p.e.i, guitarist Jun, bassist Kaz and drummer Kengo. The band’s sound incorporated a dual vocal approach, contrasting MEG’s clean vocals with t.e.p.p.e.i’s aggressive screams, and their lyrical themes centered on expressions of inner anger, sadness, suffering, and disappointment.

Billy Newton-Davis.bio: Billy Newton-Davis (born April 26, 1951, in Cleveland, Ohio, United States) is an American-Canadian R&B, jazz and gospel singer and songwriter. Newton-Davis grew up in Shaker Heights, Ohio, and as a teenager he was one of two lead singers in a local soul band called “The Illusions”. He received his Bachelor Of Fine Arts Degree from Ohio University and upon graduation moved to New York to pursue his dreams on Broadway. He appeared in several Broadway shows including “Bubblin Brown Sugar”

Deadmau5, Wolfgang Gartner.bio: Collaboration between house artists deadmau5 & Wolfgang Gartner. They released the single "Animal Rights" together.

CrazyEightyEight.bio: CrazyEightyEight is a post-hardcore trio formed in 2017 that features vocalist Lauren Babic, guitarist Jarrod Alonge and bassist Patty Walters. Jarrod joined forces with Lauren to create the initial releases, which included the 'No Words Spoken' EP and a compilation of cover songs. Patty joined shortly after, contributing to the single "Shinebox." Their debut full-length album 'Burning Alive' was self-released in December of 2018 following a successful Kickstarter campaign

Invent, Animate.bio: Invent Animate is a 5-piece djent band from Port Neches, Texas, formed in 2011. They self-released their debut EP titled Waves on March 13, 2012. After a number of regional tours and increasing popularity in the djent community, the band was signed by Tragic Hero Records in February 2014. The band announced they would release their debut full-length album titled Everchanger on August 26, 2014. Their sophomore album Stillworld was released on July 8, 2016 & their third album and first with vocalist Marcus Vik

UVERworld.bio: UVERworld (ウーバーワールド) is a Japanese rock band known for their mixture of musical genres such as hard rock, electronic, and beat-boxing. UVERchan, the band's mascot, is a fictional bear-bat like character, which appears on several of their covers. The five-member band known as UVERworld first formed in 2003, and were known as Sound Goku Road(sound極road) or, more popularly, "Sangoku Road." The band originally consisted of seven members, and released a demo CD which ended up selling 3,000 copies.

Klaxons.bio: Klaxons were an indie rock band, based in London. The band consisted of Jamie Reynolds (bass, vocals), James Righton (keyboards, vocals), Simon Taylor-Davies (guitar) and Steffan Halperin (drums). They have been on indefinite hiatus since 2014. Reynolds, Righton and Taylor-Davies formed the band in New Cross, London, United Kingdom, in 2005 Their debut single, "Gravity's Rainbow" was released on 29 March 2006 as a 500 copies limited 7-inch vinyl, decorated by the band themselves.

Audrey Fall.bio: Audrey Fall was a Latvian instrumental rock band from Jelgava, Latvia. Formed in 2010, the band went through a lineup and music style change. After working on their new material for two years, Audrey Fall returned with their self-recorded debut album Mitau, released on January 14th, 2014. Their music featured influences from both the soft side of post-rock and the heavier side of post-metal, resulting in contrasting sounds, progressive rhythms and distinct melodies.

Blue Öyster Cult.bio: Blue Öyster Cult (often abbreviated BÖC or BOC) is an American rock band formed on the campus of Stony Brook University on Long Island, New York, in 1967, whose most successful work includes the hard rock songs "(Don't Fear) The Reaper," "Godzilla," "Black Blade," "Burnin' for You" and "Shooting Shark." Blue Öyster Cult has sold more than 24 million records worldwide, including 7 million in the United States alone. The band's music videos, especially "Burnin' for You

Yard.bio: YARD is an emerging Dublin-based electro-noise/electropunk trio formed by Emmet White (vocals/synthesizer), Dan Malone (guitar), and George Ryan (drums/synthesizer). Known for intense, pulsating live performances that bridge techno and noise rock, creating an auditory onslaught that leaves audiences sweaty, energized, and strangely unnerved. The band frequently features their "Bucketman" mascot. Influenced by artists like Nine Inch Nails, SUUNS, and Gilla Band, YARD are gaining recognition for their gritty, energetic sound.

Horskh.bio: New French electro-indus sensation, Horskh is a collision between the raw energy of EBM, the violence of metal and the trance of electro. During their explosive concerts, the acoustic drums bring an organic depth to the saturated surges of the guitar and the machines. Beyond the melodic rage of the song, the live energy of the duo reinforces the impact of a sound material viscerally dedicated to movement. An oscillation between dancefloor and pogo!

umezu kazutoki KIKI band.bio: Kazutoki Umezu "Kiki" Band, is difficult to categorize. It may be the newest type of Jazz-Rock. Its sound may be like progressive rock. They like unusual beats. But basically it must be jazz. If you listen the rhythm, you'll feel rock, and if you follow the melody you feel like listening to ethnic music. And each member's solo performance is very powerful, soulful and interesting. Now their music and teamwork is very tight and full of energy. "KIKI" was formed by Kazutoki Umezu at 1999 for a tour to Africa.
similar: Anders Johansson, Jens Johansson and Allan Holdsworth

1inamillion.bio: 1inamillion is a four-piece post-rock from Shinjuku, Japan. They released their first demo on bandcamp February 29, 2016. https://1inamillion.bandcamp.com/album/demo
similar: You You You All The Same

Pete Cottrell.bio: Pete Cottrel is an instrumental 8 string metalcore/djent guitarist who posts videos of himself on youtube playing various guitars and makes extensive use of effects and equipment to make his music more interesting, Since he doesn't or hasn't released official albums or appears to be a "band" he also does the drum tracks which only leaves a vocalist to be desired. it is reccomended that those videos are translated into MP3/FLAC and tagged for ease of playback and scrobbling.

Antonio Vivaldi.bio: Antonio Lucio Vivaldi (4 March 1678 – 28 July 1741) was an Italian composer, virtuoso violinist, impresario of Baroque music, and Roman Catholic priest. He is regarded as one of the leading Baroque composers, with a significant influence across Europe during his lifetime. Vivaldi contributed to developments in orchestration, violin technique, and programmatic music. He played a key role in consolidating the concerto form, particularly the solo concerto, into a widely recognised musical idiom.

Ally Nicholas.bio: Up-and-comer Ally Nicholas is bound to make you a fan of rock and all that the genre has to offer. Born in Chicago, her journey has not been one without bumps in the road. Early adolescence proved to be tumultuous, and Nicholas found no reprieve in her teenage years. Turning to music for comfort, songwriting quickly became the only way to make sense of the world happening around her. Characterized by a seasoned, innate darkness and drawing from subgenres like grunge and metal, Ally soon found hope and direction in pursuing her artistry.

No Pressure.bio: There are multiple artists with the name No Pressure: 1) No Pressure is a pop punk band fronted by The Story So Far's Parker Cannon, Harry Corrigan of Regulate plays drums and Pat Kennedy of Light Years is on guitars. They debuted in 2020 with a 5-song self-titled EP. In 2021 they released two standalone singles "Bed Of Nails" in August & "Can't Forget" in December. In August 2022 they released their self-titled debut full-length album. They also added bassist AJ Hoenings who played for Drain.

キュビノワ.bio: (For those who can't read Japanese, Their name is 'Qubinoix') 『COME OVER TO THE DEEPEND』『NO THANK YOU』期コールター・オブ・ザ・ディーパーズそしてBP.で活躍していたイチマキさん、同じくBP. (再活動後)やSoakのテツさん、更に我らがCAUCUS初代ドラマーのガンビーさんからなるキュビノワ。 ディーパーズやBP.のオリジナ���ティ溢れるオルタナ〜シューゲイザー・サウンドを引き継いだ新バンドの初音源が遂にリリース！

Texas 3000.bio: Texas 3000 are a Japanese indie rock band.

KAQRIYOTERROR.bio: KAQRIYOTERROR, previously known as 幽世テロルArchitect (Kaqriyo Terror Architect), was a Japanese idol group formed in August 2017, under the label codomomental. On December 28, 2022, the group announced that on April 23, 2023, at their "KAQRIYOTERROR THE LOST ONEMAN LIVE", Noa Rondo, Nonamera (nonayu) and RЯ would leave the group, while Sumomo would be leaving effective immediately. NOTE: Please use the artist tag 幽世テロルArchitect for music released prior to the lilithpride single.
similar: The Idol Formerly Known As LADYBABY

ivri.bio: ivri is a singer, songwriter, and producer based in Brooklyn, New York. Her music blends lo-fi, dream pop, indie rock, and psychedelia, characterized by soft vocals and introspective lyrics. She first gained attention by anonymously posting her songs on Instagram, where they were widely sampled in the lo-fi community and racked up millions of streams. In 2020, ivri began releasing original music, starting with the single “Glow Worms”, which became a streaming success.

Living Colour.bio: Living Colour is an American rock band formed in New York City in 1984. The band's lineup consists of guitarist Vernon Reid, vocalist Corey Glover, drummer Will Calhoun, and bassist Doug Wimbish, who replaced Muzz Skillings in 1992. Their music incorporates elements of progressive rock, heavy metal, funk, jazz, hip-hop, punk rock, alternative rock, blues, ska, swing, and other styles. The band's lyrics frequently address personal and political subjects, including social commentary on racism in the United States.

Arimea.bio: Starting out in 2019, Arimea came straight out the gate with their first show at O2 Academy Oxford supporting Press to MECO with Chapter and Verse days before releasing their debut Castle Bravo - EP. Since this, the band have played numerous shows around the UK, Impressing those in attendance with their ferocious live show, Filled with energy and Power. This high energy live show helped them get through the semi finals of Metal To The Masses, a battle of the bands competition to play Bloodstock

Earth, Wind & Fire.bio: Earth, Wind & Fire (abbreviated as EW&F or EWF) is an American band formed in Chicago, Illinois, in 1969. Their music encompasses a wide range of genres, including jazz, R&B, soul, funk, disco, pop, Latin, and Afro-pop. They are among the best-selling bands in music history, with estimated worldwide sales exceeding 90 million records. The band was founded by Maurice White, evolving from an earlier group known as the Salty Peppers. Their career includes a hiatus between mid-1984 and mid-1987.

Dream Fatigue.bio: Dream Fatigue is a new band formed by Matt Wood formerly of Fleshwater.

Press to Meco.bio: Press To MECO is an English Rock band from Crawley and Croydon. It consists of bassist Jake Crawford, drummer Lewis Williams and guitarist Luke Caley. All three members take up vocal responsibilities. Their style of music is often labelled as alternative rock, progressive rock, post-hardcore, mathcore and tech metal. They are known for fusing intricate riffs, progressive sensibilities and math-influenced structures with pop choruses and three-part melodies.

X-Ecutioners.bio: The X-Ecutioners is a group of hip hop DJs/turntablists from New York. They used to include 11 members and were originally called X-Men, after the Marvel Comics characters, but had to change their name due to copyright infringement. The crew was later reduced to members Rob Swift, Roc Raida, Total Eclipse, and Mista Sinista, the last of whom later left the group. Their original name was chosen partly because Super DJ Clark Kent's crew of DJs was called the Supermen.

Scowl.bio: There are at least three bands named Scowl. 1. Scowl is a hardcore punk band from Santa Cruz, CA, United States, established in 2019. They released two albums: How the Flowers Grow (2021) and Are We All Angels (2025). Personnel: Cole Gilbert (drums), Malachi Greene (guitar), Bailey Lupo (bass), Kat Moss (vocals), Mikey Bifolco (guitar). 2. Scowl is a grind / death grind band on Speed Ritual Records. 3. Scowl is a Brooklyn, NY, United States-based grindcore band that started in 2011.

Ellis.bio: There is more than one artist with this name: 1) Ellis was a UK rock band in the 1970s that made two albums and was named after the front man/singer Steve Ellis. 2) Vocalist Steve Ellis scored several top ten hits with Love Affair in the 60s, and his soulful voice saw positive comparisons to those other great Brit vocal 'Steves' of the time (Marriott and Winwood). Ellis were a combo that he put together with Zoot Money in the early 70s. Their first

Black Peaks.bio: Black Peaks (formerly known as Shrine) were a 4 piece rock band from Brighton, England active between 2012 and 2021. The quartet offer a cross between aggressive hardcore, the grandeur of post rock and the atypical characteristics of math rock. They released two albums (Statues and All that Dividies) before splitting up in July 2021.

DAEDRA.bio: There are two artists named DAEDRA. 1] An instrumental artist. 2] An electronic music artist on MrHappyFace Records 3] Black Metal/Ambient. https://daedra666.bandcamp.com/

Panchiko.bio: Panchiko are an English alternative rock band formed in Nottingham, England in 1997, originally consisting of lead vocalist and guitarist Owain Davies, guitarist and keyboardist Andrew "Andy" Wright, bassist Shaun Ferreday, and a drummer named John. Panchiko's members were childhood friends with cheap equipment, who founded the band in 1997 as teenagers. On 21 July 2016, a 4chan user reported that they had found a copy of D>E>A>T>H>M>E>T>A>L in an Oxfam charity store in Sherwood, Nottingham.
similar: LSD and the Search for God

Thief.bio: There are, at least, seven artists known by this name. 1) THIEF is night music for haunted ballrooms and electric churches. Based in Los Angeles and fronted by D. Neal (ex-Botanist). 2) A collaborative project between singer-songwriter Sascha Gottschalk (voice & guitar) & Extended Spirit. So far they're best known for contributions to the 'Secret Love compilations', as conceived by Jazzanova & Resoul (Soultrade Record Store). They have also collaborated with Clara Hill, Andre Zimma & Airmate.

Coeur Noir.bio: Coeur noir (black heart), a side project from A.X. [Suicidal despair] and another guy.

CRX.bio: CRX is a band from Los Angeles, California comprised of Nick Valensi (Vocals/Guitar), Ralph Alexander (Drums), Brad Oberhofer (Keyboard/Backing Vocals), Jon Safley (Bass/Backing Vocals), and Darian Zahedi (Guitar/Backing Vocals). Original keyboardist Richie Follin left in 2018. The band formed in 2013 and released their debut album 'New Skin' on October 28th, 2016. The album was mostly written by just Valensi and served as his first solo effort upon branching out from The Strokes.

Lynyrd Skynyrd.bio: Lynyrd Skynyrd is an American rock band formed in Jacksonville, Florida, in 1964. The group originally formed under the name My Backyard and included Ronnie Van Zant (vocals), Gary Rossington (guitar), Allen Collins (guitar), Larry Junstrom (bass), and Bob Burns (drums). Over the next several years, the band performed under various names and underwent multiple lineup changes while touring small venues. In 1968, they adopted the name Lynyrd Skynyrd, later becoming associated with the Southern rock genre.
similar: Bob Seger & The Silver Bullet Band

Late 90s.bio: Emerging out of Perth’s coastal suburbia, LATE 90s are the product of two decades of friendship between Elliot Lee (drums) and twins Zaac Morgan (lead guitar) and Brody Morgan (bass). December 2019 saw the addition of Steve Loreck (vocals and rhythm guitar), rekindling the founding members’ passion for music after over three years without a gig.

VOWER.bio: There are at least 2 artists with the name Vower: 1. Dutch act: https://vower.bandcamp.com/ With a S/T EP (2019) with tracks 'Billionaire', 'Girls and Boys' and 'The New Life' 2. UK act: https://vowerofficial.bandcamp.com/ With their single 'Shroud' (2023)

YuToRi.bio: There are at least two bands sharing the name yutori: 1) Visual Kei duo, 2) Japanese rock band 1) Active 2011-??. A collaboration between Keke (Medi@lize) and ToruChang (ex. Medi@lize). Music: 透ch Vocals: けけ(ヶ´∀｀) Toru's Youtube channel, where you can preview YuToRi songs. https://musicbrainz.org/artist/61545427-2ad1-43d6-b388-1e16be835f21 2) Active 2021- https://musicbrainz.org/artist/96a59360-b6ff-4920-8f31-e9ee76181afb

HIGHSOCIETY.bio: HIGHSOCIETY is a electronic music producer from San Fransisco. With his trademark "DATAMOSH" style, HIGHSOCIETY finds an intricate balance between crushingly heavy metalcore-tinged cyberpunk woven together with infectious electronic, drum & bass, and trap elements. HIGHSOCIETY continues to innovate and expand his DATAMOSH universe with genre-bending collaborations alongside major rock/metal artists such as Escape The Fate, AWOLNATION, and Attack Attack.

Flow.bio: Refers to many artists: 1. The group's base was created in 1993 when brothers Kōshi (elder: vocals) and Take (younger: guitar) began playing together. They formed Flow in 1998, and were eventually joined by Keigo (vocals), Gotō (bass) and Iwasaki (drums; joined in 2000). In 2001, the band released its first maxi single, "Flow #0". The group then released two mini-albums within the same year, both of which took nationwide indie charts by storm. "Okuru Kotoba", Flow's first cover single, was released in January 2003.

Harper.bio: 1. Harper shot to internet stardom when she released an incredible one-take vocal cover of Spiritbox's breakout hit in summer 2021, clocking up 310k YouTube views and media accolades from the likes of Kerrang!, Metal Hammer, Loudwire, Rock Sound, Revolver and more. She gained millions of new fans when she auditioned for primetime NBC show, America's Got Talent with the same song, garnering a further 3.1 million YouTube views and wowing both the judges and the 4000 audience members with her impressive vocal prowess.

Boots.bio: 1. BOOTS (born Jordy Asher) is a Soul and R&B producer, rapper, singer, songwriter, video director and multi-instrumentalist who first made forays into music as half of the musical duo Blonds, and into popular music as Beyoncé's producer. After trying out a string of lesser-known groups including Blonds, Young Circles, Blond Fuzz and Stonefox, he signed to Jay Z's label Roc Nation in June 2013 to eventually release his original solo music. Boots' meteoric rise to fame came about when Beyoncé's self-titled album debuted at no.

Badflower.bio: Badflower is an alternative rock band which was formed in February 2013 in Los Angeles. Line-up: Josh Katz (lead vocals / rhythm guitar), Joey Morrow (lead guitar / backing vocals), Anthony Sonetti (drums), Alex Espiritu (bass). Discography: - About a Girl (EP, 2013) - Heroin (Single, 2014) - Soap (Single, 2015) - Animal (Single, 2016) - Temper (EP, 2016) - Heroin (Rock Edit) (Single, 2017) - Ghost (Single, 2018) - x ANA x (Single, 2018) - Promise Me (Single, 2019)

Memorist.bio: Memorist was a six-piece alternative metal band that hailed from South West England. The band consisted of Jon Sinfield on vocals and production, Chris Biddiscombe on guitars, Ash Scott on guitars and production, Crayg on electronics, Josh on bass, and Benji on drums. The concept of Memorist took shape in early 2018 when Chris encountered an old friend of Jon's during his wedding in Las Vegas, who happened to be a photographer for the event. Impressed by Jon's vocal abilities, they began collaborating remotely, writing and demoing tracks.

Splutter.bio: There are several bands with this name; 1. a Russian nu metal band, 2. a UK punk band from Manchester, 3. a UK punk band from Bristol, 4. a French hip-hop project. --- 1) Splutter (previous Rock & roll queen) is a nu-metal band based Far East. Formed in 2022 on the ruins of a previous group that has accumulated enough experience to create a massive project with material that can compete with international musical projects. Splutter has 7 members – vocalist, 2 guitarists, bassist, drummer, DJ and showman.

Hellyeah.bio: Hellyeah, is an American Groove metal supergroup, consisting of Mudvayne vocalist Chad Gray, former Nothingface guitarist Tom Maxwell, bass player Kyle Sanders (who is the brother of Mastodon bassist and vocalist Troy Sanders), and guitarist Christian Brady. The idea to form a supergroup originated in 2000 on the Tattoo the Earth tour, although plans were constantly put on hold due to scheduling conflicts. The summer of 2006 allowed the band to take the project seriously and record its first album.

Palaye Royale.bio: Palaye Royale is an American band from Las Vegas, formed in 2008 by Remington Leith, Sebastian Danzig, and Emerson Barrett, whom are all brothers. The brothers' surname was Kropp. Each one's stage name is their first name and their middle name. They formed under the name Kropp Circle in 2008 and changed their name to Palaye Royale in 2011, named after the dance hall (Palais Royale) that their grandparents met at. Style: Fashion-art rock, rock and roll, glam rock, indie rock and garage rock.

Helms Alee.bio: Helms Alee is a Seattle three-piece formed in 2007 by Ben Verellen (Harkonen, Roy, These Arms Are Snakes) and is completed by Dana James and Hozoji Margullis. The band combines heavy rock with melodic arrangements and seems to have an ear for excellent composing. All members of the band exchange lead vocals duties and occasionally teams up for harmonies. After having released their self-titled and one-sided debut LP on small labels, their full-length debut Night Terror was released on HydraHead in August 2008.

Valis Ablaze.bio: Valis Ablaze set out to provide an intriguing alternative to the progressive metal norm; low end grooves juxtaposed with soaring vocal lines, crisp milton-cleans and atmospheric synths, all driven by intricate rhythm work and growling bass - a huge spectrum of energy, emotion, power and beauty. And with the band being spread out all across the UK, playing together is not only cathartic but coalescent too. After the success of the January 2017 'Insularity' EP produced by Drewsif Stalin

Kill the Lights.bio: Kill The Lights is the name of many bands: 1. Kill The Lights was a post-hardcore/alternative rock band from Columbus, Ohio, United States. They did an EP in 2015 called Safe And Sound. And another EP in 2017 called Closing Currents. ktlband.bandcamp.com twitter.com/killthelightsoh 2. Kill The Lights is a heavy metal/metalcore band from UK and the US, consisting out of the vocalist James Clark (Throw The Fight), drummer Micheal "Moose" Thomas

Casey Edwards.bio: Casey Edwards is a composer, producer, songwriter, and multi-instrumentalist based in Los Angeles, CA and hailing from Chattanooga, TN. He is best known for composing Nero's battle theme song "Devil Trigger" from the video game, Devil May Cry 5. His work has charted internationally and also spans across the film, TV, video game, and song industries. Raised primarily on rock and pop, he would eventually pick up the guitar and begin writing songs of his own.

Bleed from Within.bio: Bleed From Within is a metal band from Glasgow, Scotland. The band formed in 2005 and currently consists of vocalist Scott Kennedy, drummer Ali Richardson, bassist Davie Provan and guitarists Craig Gowans and Steven Jones. The band has released four full-length studio albums and three EPs to date. Their fourth album "Era" was released on April 7, 2018 through Century Media in which the band moved towards a melodic metalcore style.

The Presidents of the United States of A.bio: The Presidents of the United States of America, commonly referred to as simply The Presidents or PUSA was an American alternative rock band. The band formed in Seattle, USA, in 1993. The three-piece currently comprises vocalist and "basitarist" Chris Ballew, drummer and vocalist Jason Finn and "guitbassist"/vocalist Andrew McKeag. Long time "guitbassist" and vocalist Dave Dederer departed the band in 2004, after 11 years. They have released five studio albums since forming in 1993.

Hollow Front.bio: Hollow Front is a metalcore band originating from Grand Rapids, Michigan, USA. The band was formed in 2016 and currently consists of vocalist Tyler Tate, guitarist Lee Albrecht, bassist Davion Michael, and drummer Frankie Mish. (Formally, guitarist Dakota Alvarez, bassist Brandon Rummler, and drummer Cody Davis) Hollow Front intertwines heavy-hitting elements with emotionally charged lyricism. Their music delves into personal experiences of loss

Vexed.bio: 1. Vexed are a British metalcore band from Hertfordshire, formed on New Years Day 2019, by vocalist Megan Targett, guitarist Jay Bacon and drummer Willem Mason-Geraghty. All three members had played together in a band whose direction they had grown to dislike following numerous lineup changes; Targett said that she felt limited in what she could do as a female musician in the band, and as a result "didn’t have a lot of respect for the music or myself because none of it was genuine to me.

Mega Drive.bio: Mega Drive is an American synthwave artist from Dallas, Texas. “For an 80s synth artist to surface calling himself Mega Drive brings with it an air of excitement and it’s a mantle a weak or lesser artist would be mistaken to use. This gentleman, though, is well worthy of this moniker. His music is action packed with killer set pieces, driving pace, untold drama and swells with emotion.” – Synthetix

The I.L.Y's.bio: The I.L.Y's are an American experimental rock duo from Sacramento, California. They were formed in 2015 as a side project by Death Grips members Zach Hill (vocals, drums, guitar, and keyboard) and Andy Morin (guitar and bass). They have released two digital albums through Death Grips' Third Worlds label – I've Always Been Good at True Love in 2015 and Scum with Boundaries in 2016, both of which were released without any prior publicity. The I.L.

Dave Holland Big Band.bio: Dave Holland (born October 1, 1946) is a British jazz bassist and composer who is a significant representative of avant-garde jazz. Born in Wolverhampton, England, Holland learned to play bass as a child, and spent three years studying the instrument at the Guildhall School of Music and Drama. By 1967 he was a regular player at Ronnie Scott's, the premier jazz club in London, backing visiting musicians. He was also involved in the London free improvisation scene around the drummer John Stevens

Satyr.bio: There are four bands under this name. 1. Satyr is a progressive metal band from Atlanta GA. They have made a name for themselves by blending technical proficiency, complex song structures, and dense emotion into one unique and impressive sound. Mixing equal parts post-hardcore, math rock, metalcore, and prog, their music is complemented by weighted lyrics containing forward and genuine subject matter as seen through more lighthearted eyes, which creates a sound and message that all can enjoy on the rock and metal spectrum.

illuminati hotties.bio: Illuminati Hotties is an American indie rock band from Los Angeles, California, created and fronted by producer, mixer, and audio engineer Sarah Tudzin. In 2018, Tudzin and her band released their debut full-length album Kiss Yr Frenemies. Free I.H., a mixtape, followed in 2020, and Let Me Do One More in 2021. The band's third album, Power, was released on 23 August 2024.

1000say.bio: A Japanese electropop group formed while the members were in college in 2005 in Tokyo. The group consists of Man (guitar), Api (lead vocals, bass), Michelle (synths) and Non (drums).

Boris Brejcha.bio: Born in 1981, Boris Brejcha was involved in music since early childhood. His education included drums and keyboard lessons. This was a perfect foundation for the next step - producing and performing electronic dance music. Boris Brejcha began producing and DJ-ing in November 2006. After both his first online MP3 debut "Monster" EP and the next EP titled "Yellow Kitchen" reached number one on many download platforms, he became the new techno shooting star and started playing gigs not only in his home country Germany, but also internationally.

Hanging Garden.bio: Hanging Garden was created in 2004, playing heavy, doom-oriented metal music. Over a decade, three albums and numerous line-up changes later, the bands style has evolved to a more melodic, contemporary sound. While still retaining strong roots in metal music, Hanging Garden now possesses a versatile, many-layered style of playing, drawing influence and inspiration from music and art far and wide. First started as a brainchild of Matti Reinola and Ari Nieminen, the band with a complete line-up was put together in 2005.

N.W.A.bio: N.W.A (abbreviation for Niggaz Wit Attitudes) was an American hip hop group from Compton, California, United States widely considered one of the seminal acts of the gangsta rap sub-genre, sometimes credited as the most important group in the history of hip hop. Active from 1986 to 1991, the rap group endured controversy due to the explicit nature of their lyrics, and was subsequently banned from many mainstream U.S. radio stations. In spite of this, the group has sold over 10 million CD units in the U.S. alone.

Raycevick.bio: Lucas Raycevick, better known online as Raycevick, is a Canadian YouTuber from Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada. Raycevick primarily focuses on creating videos of him doing in-depth analysis various video games, such as Call of Duty, Tom Clancy's Rainbow Six Siege, Mass Effect, The Last of Us, and more. His videos can range from 5 minutes to nearly an hour. He also has the So I've Finally Played... video series where he gives out his review on his video game that he has played and the Years Later video series

King Crimson.bio: King Crimson are an English progressive rock band formed in London in 1968 by Robert Fripp, Michael Giles, Greg Lake, Ian McDonald and Peter Sinfield. Guitarist and leader Fripp has remained the only constant member throughout the band's long history. The band drew inspiration from a wide variety of music, incorporating elements of classical, jazz, folk, heavy metal, gamelan, blues, industrial, electronic, and experimental music. They also exerted a strong influence on the early 1970s progressive rock movement

Death Cab for Cutie.bio: Death Cab for Cutie is an American indie rock band formed in Bellingham, Washington in 1997. The band consists of Ben Gibbard (vocals, guitar, piano), Nicholas Harmer (bass), Jason McGerr (drums), Dave Depper (guitar) and Zac Rae (keyboards). Death Cab began as a solo project of Ben Gibbard, now the band's vocalist and guitarist. Gibbard took the band name from the satirical song Death Cab for Cutie, performed by The Bonzo Dog Band in The Beatles' 1967 experimental film Magical Mystery Tour.

Gtronic.bio: Gtronic is a Belgian electro-house talent! Gtronic was 7 years old when he discovered his passion for music. His father had a music studio, which made it easy for him to experiment with some instruments. Just like his father did, he started to play the guitar. Growing up with music styles like Blues and Rock&Roll, he picked up a wide range of musical knowledge. After 10 years of practicing and playing with several other musicians, he discovered the electronic music world.

tomandandy.bio: tomandandy is a musical group, founded in the late 80's in NYC by Thomas Hajdu and Andy Milburn, chiefly known for their work in film scores and television commercials. The firm redefined the way in which music for media was created by appropriating aesthetics of the avant garde, bringing them into pop culture. Without investor support tomandandy became one of the largest music production companies in the world. During the 1990's, tomandandy had offices in NYC and LA and employed almost 100 full time employees around the world.

The Naked and Famous.bio: The Naked and Famous is a two piece indie band hailing from Auckland, New Zealand, now based out of Los Angeles, California. The band comprises Thomas Powers, Alisa Xayalith, Aaron Short, David Beadle and Jesse Wood, with Powers and Xayalith being the two current active members. According to the Young Blood Songfacts, they took the name from a line in UK trip hop singer-songwriter Tricky's track, Tricky Kid. After their initial formation in 2008

Camel.bio: Camel are an English progressive rock band formed in Guildford, Surrey, in 1971. Led by guitarist Andrew Latimer, they have released fourteen studio albums and fourteen singles, as well as numerous live albums and DVDs. Although they did not achieve mainstream commercial popularity, they developed a cult following in the 1970s with albums such as "Mirage" (1974) and "The Snow Goose" (1975). In the early 1980s, they moved towards a more jazz-influenced and commercially oriented style, followed by a prolonged hiatus.

https.bio: post-experiemental experimental Latest hotfix version from chrome store doesn't seem to work for everyone... Temporary solution: https://github.com/aemxdp/web-scrobbler/releases/tag/v1.47-fixed http://build.last.fm/item/1000591 https://www.youtube.com/user/lastfm this is a Lost FM issue, not chrome, yours, but the London Zoo HTTPS is an iconic band whose identity has been kept a secret. The band went through many changes over the years, originally named Hypertext Transfer Protocol the band more commonly went by HTTP.
similar: Last Week Tonight with John Oliver

Heartline.bio: 1] Heartline was anarcho / crust / punk band from Czech republic. They were active in early 90's, after their split Ep with Masskontroll went out, they moved to Caracasonne squat in France. 2] Heartline is a Heavy/Power/Speed metal band from Siegen, Germany. Ifluenced by RAGE/Helloween/Accept 3] Heartline is a Progressive Metalcore band from Adelaide, Australia Band Members : Luke Taylor - Vocals; George Carey - Guitar; Nevenko Sarunic - Bass; Oscar Taylor - Drums

The Heavy.bio: The Heavy is a soul-influenced indie rock band which formed in Bath, England in 2007. The band consists of Kelvin Swaby (vocals), Dan Taylor (guitar), Spencer Page (bass), Chris Ellul (drums) and formerly, Hannah Collins (keyboards). According to Songfacts, when The Heavy performed How Do You Like Me Now? on Late Night With David Letterman, they so impressed the host that he asked the band to play some more - the first time that's ever happened on the show.

Weightless.bio: See also:Weightless / Alberto Braida, John Edwards, John Butcher, Fabrizio Spera There are at least two bands called Weightless: 1) A pan-European improvisational jazz quartet, and 2) An indie band from Adelaide, South Australia. 1. Weightless Personnel: John Butcher: tenor saxophone, soprano saxophones Alberto Braida: piano John Edwards: double bass Fabrizio Spera: drums "British artists and intellectuals have an old fascination for the Italian culture

Riverside.bio: There are three bands who share the name Riverside. 1. Riverside was a Polish progressive rock band from Warsaw, formed in 2001 by guitarist Piotr Grudziński, drummer Piotr Kozieradzki, bassist and vocalist Mariusz Duda, and keyboardist Jacek Melnicki. Melnicki left the group in 2003 and was replaced by Michał Łapaj. Following the death of Grudziński in 2016, the band continued as a trio before later collaborating with guitarist Maciej Meller, who became an official member in 2020.

Big Spring.bio: Big Spring is brothers’ Ollie (vocals) & Alex Loring (bass and vocals), Chris Steele (drums) and Jack Rowan (guitar), formed in 2015 out of Brighton, UK. Big Spring has gained notoriety for their unique brand of alternative rock with crushing guitar riffs, catchy hooks and existential lyrics.

Boys Noize.bio: Boys Noize is the stage name of Berlin-based DJ and producer Alex Ridha. Ridha (born in 1982, Hamburg) started producing and DJing at early age to find himself supporting the likes of Felix Da Housecat and DJ Hell in a matter of time. Nowadays Ridha himself is known as a one of the most valued electronic artists, awarded with the Independent Music Award in 2010 as well as 3 years in a row Best Electronic Artist on Beatport. Has a refreshing approach: not afraid to break new grounds

similar: Louverture, OBKATIEKAT, LEECHY!, Agenius, Kuroi, Zureeal
Saint Agnes, Mimi Barks, Bobby Wolfgang
二口魔菜 Futakuchi Mana, ShotoChris
Tokyo den nou, DENONBU, picco, Neneruneru(CV:ICHIGORINAHAMU), ichigorinahamu

Jericco.bio: Jericco are a rock band from Melbourne, Victoria that formed in 2007. Influences include the likes of Karnivool. They are one of the newest hard-hitting bands to hit the Melbourne music scene, fusing middle-eastern grooves with progressive, electronic, hard rock. The original lineup of the band consisted of: Roy Amar - Bass & Oud Luke Halstead - Drums Brent Mccormick - Vocals Jordan Nagle - Guitar Fetah Sabawi - Keys & Programming After supporting Dead Letter Circus in Jericco's second gig

Garlicboys.bio: Garlic Boys are a hardcore punk band from Osaka, Japan. The current lineup is Peta (Lead Vocals), Larry(Guitar, Vocals), Pessin (Bass Guitar, Vocals), and Sexer (Drums, Vocals). They are currently known in Japan's "trendy" underground music scene as the, "godfathers of new school Japanese hardcore". Garlic Boys formed in 1985, and after releasing thrash demos such as Garlic Bomber and Garlic Boys, they released their first EP Ninniku Night in 1988 and their thrash metal debut album Psycho Thrash in 1991.

Alien Ant Farm.bio: Alien Ant Farm is a rock band that was formed in 1995 in Riverside, California, United States. Having a sound mixing alternative rock with elements of metal, punk music, and more, the band consists of Dryden Mitchell (vocals, guitar), Terry Corso (guitar), Tye Zamora (bass) and Mike Cosgrove (drums). Taking their name from the popular sci-fi trope of aliens having created humanity for their own amusement, the band became internationally famous in

Nightstop.bio: NightStop hails from Jyväskylä, Finland to bring you fresh new retro 80's electric sounds . The music is strongly influenced by blast from the 80's cyberpunk movies, video games and beautiful girls with big hair. NightStop is specifically intended for your HIFI stereo cassette player as you drive your Ferrari F40 into Miami nights.

Kim Petras.bio: Kim Petras is a German singer and songwriter based in Los Angeles. She first gained international attention due to her early gender transition and subsequent media coverage as a teenager. She released her debut EP, One Piece of Tape, in 2011 and later worked as a songwriter for artists including Fergie, JoJo, Skylar Stecker, and Twice. Her debut single, I Don't Want It at All, was released in August 2017, followed by Heart to Break,in 2018, which reached number 52 on Billboard's Dance Club Songs chart.

kinoue64.bio: kinoue64, a captivating Japanese artist known for their enchanting blend of japanese shoegaze and vocaloid rock. As both a singer and songwriter, kinoue64 has made a significant impact on the music scene, crafting soundscapes that transport listeners to ethereal realms while addressing poignant themes through their lyrics. With a distinctive style that merges dreamy melodies with intricate vocal arrangements, kinoue64 stands out for their innovative approach

3 Pill Morning.bio: Jeff Stebbins - Vocals Aaron Peterson - Guitar Trent Laugerman - Drums Ryan Lee - Bass/Vocals BIOGRAPHY Forged in the hard rock proving grounds of Minneapolis, MN in 2004, 3 Pill Morning could be described as trial by fire. Simply put, music was never a question for lead vocalist Jeff Stebbins and failure was never an option. While other musicians were moving from project to project and band to band, Stebbins took a different path, consistently growing and evolving 3 Pill Morning into an anthemic rock band no one could deny.

Viktor Vaughn.bio: Alter ego of rapper Daniel Dumile. Other alter egos include MF DOOM, Metal Fingers, DOOM and King Geedorah. The name Viktor Vaughn comes from Victor Von Doom, the real name of Fantastic Four arch-enemy Dr. Doom. Doom stated, in the song 'Doom on Vik': "Vik is more cutting edge, whatever the new stuff is or these new MC's are doing. But he still appreciates the old school art of rhyming. So there's still a level of sincerity to Viktors stuff, but it's still younger and newer

Brandy Senki.bio: Formed in August 2022, this Osaka-based three-piece band consists of 蓮月 (Hazuki (Guitar, Vocals)), みのり (Minori (Bass, Chorus)), and ボリ (Bori (Drums)). They quickly gained attention when their self-produced and edited music video for “Musica,” released on YouTube in December of the same year, surpassed 1 million views in just one month. In January 2023, they began full-scale activities, releasing their 1st EP, 「人類滅亡ワンダーランド」, in August.

österreich.bio: österreich ("Austria" in German; オストライヒ) is a Japanese math-rock/electronica solo project of the cabs guitarist 高橋國光 (Kunimitsu Takahashi) from Saitama which was been active since 2015. Member: 高橋國光 (Kunimitsu Takahashi; guitarist of the cabs ; born December 19, 1988) Participating Musicians: Vocals / Chorus: ** 鎌野愛 (Ai Kamano, ex. ハイスイノナサ) ** 飯田瑞規 (Mizuki Iida from cinema staff) ** 紺野メイ (Mei Konno from あみのず/Aminozu)
similar: TK from 凛として時雨

Closure in Moscow.bio: Closure In Moscow is an experimental post-hardcore/alternative rock band from Melbourne, Australia, formed in 2006. Discography: The Penance And The Patience (2008 EP) First Temple (2009 LP) The Impeccable Beast (2012 Single) Pink Lemonade (2014 LP) On March 10, 2023 the band released their single Better Way. The band released their album "Soft Hell" on October 27, 2023. Members: Christopher De Cinque – Vocals (2006–present) Mansur Zennelli – Guitar/Vocals (2006–present)

Aimi.bio: Given name: 愛美 Family name: 寺川 Birthday: Dec 25, 1991 Website: http://aimi-sound.com/ Member Favorites: 13 Terakawa Aimi is a voice actress (seiyuu) and singer born December 25, 1991 in Hyougo Prefecture, Japan. She is affiliated with Hibiki. Blood Type: A Nickname: Aimin Seiyuu name: 寺川愛美 (Aimi Terakawa) Vocal name: Aimi She was one of 3rd ANIMAX Anison Grand Prix Finalists with Sayaka Sasaki, and voiced a character in Cardfight!! Vanguard where Sasaki-san sang theme song to it. (Source: MyAnimeList)

Ready the Prince.bio: Toronto, Ontario hard rock New Rock Mafia

mouse on the keys.bio: A blend of minimal phrased piano and dynamic drumming. The pursuit of a live experience composed of visual and audio expression. Formed in 2006, with elements of jazz, funk, post‐rock and electronic music, Mouse on the Keys fits into a genre of their own. The trio consists of two former members of the influential Japanese underground band 9dw, Akira Kawasaki and Atsushi Kiyota. They teamed up with Daisuke Niitome, who has played drums as well as composed music for countless jazz‐funk and hip hop bands.
similar: How to count one to ten

Mercury.bio: There are at least six artists known as Mercury: 1. Mercury is an alternative rock band from Nashville, Tennessee. 2. A Swiss house duo with prominent releases on Gomma. 3. A rock band from Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania. 4. A South Korean Girl Group. 5. A late-1980's heavy metal band from Cleveland, Ohio. 6. A rapper from Atlanta. 1. Mercury is an alternative rock band from Nashville, Tennessee. The band is led by Maddie Kerr, who is known for her ethereal vocals.

Somewhere Special.bio: Somewhere Special is an electropop duo from New York City formed in 2023, comprised of Bruno Zero and Simone Alysia. Their first single, Blizzard Warning was released in February 2023, followed by their second single Cabin. Following the buzz generated by these two tracks, Somewhere Special dropped an EP titled Playground in August of 2023, featuring four tracks: Water Gun, Undercover Lover, Swimming in Your Pool f/ Zeke the Zombie Slayur, and Playground.

King 810.bio: King 810 (stylised as KING 810) is an American metal band from Flint, Michigan who is currently signed to Roadrunner Records. They released their debut album, Memoirs Of A Murderer, on August 19, 2014. King 810 consists of singer David Gunn, guitarist Andrew Beal, bassist Eugene Gill and drummer Andrew Workman.The band's lyrics talk about the realities of living in Flint, Michigan. 810 refers to Flint's area code

Des Rocs.bio: Des Rocs is an American rock artist from New York, New York. Des Rocs is a solo project produced and led by New York native Daniel Rocco. When touring Rocco is joined by his long time friends and bandmates, William Tully and Eric Mendelsohn. Des Rocs has toured with rock bands such as Muse, The Rolling Stones, and The Struts. Influences for Rocco include Muse, Elvis Presley, Jimi Hendrix, Roy Orbison, Kanye West, and Queen, though Rocco's focus is individuality and sounding unlike other rock artists.

Never Back Down.bio: Welcome to the family! Since their establishment in 2014 this motto has shaped the band Never Back Down like nothing else. Counting the 8th year in the music business, the five young men from Heinsberg/Germany already built up a huge fanbase. The unique mixture of Hardcore-, Beatdown- and Metalcore-elements, with not only powerful shouts but also emotional cleans With over 250 played concerts the band has already supported multiple national and international artists.

Genghis Tron.bio: Genghis Tron is a band from Poughkeepsie, USA. They formed as a trio in early 2004 where they met as students at Vassar College. They recorded a demo in 2004 entitled Laser Bitch, and released their debut EP Cloak Of Love the following year. "Dead Mountain Mouth", the band's first full-length, was released the following year. The newer material was a more well-blended and demented attack, focusing less on the cut-and-paste mayhem of the early songs and instead honing in on flow
similar: The Number Twelve Looks Like You

The Gloom in the Corner.bio: The Gloom in the Corner is a concept-driven metalcore band hailing from Melbourne, Australia. The band was formed in 2016 and currently comprises vocalist Mikey Arthur, guitarists Matt Stevens and Martin Wood, bassist Paul Musolino, and drummer Nic Harberle. Former members include guitarist Jaya Wirawan and bassist Michael Vallerant. Since their inception, The Gloom in the Corner has garnered attention for their unique approach to music. Describing themselves as a cinematic metalcore act

StarBenders.bio: Starbenders are a US American glam rock band from Atlanta. Members: ** Vocals & guitar: Kimi Shelter (since 2013) ** Bass: Aaron Lecesne (since 2013) ** Guitar: Kriss Tokaji (since 2015) ** Drums: Qi Wei (since 2024) Ex members: ** Rhythm guitar: Kyle Gordon (2013-2015) ** Drums: Katie Herron (2013-2017) ** Drums: Emily Moon (2017-2024) Biography: Blending glam, punk, and bubblegum pop influences, the group describe their sound as “blasphemous candy-coated bubblegum punk.

The Odious.bio: After brewing for several years amidst the filth of the Williamette river, The Odious emerged in 2007 as a single entity whose scream would shake the foundation of Portland for years to come. Bringing a fresh new face to the local death metal scene, this group of seventeen year olds are raising the bar with innovative riffage, reaching beyond the confines of their genre to create music that will fornicate you in the fleshy gray matter of your mind.

Parris Bridge.bio: Parris Bridge is a Rock band with Alternative, Punk, and Metal influences based out of Greenville, SC. The band’s lineup includes bassist Andrew Doolittle, lead guitarist Josh Bishop, drummer Christian Tucker, guitarist and vocalist Dylan Fipps, and lead vocalist Scott Beardsley.

Beach House.bio: Beach House is an American dream pop band originating from Baltimore, Maryland, United States. The group was formed in 2004 by Victoria Legrand (vocals, organ), who is the niece of French composer Michel Legrand, Alex Scally (backing vocals, guitar), As of 2022, James Barone (drums, percussion, bass guitar) has been listed as the official third member of the band. Since their formation, the group has released eight studio albums: Beach House (2006)

The Omnific.bio: The Omnific is an instrumental progressive metal band from Melbourne, Australia, formed in 2016. The Omnific consist of two bassists - Matthew Fackrell and Toby Peterson-Stewart - and a drummer - Jerome Lematua. Since their formation, the trio has released 3 EP's independently. They made their first international debut recently completing a European tour with Intervals and Sithu Aye. The band has attracted immense interest from all corners of the globe.

Crooked Royals.bio: Crooked Royals are a Progressive Metalcore band formed in Auckland, New Zealand. They have recently joined Periphery's record label 3DOT Recordings and released their debut album "Quarter Life Daydream" Crooked Royals are a dual fronted band with soaring melodic cleans and intense screaming that is layered upon technical riffs and chuggy breakdowns. They infuse metalcore with moments of head-spinning off-kilter prog, alternative melodies, and even nocturnal trap R&B.

Dream On Dreamer.bio: Dream On, Dreamer is a post-hardcore band from Melbourne, Australia. The members are: • Marcel Gadacz – Vocals • Callan Orr – Guitar • Zachary Britt – Guitar/Vocals • Chris Shaw – Bass • Aaron Fiocca – Drums They formed around 2008 and have released two EPs, Hope and Sails Set, Armada - EP, and one album, Heartbound. Hope was picked up by Australian's biggest hardcore label, Boomtown Records, and the band was invited to play on the Australian leg of the 2010 Take Action Tour alongside Attack Attack! and Pierce the Veil.

Whipped Cream.bio: There are multiple artists named Whipped Cream 1: Canada based EDM Producer. 2: Swedish pop band formed in 1989. 1. Caroline Cecil, known professionally as WHIPPED CREAM, is a Canadian DJ and producer based on Vancouver Island. She is currently signed to Big Beat Records. She released her major label debut EP, Who Is Whipped Cream?, in August 2020 on Big Beat. In 2012, Cecil attended the Sasquatch! Music Festival where she watched a performance by Active Child that spurred her to create her own music.

DESTRYUR.bio: Destryur, real name Chris Taylor, is a Brooklyn based (New Orleans native), electronic producer focusing on Synthwave and Horrorwave. In 2015 he released his first EP, Endurance. His heavily 80’s inspired horror driven dance music has caught the eye of the label "New Retro Wave" where he has released two back-to-back Grindhouse albums with his co-collaborator, STRNGR.

The Burden.bio: With an ever-growing maturity, living in the juxtaposition of chaos and candor, the Canadian progressive post-hardcore quartet The Burden is as much a band as they are a journey into the shadows of the heart. Drawing on elements of the glory days of post-hardcore from artists like Underoath and Silverstein and weaving them with intricate and honest reflections of love and loss, the Northern British Columbia band’s continual rise to prominence is only amplified by their unapologetic approach to raw lyricism and therapeutic song writing.

Ymir.bio: There are five bands called Ymir: 1. Ymir is a Pagan Black Metal band from Finland. They started in 1998, released a demo on 1999, then they did a demo in 2006 and than after fourteen years they have released their first full-length album self-titled "Ymir". 2. Ymir is a one-man ambient pagan black metal band born in Italy in January 2008 from an idea of Nartum, composer, player of all instruments, vocalist, and programmer. The idea was to recreate those cold and depressive visions of Nartum's primordial dreams

Dreamshade.bio: The new wave of melodic death metal is coming from Switzerland, a country which has never given birth to any important act of this genre. Dreamshade's newest album “The Gift Of Life” Dreamshade present the sound of progression. New singer Kevin Calì (who has been in the band since October 2011) has indeed brought to the band "The Gift Of Life". Whereas the debut album “What Silence Hides” showcased the energy, musicianship and technical prowess of a young up-and-coming group

Caroline Polachek.bio: Caroline Elizabeth Polachek is an American singer, songwriter and producer. Raised in Connecticut, Polachek co-founded the indie pop band Chairlift while studying at the University of Colorado. The duo emerged from the late-2000s Brooklyn music scene with the sleeper hit "Bruises". During her time in the band, she worked on the solo projects Ramona Lisa and CEP before embarking on a career under her own name after Chairlift's disbandment in 2017.

Atlas.bio: There are at least 23 acts with this name: 1. Atlas - 'The two Leeds producers that make up Atlas, Rem Sutton and Luke Fantom, are night owls themselves, making a sort of downtempo garage aided by vocalist Emily Harris' smooth vocals. They've been slowly perfecting this haunted slow-jam formula since their Cities EP’ XLR8R (Facebook). 2. Atlas (renamed to Half Sight) are A Melodic Hardcore outfit from Leeds, UK. EST 2010 Facebook, Band Camp, Tumblr, Twitter

Kitty.bio: Kitty is the name of more than one artist: 1) Kitty Ray (born Kathryn-Leigh Beckwith), known mononymously as Kitty (formerly Kitty Pryde and ♡kitty♡), is a musician from Florida, United States. Kitty began making music in 2010, which she shared via Tumblr. In 2011, she fronted the short-lived comedy hip-hop group Jokers in Trousers, and received recognition from rapper Riff Raff. The pair collaborated on a song, "Orion's Belt," in June 2012. Kitty's 2012 single "Okay Cupid" became an internet sensation

Transient.bio: Transient names at least seven musical entities. 1) Led by former Smile Empty Soul and 12 Stones bassist Jody Linnell, the heavy rock/metal band Transient is rounded out by seasoned musicians. Based out of New Orleans, Louisiana, the project began as a solo endeavor, but after about a year Linnell decided to recruit good friends from his regional area to complete the lineup. The band consists of Jody Linnell (guitar), Trey J. Mollo (vocals), Emmett Shumate (guitar), Brian Bennett (bass), and Phil Krohn (drums).

Thousand Foot Krutch.bio: Thousand Foot Krutch (often abbreviated TFK) is a Canadian Christian rock band from Peterborough, Ontario, Canada. They made their debut in 1995 when Trevor McNevan, Joel Bruyere, and Steve Augustine started putting a praise & worship spin on the nu-metal sound of the time. After building a buzz around Ontario with numerous shows and a strong demo titled That's What People Do, Thousand Foot Krutch signed with Diamante and issued their debut LP, Set It Off, in 2000.

Powderfinger.bio: Powderfinger were a rock band from Brisbane, Australia. Formed in 1989, the band managed to keep the same line up since they hit the big time, Bernard Fanning (Vocals, guitars), Darren Middleton (guitars), Ian Haug (guitars), John Collins (Bass) and Jon Coghill (drums). Powderfinger are winners of nearly every major award in Australia – including the ARIA (Australian Record Industry Awards) for Album of the Year, Best Rock Album and Song of the Year.

Victoria.bio: There are various artists named Victoria: 1) Victoria Georgieva (born 1997) is a Bulgarian singer-songwriter. She began her career after participating in season four of X Factor Bulgaria. Georgieva would have represented Bulgaria at the Eurovision Song Contest 2020 in Rotterdam, with her song "Tears Getting Sober". Due to the 2020 contest's cancellation, Georgieva represented Bulgaria at the Eurovision Song Contest 2021 instead, this time with "Growing Up Is Getting Old".

You Am I.bio: You Am I is a four-piece Australian power pop/alternative rock band, fronted by vocalist/guitarist and songwriter Tim Rogers. They were the first Australian band to have three albums consecutively debut at #1 on the ARIA Charts and are renowned for their energetic live shows. Tim Rogers formed the first version of the band in Sydney, Australia, with his school friend Nick Tischler and his older brother Jaimme in December 1989. This initial line-up was short lived and by the end of 1990 both Nick and Jaimme had left the band.

The Agonist.bio: The Agonist was a Canadian metal band from Montreal, Quebec, formed in 2004. Their last line up consisted of guitarists Danny Marino and Pascal (Paco) Jobin, bassist Chris Kells, drummer Simon Mckay, and vocalist Vicky Psarakis. Their former lead singer [airtist]Alissa White-Gluz ,who fronted the band from 2004 to 2014 left to join arch enemy Originally known as The Tempest, the band adopted their current moniker upon their signing to Century Media in 2007.

Pymlico.bio: Pymlico is Arild Brøter’s solo project where he writes and produces instrumental music. He is very inspired by a lot of progressive acts, both old and new, but also some very different generes like film music, fusion, world music, ambient music and good pop music. The first album “Inspirations” was released in the spring of 2011. With the help from 11 friends Arild produced an album full of different styles of instrumental music. Ranging from symphonic melodies to dark passages and from circus music to ambient themes.

Haezer.bio: HAEZER is an electro DJ that combines elements of punk, breakbeat, industrial music and glitch-ridden Electroclash. HAEZER originates from Cape Town, South Africa and has been producing his signature trash/electro sound since late 2008. He began his live career at Cape Towns mega dance club, THE ASSEMBLY, opening for visiting internationals such as Steve Aoki, Gtronic, Djedjotronic and Le Castle Vania. Soon after, HAEZER teamed up with Cyberpunkers (ITALY) and Saint Pauli (GERMANY) to collaborate on his debut single Who The Fuck is HAEZER .

otesla.bio: Otesla is Oyogi Tesla, a producer from Copenhagen, Denmark.

Sadus.bio: Sadus formed in 1984 in Antioch, California. The band started off playing deathrash, a style in between thrash metal and death metal, and released their debut album Illusions, which was later re-released as Chemical Exposure, in 1988. The sophomore LP Swallowed in Black came out in 1990. With their albums A Vision of Misery and Elements of Anger their sound shifted towards technical thrash metal. Despite suffering from poor media exposure, this band has been able to reach the highest peaks in the genre

Official Call of Duty®.bio: Members of Official Call of Duty® went on to form Bishie later on in their career. Starting in 1977, they started the snowball that would eventually form forest metal days later. Coined as an influence by bands like Blonk 184, Colderplay, and Have a Nice Day. Though not well known outside of their nation, their live antics are what jump started the stereotype behavior of live bands today. Their shows consisted of acts like taking the whole audience to the local grocery mart to purchase fruit

Tindersticks.bio: Tindersticks are an English indie rock band formed in 1992 in Nottingham, England. Their sound is characterized by a synthesis of orchestral backing, lounge jazz, and soul; the lush orchestrations of multi-instrumentalist Dickon Hinchliffe and the smoky baritone of lead vocalist Stuart A. Staples are the band's hallmarks. Tindersticks have employed electric guitars, as most rock bands have done, but augment their instrumentation with a wide array of instruments: Rhodes piano

similar: Solar/Białas ft. ReTo, Quebonafide, Wac Toja

El Ten Eleven.bio: El Ten Eleven is a Los Angeles-based instrumental rock duo formed in Los Angeles, California in 2002, consisting of Kristian Dunn and Tim Fogarty. What separates El Ten Eleven from their musical peers is that they are only two musicians on stage creating pounding landscapes of sound with no laptops or sequencers. Dunn switches off (sometimes mid-song) between a double-neck bass/guitar and a fretless bass, while his feet dance on an extensive floorboard of looping devices and effects pedals.

similar: Five Nights at Freddy's: Pizzeria Simulator
Five Nights at Freddy's: Sister Location
Five Nights at Freddy's: Ultimate Custom Night
Five Nights at Freddy's: Help Wanted
Five Nights At Freddy's: Security Breach
Five Nights at Freddy's: Help Wanted 2
Five Nights at Freddy's Security Breach: RUIN

Angel Witch.bio: Angel Witch are a British heavy metal band formed in London in 1976 and are recognized as one of the pioneering groups of the new wave of British heavy metal (NWOBHM). The band was originally established as Lucifer by guitarist and vocalist Kevin Heybourne and went through several line-up changes before adopting the name Angel Witch in 1978, with Heybourne remaining the central member. Their self-titled debut album, "Angel Witch," was released in 1980 by Bronze Records and is considered a seminal work in the NWOBHM movement.

Flyying Colours.bio: Heavily inspired by the loud, fuzzy guitars of shoegaze groups like Ride and the sweet melodies of dream pop artists like Slowdive, Melbourne, Australia's Flyying Colours formed in 2013 to make their own version of the sounds they loved. The band is built around the guitars and vocals of Brodie J Brümmer and Gemma O'Connor joined by bassist Melanie Barbaro and drummer Andy Lloyd Russell. First single, "Wavygravy," and a self-titled EP were issued in November of 2013.

Evil Nine.bio: They aren't evil and there are only two of them, but other than not living up to their randomly chosen yet aesthetically pleasing name Tom Beaufoy and Pat Pardy in no way disappoint. That's if you are into a distinctly individual sound that defies categorization and has delivered both cerebral stimulation and dancefloor satisfaction with every release so far. Tom and Pat first combined skills in 1998 and were quickly identified as a quality DJ/Producer duo by Adam Freeland who signed them to his Marine Parade label.

Angèle.bio: Angèle Van Laeken (born 3 December 1995), better known by her stage name Angèle, is a Belgian singer-songwriter, musician, pianist, record producer and actress. Angèle Van Laeken is the daughter of the singer Marka and of the actress Laurence Bibot. She was born in Uccle, Brussels-Capital Region. Her father encouraged her from an early age to learn the piano, which inspired her musical career. Angèle spent a difficult adolescence in a strict Catholic school before attending the Decroly School.

Triángulo de Amor Bizarro.bio: Triángulo de Amor Bizarro (Bizarre Love Triangle) is a band from A Coruña, Galicia. Their palette of sounds moves between the obsessiveness and hypnotism of shoegazer geometry, exactly as The Jesus and Mary Chain and My Bloody Valentine used to do. They are followers of No Wave and Kraut progression taken from the legacy of bands such as Stereolab. They self-produced and released themselves their first two demos, coming second twice in a row in the Demo Project, organized by the Benicàssim International Festival (FIB).

Bloomer.bio: There are multiple artists named "Bloomer". 1) Bloomer (Aka. Phantom Sage) is an indie shoegaze artist from Iowa. 2) Bloomer are an experimental psych/emo band from Harford County, Maryland. 3) Bloomer (London) makes bratty indie pop. 4) Bloomer (Aka. Wade Clarke) (Aka. Aeriae) produced a handful of videogame soundtrack remixes from 2003-2005.

CYBERTHING!.bio: CYBERTHING! is a synthwave/darksynth music project inspired by 80s action, horror and science fiction movies. When human has been resurrected by future technologies - he became a cyborg. When cyborg has been brought back to life by hellish witchcraft - it turns into the CYBERTHING!

Earth Tongue.bio: Earth Tongue is a Wellington, New Zealand-based heavy psych-rock duo formed in 2016 by Gussie Larkin (Mermaidens) and Ezra Simons. They create a "doom fuzz" sound blending stoner rock, proto-metal, and 70s prog. Known for, high-energy live shows and fuzz-laden riffs, they have gained international recognition, including releasing 2026's Dungeon Vision produced by Ty Segall.

Edyta Bartosiewicz.bio: Edyta Bartosiewicz (born January 11, 1966 in Warsaw) is a Polish rock singer. Her career began with the band Holloee Poloy an underground band with which she parted ways to pursue a solo career. She went to London and recorded her first solo album, sung entirely in English. The record, titled "Love", gained the singer some well-deserved acclaim from radio stations, but wasn't exactly a hit. Edyta decided to sing in Polish and that's what brought her an incredible succeess

Iglooghost.bio: English producer Seamus Malliagh makes extremely giddy, hyperkinetic electronic music as Iglooghost, throwing juke/footwork, anime, IDM, hip-hop, and pop into a blender and hitting infinity. The restlessly creative artist's tracks are extremely dense, fast-moving, and cartoonish, loaded with complex beats, high-pitched voices, and rapid-fire samples. While this sounds like it could easily end up being a nauseating sugar rush, his tracks have an advanced pop sensibility and focus that keeps them both exciting and memorable

No Buses.bio: Vocal,Guitar: Taisei Kondo Guitar: Shinya Goto Guitar: Haruki Wada Bass: Saori Sugiyama Drums: Issei Ichikawa

Impale.bio: IMPALE has been around since 2002 (First ever gig was June 15, 2002 at a block party). The original members, Frank Shannon - guitar, Phil Messina - drums, and Ed Cichon - bass, started the band, and through different member changes, found Jay McGury - vocals, and Kenny McClarin - guitars (ex members of metal band Rupture). IMPALE has had it's ups and downs, struggling through numerous member changes, people having to leave and come back, periods of time with little to no shows and the long

Zillion.bio: There are multiple artist going by the name of Zillion 1) ZILLION is a CO-ED group under Sony Music Japan. The group was scouted trought the "One in a Billion" Auditions held by the JPop-artist Shota Shimizu in 2021. They made their pre-debut on December 22nd, 2021 with the digital single "Timeless". The group's goal is to aim for a debut. The member are Luna, Tyra, Keiji, Rion, Cashin, Kaora, Wataru and Moka. Member Hiroki allready had left the group in 2022.

Bicurious.bio: Bicurious are Gavin Purcell and Taran Plouzané; a confused, loud, experimental two-piece from Dublin, Ireland and Brest, France. They’ve built a loyal fanbase through extensive touring and have won over crowds both at home and abroad, establishing themselves as a recognizable force on the European instrumental rock scene. Known for riff sing-alongs, grooves that open up mosh pits and get heads banging, and the undeniable chemistry between the two remarkable musicians
similar: And So I Watch You From Afar

House of Hatchets.bio: Hailing from Edinburgh and comprised of Chris Allison (vocals), Pete Cook (bass), Frazer Parker (drums), and Jamie Parker and Lewis Wheeler (guitars), House of Hatchets revealed their engrossing debut EP, The Grind, in the Summer of 2017. Mixed and mastered by Romesh Dodangoda (Bring Me the Horizon, Bullet for my Valentine, Monuments), the record secured positive attention in both the US and the UK. Scottish DJ and journalist Jim Gellatly (Amazing Radio) also championed the band in the Scottish Sun

Pitchshifter.bio: Pitchshifter (originally Pitch Shifter) are an English industrial metal band from Nottingham, formed in 1989. The band were started by lead guitarist and programmer Johnny A. Carter, and bassist and vocalist Mark Clayden. The band's early material was characterised for its gritty industrial metal sound with downtuned guitars and the use of drum machines, and has been cited as one of the originators of the genre along with Godflesh. With later albums

Nicki Taylor.bio: Where the fuck has rock gone? Los Angeles four piece Nicki Taylor & The Too Far Gone have been wondering just that. “It makes it really hard to put together a fun bill for Friday night when every other band in LA seems to be whining about there shitty girlfriend. Grow some balls hombre,” says Nicki Taylor. “No worries, we’ll take it from here.” Seems to be the band’s stance on the future. There’s a lot of sound to come out of the small package known as Nicki Taylor.

Cabin Boy Jumped Ship.bio: Formed back in 2013, Cabin Boy Jumped Ship is a British Post-Hardcore/Electronic/Metalcore band from Leicester - Leicestershire. Members: Conor Owen Peek - Vocals, Jay Hillyer - Guitars, Lloyd Watson - Guitars, Kie Mills - Bass, Chris Thornton - Drums. Active Members: Conor Owen Peek - Vocals, Abel Aguado Sanchez - Guitar, Jake Russell Davenport - Bass / Vocals, Reece George Miller - Drums / Clean Vocals. Latest record called 'Sentiments' out May 27th 2022 through the german powerhouse label AFM Records. Official Facebook

Breaking Benjamin.bio: Breaking Benjamin is an alternative metal band Formed in Wilkes-Barre, Pennsylvania, USA in 1998 by vocalist Benjamin Burnley and drummer Jeremy Hummel, Breaking Benjamin quickly garnered a strong local following. 1st Studio Album (2002) - Saturate The band signed to Hollywood Records in 2001 after the success of an independently-released, self-titled EP, which sold all 2,000 copies that were manufactured. Hollywood Records released their full-length, major-label debut, Saturate, on August 27, 2002.

ShockOne.bio: Hailing from Perth, the epicenter of the Australasian drum and bass scene, ShockOne aka Karl Thomas is fast becoming one of bass music's major talents. Since the release of 'The ShockOne EP' in 2009, which featured the chart topping 'Polygon', ShockOne has been one of the hottest names in bass music. 'Polygon' received major air-play worldwide with high-rotation play listing on Australia's Triple J radio and Radio 1's Annie Mac penning it as, 'The biggest drum and bass tune out'.

Embracer.bio: Embracer is a melodic hardcore band from Charleston, WV, United States. Forming in 2011, they released their debut EP Dreamers soon after, promoting their brand of modern hardcore, with strong melodic guitar riffs. They have released 1 EP to date, Dreamers, a 6 song EP in 2011. Two singles were released in 2012 entitled, Glory Days and Ghost Town. Both Dreamers EP and Glory Days can be downloaded, free of charge, from the 'About' section on their official Facebook page.

Vein.fm.bio: Vein.fm (formerly known as Vein) is an American metalcore band from Billerica, Massachusetts that formed in 2013. In 2017 the band was signed onto Closed Casket Activities, who would issue their first full-length work Errorzone to warm reception among critics, even appearing on Revolvers early "Best Albums of 2018" list. The album also managed to peak at number 21 on Billboard's Hard Rock album chart. In July 2020, after the release of their remix album, the band announced it changed its name to Vein.fm.

Sam Smith.bio: Samuel Frederick Smith (born 19 May 1992) is an English singer and songwriter. They rose to prominence in October 2012 after being featured on Disclosure's breakthrough single "Latch", which peaked at number eleven on the UK Singles Chart. Smith was subsequently featured on Naughty Boy's "La La La", which became a number one single in May 2013. In December 2013, they were nominated for the 2014 Brit Critics' Choice Award and the BBC's Sound of 2014 poll, winning both.

Kesha.bio: Kesha Rose Sebert (born March 1, 1987), better known mononymously as Kesha (formerly stylized as Ke$ha), is an American recording artist. An active singer since before 2005, her breakthrough came in early 2009 after appearing on Flo Rida's number one single Right Round. Kesha later supported Calvin Harris on tour in the UK and toured with Mickey Avalon in the US. She also performed at Madison Square Garden on December 11, 2009 for the Z100 Jingle Ball.

Stay Lost.bio: Stay Lost is a five piece post hardcore band from Birmingham, Alabama. Debut EP, “Smoke Signals”, out now.

Stage Kids.bio: Friends from high school, Stage Kids are a group of relatively young musicians hailing from North San Diego, carrying with them a barrage of genres formed into an incandescent whole.

Converge.bio: Converge is an American metalcore band formed in 1990 in Salem, Massachusetts by vocalist Jacob Bannon and guitarist Kurt Ballou. During the recording of their fourth studio album "Jane Doe" in 2001, the band became a four-piece following the departure of guitarist Aaron Dalbec and the addition of bassist Nate Newton and drummer Ben Koller; this lineup has remained stable since. Members have participated in various side projects and collaborations, including Supermachiner, Old Man Gloom, and Mutoid Man.

similar: トップシークレットマン

toddle.bio: Toddle member profiles (translated from official site http://toddle.to/about/index.html) Tabuchi, Hisako 田渕ひさ子 - guitar, vocals born 1975 DEC 09 from Fukuoka prefecture since starting guitar at age 13, continued to play guitar in girl bands without stopping. joined Number Girl at 19 and moved to Tokyo in 1998. after the break-up in 2002, formed Toddle. in 2003, joined Bloodthirsty Butchers. Her intense guitar playing that would wake a sleeping child is impressive

mudy on the 昨晩.bio: Mudy on the Sakuban (Sakuban is literally translated to 'Last Night'). This 5 member instrumental band hailing from Nagoya, Japan originally started back in January of 2006. Currently, the members are in their Junior year at their university. The sheer variety of their music makes it impossible to categorize their music. Everything from dancing, to screaming to brute chaos is on display at their live performances. They were selected to take part in JESU's Shibuya Quattro performance in the Autumn of 2007 by Justin K.

Propellerheads.bio: Propellerheads was a British big beat music band formed in 1995 in Bath, consisting of electronic producers Will White and Alex Gifford. The name "Propellerhead" is Californian slang for a computer nerd, which the band members found fitting after hearing it from a friend. Their first release was an EP titled "Dive!" in 1996 on the independent label Wall of Sound. They gained recognition in 1997 by contributing a remix for David Arnold’s Bond tribute album "Shaken & Stirred

Cleopatrick.bio: Cleopatrick is a Canadian heavy alt-rock duo from Cobourg, Ontario. They have their own record label, called Nowhere Special Recordings. Childhood friends Ian Fraser and Luke Gruntz grew up in Cobourg, Ontario had been friends since age 4[4] and began getting interested in music around age 8 when they first listened to AC/DC. Later in their teens they began recording their own music. Their debut EP, titled '"14"' was released on 14 February 2016, though they began to gain popularity with single "Hometown".

Bird Problems.bio: Bird Problems is a Progressive Metal band from Montréal, Canada started by brothers Michael (Vocals/Guitar) & Daniel "Djank" Smilovitch (Drums) in 2013. They soon invited Joseph Anidjar, whom they knew from school, to play Lead guitar. At some point, they also gained Max Laramee as their bassist. Early on in the band's life, Micheal decided to abandon playing guitar finding that he preferred focusing on being a frontman, and feeling that Joseph contributed enough as a lone guitarist.

BlackWaters.bio: Blackwaters is a South West Based band Mixing a indie/rock feel with acoustic sounds stripping back music to its most naked.

Darke Complex.bio: (Formerly known as Widow) is a nu metal band haling from Houston Texas. Formed in May 2012. Their debut EP "Widow" will be released on all digital markets on Spinefarm Records on March 17th, 2015

The Bonez.bio: JESSE and THE BONEZ was formed in 2012/11/11 through the multi entertainment project "Stand Up!" which JESSE launched in 2011/11/11 after he faced the 03/11 disaster, with the goal of maximizing opportunities where fans can participate spontaneously and mash up a variety of new creative sounds with JESSE. The background and members of JESSE and THE BONEZ enable them to create their own unique "Mixture" of sounds from alternative, hip-hop, to folky, even black contemporary.

Wrench.bio: At least five bands currently go under this name. 1) Australian stoner rock band. Formed in the early 90's, Adelaide, Australia's Wrench delve in California Desert-bred stoner rock spiced with space rock and some psychedelics. Often compared to the legendary Kyuss, the group comprises vocalist Nigel Ryan, guitarist Craig Elliot, bassist/keyboardist Jon McNichol and drummer Chris McNichol. After debuting with 1997's Planets Surface, they returned two years later with Oscillator Blues.

I Met A Yeti.bio: 5 piece Post-Hardcore band from Orlando, FL. Vocals - Daisy Chamberlin Guitar - Anthony Gonzalez Guitar - Ricardo Antonio Rodriguez Bass - Lui Wancel Drums - Devin Russ

Vex Red.bio: Vex Red are a band from Aldershot, England who merged hard rock with electronica. The band was first active from 1995-2002, before reuniting in 2015. They were signed to US Producer Ross Robinson's Virgin Records Imprint record label, I Am, after sending a demo EP to Robinson. They litigated their way off the label in late 2002, and singer Terry Abbott formed a new band Septembre. Some of the remaining members went on to form another new band, Scenes.

About Tess.bio: about tess is a math rock band from Tokyo, Japan formed sometime around late 2005/early 2006. An instrumental band consisting of two drummers, two bassists and two guitarists, their music involves interminably repeated riff and rhythms overlapping each other, and they place uncalculated components in apposition with constructed parts. You will feel, rather than watch or listen to, their lives that present fast and furious transit from silence to rumble. They are energizing countrywide specially in Tokyo.
similar: You You You All The Same

Envy.bio: There are multiple groups, or aliases, by the name of Envy: 1. envy is a Japanese screamo/hardcore band which formed in 1992. Members: ** Vocals, Sequenzer: 深川哲也 (Tetsuya Fukagawa; like bassist Manabu Nakagawa, he is a steadfast original member dating back to the BLIND JUSTICE era; he serves as the band's leader and a central figure, handling tasks such as booking live shows; 1992-2016; 2019-present) ** Guitar: 河合信賢 (Nobukata Kawai; main composer)

Villain of the Story.bio: Villain of the Story is a metalcore band based out of Minnesota that was put together from the minds of Sam Fassler (We are the Blog! / Insomniac) and Andrew Bart (ex Out Came the Wolves). They hand picked Christian Grey (vocals), Logan Bartholomew (vocals/bass), and Cody Pauly (guitar) for their experience within the local scene, having been in previous bands. The 2 wanted to help manage a new band just for sake of seeing where it went. The then

Storm Orchestra.bio: STORM ORCHESTRA is french alternative rock band, has been distilling modern, unadorned rock for several years, whose solid foundations are based on aggressive riffs, catchy choruses and effective gimmicks, inspired by the cream of UK groups such as Royal Blood, Nothing But Thieves and ect.

Crim3s.bio: Crim3s are an English electronic duo formerly known as Story of Isaac from London. The group consists of Rou Rot and Sadie Pinn, who met while living in a warehouse in North London.
